[title] TWELVE MONKEYS 

[author_marker]           An original screenplay by 

[author_names] David Peoples 
[author_names]      & 
[author_names] Janet Peoples 

[source_marker]  Inspired by 

[source_names] LA JETEE, a Chris Marker Film 

[notes] Production Draft 
[draft_date] June 27, 1994 

[transition] FADE IN: 

[scene_heading] INT.  CONCOURSE/AIRPORT TERMINAL - BAY 

[action] CLOSE ON A FACE.  A nine year old boy, YOUNG COLE, his eyes wide 
[action] with wonder. watching something intently.  We HEAR the sounds of 
[action] the P.A. SYSTEM droning Flight Information mingled with the 
[action] sounds of urgent SHOUTS, running FEET, EXCLAMATIONS. 

[action] YOUNG COLE'S POV:  twenty yards away, a BLONDE MAN is sprawled on 
[action] the floor, blood oozing from his gaudy Hawaiian shirt. 

[action] A BRUNETTE in a tight dress, her face obscured from YOUNG COLE'S 
[action] view, rushes to the injured man, kneels beside him, ministering 
[action] to his wound. 

[action] ANGLE ON YOUNG COLE, flanked by his PARENTS, their faces out of 
[action] view, as they steer him away. 

[character] FATHER'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Come on, Son --this is no place for us. 

[action] YOUNG COLE resists momentarily, mesmerized by the drama. 

[action] YOUNG COLE'S POV:  intermittently visible through a confusion of 
[action] FIGURES rushing through the foreground, the BLONDE MAN reaching 
[action] up and touching the cheek of the kneeling BRUNETTE in a gesture 
[action] of enormous tenderness, a gesture of farewell, while the P.A. 
[action] SYSTEM continues its monotonous monotone... 

[character] P.A. SYSTEM 
[dialogue] Flight 784 for San Francisco is now 
[dialogue] ready for boarding at inmate number 
[dialogue] 66578, Greely. 

[scene_heading] INT.  PRISON DORMITORY/FUTURE - ETERNAL NIGHT 

[character] PRISON P.A. SYSTEM 
[dialogue] --number 5429, Garcia -- number 87645, Cole... 

[action] COLE, late thirties, dark hair, comes awake in a bunk cage, one 
[action] of many stacked four high along both sides of a long dim 
[action] corridor.  He blinks in the near dark, shaken, disoriented. 

[action] Then, as he "recovers" from his very vivid dream, WE GET OUR 
[action] FIRST LOOK AT HIS ENVIRONMENT...A WINDOWLESS UNDERGROUND WORLD OF 
[action] ETERNAL NIGHT SOMETIME IN THE FUTURE...AN ALMOST COLORLESS 
[action] "REALITY" OF BLURRED EDGES AND ECHOEY SOUNDS, MUCH MORE 
[action] "DREAMLIKE" THAN HIS DREAM. 

[action] Flashlights glare. In the half-light, COLE sees spooky figures, 
[action] GUARDS, moving among the locked bunk/cages. 

[action] COLE turns and whispers to the occupant of the next cage, JOSE... 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Ssssst!  Jose, what's going on? 

[action] JOSE's face is almost lost in shadow.  What there is of it is 
[action] youthful.  He's just a scared Puerto Rican kid! 

[character] JOSE 
[dialogue] "Volunteers" again. 

[action] JOSE immediately rolls over and feigns sleep as SCARFACE, a 
[action] menacing guard with a jagged scar running down his cheek, looms 
[action] close to COLE's cage and unlocks it. 

[character] SCARFACE 
[dialogue] "Volunteer duty". 

[action]     The PRISONERS in the other cages watch silently with narrowed eyes. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I didn't volunteer. 

[character] SCARFACE 
[dialogue] You causing trouble again? 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (controls his temper) 
[dialogue] No trouble. 

[scene_heading] INT.  EQUIPMENT ROOM - ETERNAL NIGHT 

[action] COLE's alone, struggling to get into what looks like a space suit 
[action] in a room where suits hang like ghosts with blank eyes. 

[character] TITLES BEGIN SUPERED OVER THE SCENE 

[action] COLE has the torso of the suit on now and is trying to close it. 

[character] OFFSCREEN VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] All openings must be closed. 

[action] COLE looks for the source of the voice, a tiny grate in the wall. 

[character] OFFSCREEN VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] If the integrity of the suit is compromised 
[dialogue] in any way, if the fabric is torn or a zipper 
[dialogue] not closed, readmittance will be denied. 

[scene_heading] INT.  SEALED CHAMBER - MINUTES LATER (ETERNAL NIGHT) 

[action] COLE, wearing the "space suit" and a helmet with a plastic visor, 
[action] steps into a tiny chamber, a kind of air lock.  The heavy door 
[action] clangs shut behind him.  He's alone.  COLE'S breath comes quicker 
[action] now as he sucks oxygen from the air tanks on his back. 

[action] On the opposite wall is another door with a huge wheel lock. 
[action] COLE turns the heavy wheel, opens the door, steps through It 

[scene_heading] INT.  ELEVATOR - SECONDS LATER (ETERNAL NIGHT) 

[action] COLE'S in an ascending elevator that groans and creaks.  He looks 
[action] down at a crudely drawn map he holds in his gloved hand. 

[action] The map shows a series of tunnels and ladders. 

[scene_heading] INT.  SEWER PIPE - MINUTES LATER (NIGHT) 

[action] COLE pans a flashlight, probing the filthy sewer he's wading through 

[action] RATS flee the blade of light, scurry across islands of rusting junk. 

[action] The flashlight beam settles on a ladder mounted in the wall. 

[action] Reaching the rusted ladder, COLE starts to climb awkwardly. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  CITY STREET/FUTURE - MOMENTS LATER (NIGHT) 

[action] A SCRAPING NOISE as a heavy man-hole cover is pushed up and moved 
[action] aside.  COLE'S helmeted head emerges from below. 

[action] COLE'S POV THROUGH HIS PLASTIC-VISORED HELMET:  a city in 
[action] moonlight!  A surreal image of abandoned buildings.  No people 
[action] anywhere.  The only sounds are the WIND and COLE'S BREATHING. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  ANOTHER CITY STREET - MINUTES LATER (NIGHT) 

[action] COLE'S light reveals abandoned vine-covered automobiles. 

[action] Moving to the nearest car, COLE searches in the vines for 
[action] something.  Finds it.  An insect. 

[action] COLE takes the bug in his gloved hand.  As he clumsily inserts it 
[action] into a collection tube, something makes him turn. 

[action] There's something across the street in the dark.  Something alive. 

[action] COLE points his flashlight and reveals...a BEAR!  Startled by the 
[action] light, the animal blinks, then stands on its rear legs and ROARS. 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, staring wide-eyed. 

[action] Then, the BEAR sinks down onto all fours and, trying to avoid the 
[action] flashlight, it pads quickly down the street. 

[scene_heading] INT.  SUBTERRANEAN PARKING GARAGE - NIGHT 

[action] Using the flashlight to see, COLE reaches down to the cracked 
[action] floor and gets another specimen.  DOGSHIT! 

[action] The only sound is COLE'S labored BREATHING. 

[action] Then, a different SOUND.  GRRRR!  A dog.  More GRRRRS.  More 
[action] dogs.  Then, a YIP.  Then, VICIOUS GROWLS.  It's a DOGFIGHT! 

[scene_heading] EXT.  STREET - NIGHT (FIRST LIGHT) 

[action] A giant OWL, perched on an overhead traffic light, raises its wings 
[action] and lifts off...rising higher and higher into the brightening sky. 

[action] Below, on the street, COLE trudges along, passing deserted 
[action] buildings, windows broken, rusted signs dangling. 

[scene_heading] INT.  DEPARTMENT STORE - NIGHT (FIRST LIGHT) 

[action] COLE'S light reveals a spider web just inside the store.  A large 
[action] SPIDER tries to hide from the light. 

[action] COLE reaches carefully into the web and plucks the spider and 
[action] puts it into one of his specimen tubes. 

[action] Then, he shines his light all around the once elegant store.  There's 
[action] nothing but aisle after aisle of moldering consumer goods. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  DEPARTMENT STORE - DAWN 

[action] As COLE comes out of the store, the first rays of the sun hit the 
[action] building.  COLE stops, squints into the light through his visor. 

[action] COLE'S POV:  spray-painted on the wall a long time ago is a stenciled 
[action] logo of twelve monkeys holding hands in a circle.  Over it is 
[action] written, "WE DID IT!" 

[action] COLE looks up. 

[action] COLE'S POV:  high up on a building across the street, a LION 
[action] patrols a ledge, pauses, looks out majestically over his world. 

[character] TTTLES END 

[scene_heading] INT.  FIRST UNDERGROUND DECONTAMINATION CHAMBER - ETERNAL NIGHT 

[action] ROARING WATER, powerful torrents gushing from nozzles in the 
[action] wall, pummel the still-suited COLE. 

[scene_heading] INT.  SECOND UNDERGROUND DECONTAMINATION CHAMBER - ETERNAL NIGHT 

[action] Stark naked and shivering, COLE is being scrubbed with brushes on 
[action] long poles (like the ones used to wash cars) wielded by two HULKING 
[action] FIGURES in bulky decontamination suits, their personas lost in their 
[action] windowed masks.  It's a grim scene in a grim cement room with damp, 
[action] dripping walls.  From an unseen source comes an AMPLIFIED VOICE, 

[character] AMPLIFIED VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Raise your arms above your head. 

[action] COLE lifts his arms and the FIGURES start scrubbing his armpits. 

[scene_heading] INT.  TINY CHAMBER - SHORTLY (ETERNAL NIGHT) 

[action] Still naked, COLE is seated on a stool while a MASKED TECHNICIAN 
[action] in a less elaborate, less bulky decontamination outfit draws 
[action] blood from COLE'S arm with an old-fashioned hypodermic needle. 

[action] COLE glances toward a single, nearly opaque "window" of thick 
[action] plastic in the rusty iron wall.  VAGUE FIGURES seem to lurk 
[action] behind the translucent aperture, studying him. 

[action] The TECHNICIAN slips the blood sample through a slot in the wall. 

[scene_heading] INT.  ENGINEERING OFFICE/FUTURE WORLD - ETERNAL NIGHT 

[action] Ushered in by two guards, TINY and SCARFACE, COLE looks around. 

[action] COLE'S POV:  wails hidden by old headlines, articles, maps, charts... 
[action] a blackboard covered with elaborate, sophisticated formulae...surfaces 
[action] heaped with cracked monitors, gerry-rigged computers held together 
[action] with 
[action] string, lasers lost in tangles of cable, ancient tube amplifiers, a 
[action] dilapidated cardboard reconstruction of a city, stacks of moldering 
[action] books and tattered computer printouts...and, seated at a long 
[action] conference 
[action] table, staring at COLE, six SCIENTISTS:  an ASTROPHYSICIST, ENGINEER, 
[action] BOTANIST, MICROBIOLOGIST, ZOOLOGIST, and a GEOLOGIST.  They represent 
[action] a "modern" science where brilliant new ideas interface with crude, 
[action] outdated, patched-together technologies. 

[character] TINY 
[dialogue] James Cole.  Cleared from quarantine. 

[character] MICROBIOLOGIST 
[dialogue] Thank you.  You two wait outside. 

[character] SCARFACE 
[dialogue] He's got a history, Doctor.  Violence. 

[action] COLE'S eyes return to the walls. 

[action] Headlines:  "CLOCK TICKING!  NO CURE YET!" 

[character] SCARFACE 
[dialogue] Anti-social six -- doing 25 to life. 

[character] ENGINEER 
[dialogue] I don't think he's going to hurt us.  You're 
[dialogue] not going to hurt us, are you Mr. Cole? 

[action] COLE'S head turns quickly to the ENGINEER. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] No, sir. 

[action] The GUARDS exchange a look, shrug, exit, closing the door. 

[character] MICROBIOLOGIST 
[dialogue] Why don't you sit down, Mr. Cole. 

[action] COLE goes to the empty chair at the conference table, sits down. 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 
[dialogue] We want you to tell us about last 
[dialogue] night. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I went to the surface and I collected 
[dialogue] specimens like I was told. 

[action] The SCIENTISTS don't say anything.  They just study him carefully. 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (worried) 
[dialogue] I mashed the spider, didn't I? 

[character] MICROBIOLOGIST 
[dialogue] We'll get to the spider later, Mr. 
[dialogue] Cole.  Right now, we want to know 
[dialogue] everything that you saw. 

[scene_heading] INT.  ENGINEERING OFFICE - AN HOUR LATER (ETERNAL NIGHT) 

[action] COLE, starting to look very tired now, stands at the blackboard 
[action] sketching a detailed map of exactly where he was last night. 

[character] ASTPOPHYSICIST 
[dialogue] Where you collected sample #4, what 
[dialogue] street was that? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Uh... 

[character] BOTANIST 
[dialogue] It's important to observe everything. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I think it was...I'm sure it was 2nd Street. 

[action] As the SCIENTISTS start to whisper animatedly among themselves, 
[action] COLE'S eyes drift across the newspaper clippings taped to the 
[action] wall.  One headline screams, "VIRUS MUTATING!"  Another features 
[action] a photo of an OLD MAN (DR. MASON, who we'll see again later on) 
[action] and the words, SCIENTIST SAYS, "IT'S TOO LATE FOR CURE". 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Close your eyes, Cole. 

[action] Startled, COLE closes his eyes obediently. 

[action] BLACKNESS.  Like COLE, WE SEE NOTHING.  But we HEAR their VOICES. 

[character] ENGINEER'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Tell us in detail what you've seen in 
[dialogue] this room. 

[character] COLE'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Uh, in this room?  Uh... 

[character] MICROBIOLOGIST'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] How many of us are there? 

[character] COLE'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Six...seven, if you count me. 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Tell us about the pictures on the wall... 

[character] COLE'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Uh, you mean the newspapers? 

[character] A MONTAGE OF OVERLAPPING VOICES (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Tell us about the newspapers.  Can you 
[dialogue] hear my voice?  What do I look like? 
[dialogue] What does he look like, the man who 
[dialogue] just spoke?  How old were you when you 
[dialogue] left the surface? 

[action] The VOICES blur into a cacophony and FADE INTO the droning P.A. 
[action] SYSTEM at the airport. 

[scene_heading] INT.  CONCOURSE/AIRPORT - DAY 

[action] THE DREAM AGAIN!  But at an earlier moment.  YOUNG COLE, flanked 
[action] by his PARENTS, whose faces are out of view, is watching a PLANE 
[action] land through one of the big glass windows that lines the concourse 
[action] leading to the departure gates. 

[character] P.A. SYSTEM (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Flight 784 now boarding at gate... 

[action] Suddenly, a SHOUT, followed by raised VOICES, interrupts the 

[action] monotonous airport routine.  As YOUNG COLE and his PARENTS turn 
[action] to see what's going on, a man we'll call MR. PONYTAIL, his face 
[action] averted, hurries past them, bumping YOUNG COLE with a Chicago 
[action] Hulls Sports Duffle Bag. 

[character] MR. PONYTAIL 
[dialogue] WATCH IT! 

[action] YOUNG COLE sees little more than the gaudy pants, the duffle, and 
[action] the man's ponytail flopping as he rushes towards the gates. 

[action] Just then, a WOMAN'S VOICE cries out, "NOOOOOOOOO!" 

[action] YOUNG COLE turns back toward the Security Check Point just as 
[action] TRAVELERS scatter madly, some diving to the floor, others 
[action] running.  A TERRIFIED TRAVELER, hitting the floor close by, looks 
[action] up at YOUNG COLE with panicky eyes, and asks.... 

[character] TERRIFIED TRAVELER 
[dialogue] Just exactly why did you volunteer? 

[scene_heading] INT.  ENGINEERING OFFICE/FUTURE WORLD - (ETERNAL NIGHT) 

[action] COLE comes abruptly awake.  Seated now, he's facing the SCIENTISTS. 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 
[dialogue] Wake up, Cole. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Uh, I didn't hear the... 

[character] MICROBIOLOGIST 
[dialogue] (tapping a pencil on the table) 
[dialogue] I asked you, why did you volunteer? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Well, the guard woke me up.  He told me 
[dialogue] I volunteered. 

[action] The SCIENTISTS react, whispering urgently among themselves. 

[action] COLE starts to nod off again, then comes awake with a start as 
[action] the ENGINEER speaks to him. 

[character] ENGINEER 
[dialogue] We appreciate you volunteering.  You're 
[dialogue] a very good observer, Cole. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Uh, thank you. 

[character] ENGINEER 
[dialogue] You'll get a reduction in sentence. 

[action] COLE keeps his face impassive. 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 

[dialogue] To be determined by the proper authorities. 

[character] ENGINEER 
[dialogue] You don't want to jeopardize that reduction, 
[dialogue] do you, Cole?  Have it taken away? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] No, sir! 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 
[dialogue] We have a very advanced program, something 
[dialogue] very different, requires very skilled people. 

[character] MICROBIOLOGIST 
[dialogue] An opportunity to reduce your sentence 
[dialogue] considerably... 

[character] ZOOLOGIST 
[dialogue] And possibly play an important role in 
[dialogue] returning the human race to the surface 
[dialogue] of the earth. 

[character] ENGINEER 
[dialogue] We want tough minded people.  Strong 
[dialogue] mentally.  We've had some...misfortunes 
[dialogue] with "unstable" types. 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 
[dialogue] For a man in your position...an opportunity. 

[character] BOTANIST 
[dialogue] Not to volunteer could be a real mistake. 

[character] MICROBIOLOGIST 
[dialogue] (tapping his pencil again) 
[dialogue] Definitely a mistake! 

[action] COLE gives away nothing.  He's in a box here.  He has no choices. 
[action] He stares at the tapping pencil. 

[scene_heading] INT.  ART GALLERY - NIGHT 

[action] A strikingly "real" world of bright colors.  Extravagant paintings 
[action] adorn the walls.  A POET, tiny and ruddy faced, squints over his 
[action] glasses as he reads in a booming voice to an AUDIENCE of thirty 
[action] seated on folding chairs. 

[character] POET 
[dialogue] Still among the myriad microwaves, the 
[dialogue] infra-red messages, the gigabytes of ones 
[dialogue] and zeroes, we find words, infinitesimally 
[dialogue] small, byte-sized now, tinier even than 
[dialogue] science lurking in some vague electricity 
[dialogue] where, if we listen we can hear the solitary 
[dialogue] voice of that poet telling us, 
[dialogue] "We are no other than a moving row 
[dialogue] Of Magic shadow-shapes that come and go 

[dialogue] Round with the Sun-illumined Lantern hold 
[dialogue] In Midnight by the Master of the show." 

[action] As the POET reads, we STUDY the audience, mostly YUPPIE CULTURE 
[action] JUNKIES or BOHEMIANS.  Among them, a light-haired woman of twenty- 
[action] eight, soberly dressed, wearing glasses.  She's KATHRYN RAILLY.  And 
[action] it's her beeper that suddenly BEEPS.  BEEP!  BEEP! 

[character] POET'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] "The Moving Finger writes; and, having writ, 
[dialogue] Moves on:  nor all your Piety nor Wit 
[dialogue] Shall lure it back to cancel half a Line, 
[dialogue] Nor all your Tears wash out a Word of it." 

[action] BEEP!  BEEP!  Scowling at the outrageous interruption, the POET 
[action] looks up from the text just as RAILLY, tumbling, shuts off the 
[action] beeper and rises, embarrassed.  As she makes her way to an exit, 
[action] the glaring POET continues... 

[character] POET 
[dialogue] "Yesterday This Day's Madness did prepare; 
[dialogue] Tomorrow's Silence, Triumph or Despair: 
[dialogue] Drink!  for you know not whence you came, nor why: 
[dialogue] Drink!  for you know not why you go, nor where." 

[scene_heading] INT.  CORRIDOR/POLICE STATION - NIGHT 

[action] DETECTIVE FRANKI leads RAILLY past crowded holding cells. 

[character] FRANKI 
[dialogue] -- so they get there and they ask the guy 
[dialogue] real nice for some kind of i.d., and he gets 
[dialogue] agitated, starts screaming about viruses. 
[dialogue] Totally irrational, totally disoriented, 
[dialogue] doesn't know where he is, what day it is, 
[dialogue] alla that stuff.  All they got was his 
[dialogue] name.  They figure he's stoned out of 
[dialogue] his mind, it's some kinda psychotic 
[dialogue] episode, so they're gonna bring him... 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] He's been tested for drugs? 

[character] FRANKI 
[dialogue] Negative for drugs.  But he took on 
[dialogue] five cops like he was dusted to the 
[dialogue] eyeballs.  No drugs.  You believe that? 

[action] FRANKI pauses, indicating a tiny observation window of thick meshed 
[action] glass in an otherwise solid door, and RAILLY looks through it. 

[action] RAILLY'S POV THROUGH THE WINDOW:  a MAN, his back to her, in 
[action] strait-jacket and prison denims, examining the wall of the padded 
[action] cell with the distorted intensity of a "mental case". 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] You have him in restraints. 

[character] FRANKI 
[dialogue] Were you listening?  We got two officers in 
[dialogue] the hospital.  Yeah, he's in restraints, plus 
[dialogue] the medic gave him enough stellazine to kill 
[dialogue] a horse.  Look at him!  Still on his feet. 

[action] RAILLY'S POV THROUGH THE WINDOW:  the MAN in the cell turns, looks 
[action] right at her.  In spite of the cuts and welts, it's clearly COLE. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] That would explain the bruises, I 
[dialogue] guess.  The struggle. 

[character] FRANKI 
[dialogue] You want to go in?  Examine him? 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Yes, please.  You said he gave a name... 

[character] FRANKI 
[parenthetical] (unlocking the door) 
[dialogue] James Cole.  That's everything we got.  None 
[dialogue] of the James Coles on the computer match him. 
[dialogue] No license, no prints, no warrants.  Nothing. 
[dialogue] You want me to go in with you? 

[character] RAILLY 
[parenthetical] (entering) 
[dialogue] No, thank you. 

[character] FRANKI 
[dialogue] I'll be right here...just in case. 

[scene_heading] INT.  ISOLATION CELL 

[action] COLE stares at RAILLY.  The environment is intensely real...vivid 
[action] colors...each sound, however slight, very distinct, almost loud... 
[action] and yet she appears to him almost like a vision. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Mr. Cole?  My name is Doctor Railly. 
[dialogue] I'm a psychiatrist.  I work for the 
[dialogue] County -- I don't work for the police. 
[dialogue] My only concern is your well being -- 
[dialogue] do you understand that? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I need to go now. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] I'm going to be completely honest.  I'm 
[dialogue] not going to lie to you.  I can't make 
[dialogue] the police let you go...but I do want 
[dialogue] to help you.  And I want you to trust 
[dialogue] me.  Can you do that, James?  May I 
[dialogue] call you "James"? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] "James"!  Nobody ever calls me that. 

[character] RAILLY 
[parenthetical] (frowns, studies him) 
[dialogue] Have you been a patient at County? 
[dialogue] Have I seen you someplace? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] No, not possible.  Listen, I have to 
[dialogue] get out of here.  I'm supposed to be 
[dialogue] getting information. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] What kind of information? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] It won't help you.  You can't do anything 
[dialogue] about it.  You can't change anything. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Change what? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I need to go. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Do you know why you're here, James. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Because I'm a good observer.  Because I 
[dialogue] have a tough mind. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] I see.  You don't remember assaulting a 
[dialogue] police officer...several officers? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] They wanted identification.  I don't have 
[dialogue] any identification.  I wasn't trying to 
[dialogue] hurt them. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] You don't have a driver's license, 
[dialogue] James?  Or a Social Security card? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] No. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Why not?  Most people have some ID. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] You wouldn't understand. 

[character] RAILLY 

[dialogue] You've been in an institution, haven't 
[dialogue] you, James?  A hospital? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I have to go. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] A jail?  Prison? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Underground. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Hiding? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I love this air.  This is wonderful air. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] What's wonderful about the air, James? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] It's so clean.  No germs. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] You're afraid of germs? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I have to go. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Why do you think there aren't any germs 
[dialogue] in the air, James? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] This is April, right? 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] July. 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (sudden panic) 
[dialogue] July?! 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Do you know what year it is? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] What year is it? 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] What year do you think it is? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] 1995? 

[character] RAILLY 

[dialogue] You think it's July of 1995?  That's 
[dialogue] the future, James.  Do you think you're 
[dialogue] living in the future? 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (slightly confused) 
[dialogue] No, 1995 is the past. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] 1995 is the future, James.  This is 1989. 

[action] COLE looks stunned. 

[scene_heading] INT.  POLICE STATION CORRIDOR - MORNING 

[action] COLE, bound tightly by the strait-jacket, heavy manacles on his 
[action] ankles, is being escorted down the corridor by two surly POLICEMEN. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Where are you taking me? 

[character] POLICEMAN #1 
[dialogue] South of France, buddy.  Fancy hotel. 
[dialogue] You're gonna love it. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] South of France?!  I don't want to go 
[dialogue] to the South of France.  I want to make 
[dialogue] a telephone call. 

[action] POLICEMAN #2 smirks as he unlocks a heavy steel door. 

[character] POLICEMAN #2 
[dialogue] Zip it, scumbag -- you fooled the shrink 
[dialogue] with your act, but you don't fool us. 

[action] Then, POLICEMAN #2 swings the steel door open and sunlight 
[action] overwhelms COLE, blinding him in a dazzling fury of white light. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  CITY STREET/MINI-VAN - DAY 

[action] A Mini-van, the kind of vehicle used to transport a half dozen 
[action] prisoners, crawls through a busy street.  The Police Department 
[action] logo is prominent on the side of the van beneath barred windows. 

[scene_heading] INT. COUNTY HOSPITAL/SHOWERS - AN HOUR LATER (MORNING) 

[action] Fierce spray recalls the decontamination in the future.  COLE 
[action] stands stark naked under the shower while two muscular attendants, 
[action] PALMER and BILLINGS, supervise. 

[action] As PALMER shuts off the water, BILLINGS hands COLE a towel and 
[action] starts inspecting his scalp... 

[character] BILLINGS 
[dialogue] Lemme see your head, Jimbo, see if you 
[dialogue] got any creepy crawlies. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I need to make a telephone call. 

[character] BILLINGS 
[parenthetical] (pulling Cole's head) 
[dialogue] Gotta work that out with a doctor, Jimbo. 
[dialogue] Can't make no calls 'til the doctor says. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] It's very important. 

[character] BILLINGS 
[dialogue] What chew gotta do, Jimbo, is take it 
[dialogue] easy, relax into things.  We all gonna 
[dialogue] get along fine if you just relax. 

[action] COLE gets the hint of menace in the message and submits to the 
[action] lice inspection, only his eyes revealing his frustration. 

[scene_heading] INT.  HOSPITAL/DAYROOM - HALF AN HOUR LATER (DAY) 

[action] COLE stands in the doorway, stunned by his first sight of the 
[action] large room.  His eyes go to the heavily-grilled windows where 
[action] light pours in from outside.  Then, to the TV, where a CARTOON 
[action] COMMERCIAL makes raucous noises. 

[action] PATIENTS, in K-Mart street clothes or ratty robes, stare gloomily 
[action] at the TV, or play cards, pace, or just stare blankly. 

[action] BILLINGS is at COLE'S side, beckoning to a patient, JEFFREY MASON, 
[action] a twenty year old white youth dressed in khakis and a plaid shirt. 

[character] BILLINGS 
[dialogue] Jeffrey.  Yo!  Jeffrey.  This here is James. 
[dialogue] Whyncha show James around?  Tell him the TV 
[dialogue] rules, show him the games an' stuff, okay? 

[character] JEFFREY 
[parenthetical] (with a sly look) 
[dialogue] How much you gonna pay me?  Huh?  I'd 
[dialogue] be doing your job. 

[character] BILLINGS 
[dialogue] Five thousand dollars, my man.  That 
[dialogue] enough?  I'll wire it to your account 
[dialogue] as usual, okay? 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Okay, Billings.  Five thousand.  That's 
[dialogue] enough.  Five thousand dollars.  I'll 
[dialogue] give him the Deluxe Mental Hospital Tour. 

[action] As BILLINGS walks away chuckling, JEFFREY turns to COLE. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Kid around, kid around.  It makes them feel 

[dialogue] good, we're all pals.  We're prisoners, they're 
[dialogue] the guards, but it's all in good fun, you see? 

[action] COLE nods and JEFFREY indicates card tables where PATIENTS are 
[action] playing cards, checkers, chess, or working on jig saw puzzles. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Here's the games.  Games vegitize you. 
[dialogue] If you play the games, you're 
[dialogue] voluntarily taking a tranquilizer. 

[action] COLE sees a partially completed puzzle of the well-known painting, 
[action] THE PEACEABLE KINGDOM, depicting a serene world of animals in harmony. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] What'd they give you?  Thorazine?  How 
[dialogue] much?  Learn your drugs -- know your doses. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I need to make a telephone call. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] A telephone call?  That's communication 
[dialogue] with the outside world!  Doctor's 
[dialogue] discretion.  Hey, if alla these nuts 
[dialogue] could just make phone calls, it could 
[dialogue] spread.  Insanity oozing through telephone 
[dialogue] cables, oozing into the ears of all those 
[dialogue] poor sane people, infecting them!  Whackos 
[dialogue] everywhere!  A plague of madness. 
[dialogue] (suddenly sly and confidential) 
[dialogue] In fact, very few of us here are actually 
[dialogue] mentally ill.  I'm not saying you're 
[dialogue] not mentally ill, for all I know you're 
[dialogue] crazy as a loon.  But that's not why 
[dialogue] you're here.  Why you're here is because 
[dialogue] of the system, because of the economy. 
[parenthetical] (indicating the TV) 
[dialogue] There's the TV.  It's all right there. 
[dialogue] Commercials.  We are not productive 
[dialogue] anymore, they don't need us to make 
[dialogue] things anymore, it's all automated.  What 
[dialogue] are we for then?  We're consumers.  Okay, 
[dialogue] buy a lot of stuff, you're a good citizen. 
[dialogue] But if you don't buy a lot of stuff, you 
[dialogue] know what?  You're mentally ill! That's 
[dialogue] a fact!  If you don't buy things...toilet 
[dialogue] paper, new cars, computerized blenders, 
[dialogue] electrically operated sexual devices... 
[parenthetical] (getting hysterical) 
[dialogue] SCREWDRIVERS WITH MINIATURE BUILT-IN 
[dialogue] RADAR DEVICES, STEREO SYSTEMS WITH 
[dialogue] BRAIN IMPLANTED HEADPHONES, VOICE- 
[dialogue] ACTIVATED COMPUTERS, AND... 

[action] A woman orderly, TERRY, turns from the feeble PATIENT she's helping. 

[character] TERRY 
[dialogue] Take it easy, Jeffrey.  Be calm. 

[action] Abruptly, JEFFREY stifles his hysteria, takes a deep breath and 
[action] continues, completely calm now.  But COLE isn't listening.  He's 
[action] mesmerized by the TV. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] So if you want to watch a particular 
[dialogue] program, say "All My Children" or 
[dialogue] something, you go to the Charge Nurse 
[dialogue] and tell her what day and time the show 
[dialogue] you want to see is on.  But you have to 
[dialogue] tell her before the show is scheduled 
[dialogue] to be on.  There was this one guy who 
[dialogue] was always requesting shows that had already 
[dialogue] played.  He couldn't quite grasp the 
[dialogue] idea that the Charge Nurse couldn't 
[dialogue] just make it be yesterday for him, turn 
[dialogue] back time ha ha.  What a fruitcake!! 

[action] This last thought actually penetrates COLE'S focus on the TV and 
[action] he turns to JEFFREY who's picking up speed again. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Seriously, more and more people are 
[dialogue] being defined now as mentally ill.  Why? 
[dialogue] Because they're not consuming on their 
[dialogue] own.  But as patients, they becone 
[dialogue] consumers of mental health care.  And 
[dialogue] this gives the so-called sane people work! 
[parenthetical] (hysteria again) 
[dialogue] WHOOO!  SHOCK THERAPY!  GROUP THERAPY! 
[dialogue] HALLUCINATIONS!  THERAPEUTIC DRUGS! 
[dialogue] IGGIDY DIGGIDY DIG!  PERFECT!  THE 
[dialogue] SYSTEM IN HARMONY LIKE A BIG MACHINE... 

[character] TERRY 
[dialogue] Okay, that's it, Jeffrey, you're gonna 
[dialogue] get a shot.  I warned you... 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] (calming himself, smiling) 
[dialogue] Right!  Right!  Carried away, heh heh. 
[dialogue] I got "carried away".  Explaining the 
[dialogue] workings of...the institution. 

[action] Just then, TJ WASHINGTON, a somber-looking African American in a 
[action] bathrobe, taps COLE on the shoulder. 

[character] TJ WASHINGTON 
[dialogue] I don't really come from outer space. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] This is TJ Washington, Jim -- he 
[dialogue] doesn't really come from outer space. 

[character] TJ WASHINGTON 
[dialogue] Don't mock me, my friend. 
[parenthetical] (to Cole) 
[dialogue] It's a condition of "mental divergence". 
[dialogue] I find myself on another planet, Ogo, 
[dialogue] part of an intellectual elite, preparing 
[dialogue] to subjugate barbarian hordes on Pluto. 
[dialogue] But even though it's a totally convincing 
[dialogue] reality in every way...I can feel, breathe, 
[dialogue] hear...nevertheless, Ogo is actually a 
[dialogue] construct of my psyche.  I am mentally 
[dialogue] divergent in that I am escaping certain 
[dialogue] unnamed realities that plague my life 
[dialogue] here.  When I stop going there, I will 
[dialogue] be well.  Are you also divergent, friend? 

[action] The P.A. SYSTEM interrupts, startling COLE. 

[character] P.A. SYSTEM (v.o.) 
[dialogue] James Cole.  Report to Staff.  James Cole! 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Staff!  Whoo!  Time for Staff.  Now the 
[dialogue] geniuses cure you.  Hallelujah! 

[scene_heading] INT.  PSYCH WARD CONFERENCE ROOM - MINUTES LATER (DAY) 

[action] COLE is agitated, speaking forcefully. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] This is a place for crazy people!  I'm 
[dialogue] not crazy! 

[action] RAILLY, four other PSYCHIATRIC RESIDENTS, including RAILLY'S best 
[action] friend, MARILOU MARTIN, and their chief, DR. OWEN FLETCHER, sit 
[action] around a beat-up conference table, watching COLE, who sits facing 
[action] the doctors, with BILLINGS looming behind him.  (Some of the DOCTORS 
[action] bear a strong resemblance to the SCIENTISTS OF THE FUTURE.) 

[character] RESIDENT #1 
[dialogue] We don't use that term..."crazy", Mr. Cole. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Well, you've got some real nuts in here! 
[dialogue] Listen to me, all of you -- I have to 
[dialogue] tell you something that's going to be 
[dialogue] difficult for you to understand, but... 

[character] DR. RAILLY 
[dialogue] James...please.  These are all doctors 
[dialogue] here and we want to help you. 

[character] DR. FLETCHER 
[dialogue] Mr. Cole -- last night you told Dr. 
[dialogue] Railly you thought it was... 
[parenthetical] (checking a file) 
[dialogue] 1995. ... How about right now?  Do you 

[dialogue] know what year it is right now? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] 1989.  Look, I'm not confused.  There's been 
[dialogue] a mistake, I've been sent to the wrong place. 

[action] Suddenly, COLE reaches out and BILLINGS lunges forward, but COLE 
[action] is just grabbing a pad and pencil. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Hey, I'm not going to hurt anybody. 

[action] FLETCHER restrains BILLINGS with a hand signal. 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (drawing) 
[dialogue] Do any of you know anything about the 
[dialogue] Army of the Twelve Monkeys?  They paint 
[dialogue] this, stencil it, on buildings, all 
[dialogue] over the place. 

[action] COLE waves a sketch of the dancing monkey logo we saw earlier. 

[character] DR. CASEY 
[dialogue] Mr. Cole... 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Right.  I guess you wouldn't, this is 
[dialogue] 1989, they're probably not active yet. 
[dialogue] That makes sense!  Okay.  Listen to me, 
[dialogue] three billion people died in 1995. 
[dialogue] Three billion, got that?  Almost 
[dialogue] the whole population.  Of the world! 
[dialogue] Only about one percent survived. 

[action] DOCTORS exchange knowing looks.  This is an old story, apparently. 

[character] RESIDENT #2 
[dialogue] Are you going to save us, Mr. Cole? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Save you?  How can I save you?  It 
[dialogue] already happened!  I can't save you.  I'm 
[dialogue] simply trying to get some information for 
[dialogue] people in the present so that someday... 
[parenthetical] (sees their eyes) 
[dialogue] You don't believe me.  You think I'm 
[dialogue] crazy.  But I'm not crazy.  I'm a convict, 
[dialogue] sure, I have a quick temper, but I'm as 
[dialogue] sane as anyone in this room.  I... 

[action] COLE stops, sees DR. FLETCHER tapping his pencil.  COLE'S seen 
[action] that tapping before -- in the future!  It disorients him. 

[character] DR. RAILLY 
[dialogue] Can you tell us the name of the prison 
[dialogue] you've come from? 

[action] COLE doesn't answer.  He's staring at the tapping pencil. 

[character] DR. FLETCHER 
[dialogue] Does this bother you, Mr. Cole? 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (recovering, new tack) 
[dialogue] No!  Look, I don't belong here!  What I 
[dialogue] need to do is make a telephone call to 
[dialogue] straighten everything out. 

[character] DR. FLETCHER 
[dialogue] Who would you call, Mr. Cole, who would 
[dialogue] straighten everything out? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Scientists.  I'm supposed to report in 
[dialogue] to them.  They'll want to know they 
[dialogue] sent me to the wrong time. 

[character] DR. FLETCHER 
[dialogue] So you could talk to these scientists 
[dialogue] and they do what?  Send you to the future? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] No, no.  I can't talk to them.  It's called, 
[dialogue] "voice mail".  I'm supposed to leave messages. 
[dialogue] They monitor it from the present. 

[character] RESIDENT #2 
[dialogue] "From the present."  We're not in the 
[dialogue] present now, Mr. Cole? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] No, no.  This is the past.  This has 
[dialogue] already happened.  Listen... 

[character] RESIDENT #3 
[dialogue] Mr. Cole, you belong in 1995 -- that's 
[dialogue] the present, is that it? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] No, 1995 is the past, too.  Look... 

[character] DR. FLETCHER 
[dialogue] These scientists, Mr. Cole?  Are they 
[dialogue] doctors like ourselves? 

[action] Two of the residents exchange quick knowing looks. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] No!  I mean yes, but... Look, I've been given 
[dialogue] a lot of drugs but I'm still perfectly lucid. 
[dialogue] You have to let me use the phone.  One call! 

[action] COLE looks desperately toward RAILLY, pleading eyes meeting hers. 

[scene_heading] INT.  LOW RENT APARTMENT - DAY 

[action] Four little KIDS SCREAM and SQUABBLE while the phone CHIRPS 
[action] insistently in the tiny, cluttered apartment and a harried MOTHER 
[action] lunges for the phone, answers sharply... 

[character] MOTHER 
[dialogue] Yes? 
[parenthetical] (listens, frowns, then) 
[dialogue] Whaaaaat?  "Voice mail"!  I don't know 
[dialogue] what you're talkin' about. ... Is this 
[dialogue] a joke?  I don't know any scientists. 
[dialogue] James who?  Never heard of you! 

[action] The MOTHER slams down the phone. 

[scene_heading] INT.  RAILLY'S OFFICE/COUNTY HOSPITAL - DAY 

[action] A dismayed COLE still has the receiver in his hand.  Sympathetically, 
[action] RAILLY takes it from him. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] It wasn't who you expected? 

[action] COLE is clearly agitated, starts to pace, upset.  Nuts? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] It was some lady.  She didn't know anything. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Perhaps it was a wrong number... 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] No.  That's the reason they chose me -- 
[dialogue] I remember things. 

[action] RAILLY frowns, studying the distracted man with intense interest. 
[action] It's clear COLE is becoming a special patient and RAILLY'S cool, 
[action] detached demeanor is giving way ever so slightly. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] James, where did you grow up?  Was it 
[dialogue] around here?  Around Baltimore? 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (lost in thought) 
[dialogue] What? 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] I have the...strangest feeling I've met 
[dialogue] you before...a long time ago, perhaps. 
[dialogue] Were you ever...? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Wait!  This is only 1989!  I'm supposed 
[dialogue] to be leaving messages in 1995.  It's 

[dialogue] not the right number yet.  That's the 
[dialogue] problem.  Damn!  How can I contact them? 

[action] RAILLY recovers her distance, her poise, as she takes a bottle, 
[action] pours out some tablets, and holds them out to COLE. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] James, take these. 
[parenthetical] (watching him step back) 
[dialogue] Please -- I helped you like I said I 
[dialogue] would.  Now I want you to trust me. 

[scene_heading] INT.  AIRPORT CONCOURSE - DAY (THE DREAM) 

[action] MR. PONYTAIL races past the startled YOUNG COLE. 

[character] MR. PONYTAIL 
[dialogue] WATCH IT! 

[action] Was it JEFFREY wearing gaudy pants and a ponytail?  It was 
[action] definitely JEFFREY'S VOICE. 

[action] TRAVELERS dive for cover as a WOMAN'S VOICE cries out... 

[character] WOMAN'S VOICE 
[dialogue] NOOOOOOOOOO! 

[action] The TERRIFIED TRAVELER looks up at YOUNG COLE, makes eye contact, but 
[action] doesn't speak.  The TERRIFIED TRAVELER looks a lot like DR. FLETCHER! 

[action] Just then, YOUNG COLE is distracted by a running figure.  It's 
[action] the BLONDE MAN in the Hawaiian shirt, but he's not injured.  He's 
[action] sprinting toward the gates, glancing back over his shoulder, his 
[action] moustache slightly askew! 

[action] A sharp CRACK of a GUNSHOT rings out!  Then, DAZZLING LIGHT. 
[action] Everything goes white! 

[scene_heading] INT.  DORMITORY (PSYCH WARD)/COUNTY HOSPITAL - NIGHT 

[action] COLE'S eyes blink awake, blinded by a flashlight. 

[action] He's lying in one of thirty beds in a darkened ward.  Disoriented. 
[action] Which world is this?  The room is full of BREATHING, SNORING, 
[action] occasional MOANS.  He can barely discern the shadowy figures of 
[action] an ORDERLY and a NURSE, making their rounds, checking each bed. 

[action] His eyes adjusting to the darkness, COLE watches them exit. 

[action] He turns and sees a patch of moonlight coming in a barred window. 

[action] With a quick glance at the sleeping PATIENTS, he slips out of 
[action] bed, makes his way stealthily to the window, peers out. 

[action] COLE'S POV:  the moon, glowing in the sky, illuminating a single 
[action] tree.  Under the tree, in silhouette, a COUPLE embraces, kisses. 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, looking out the window, absorbed. 

[character] VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] It won't work. You can't open it. 

[action] Alarmed, COLE turns, sees JEFFREY in the next bed. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] You think you can remove the grill but 
[dialogue] you can't.  It's welded. 

[action] COLE checks the grill anyway. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] See?  I toldja.  And all the doors are 
[dialogue] locked, too.  They're protecting the 
[dialogue] people on the outside from us.  But the 
[dialogue] people outside are as crazy as us. 

[action] COLE has become preoccupied with a small SPIDER creeping along 
[action] the window sill.  He's staring at it when he's distracted by a 
[action] sudden SOUND.  Grabbing the SPIDER, COLE scrambles back into bed 
[action] just as the door opens and an ORDERLY probes the dark room with 
[action] the blade of his flashlight. 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, in bed, feigning sleep. 

[action] The flashlight clicks off and COLE hears the door close. 

[action] For a long moment the ward is silent except for BREATHING, 
[action] SNORES, occasional MOANS.  Then, COLE hears JEFFREY'S hoarse 
[action] whisper, picking up right where he left off. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] You know what "crazy" is?  "crazy" is 
[dialogue] "majority rules".  Take germs for example. 

[action] Although COLE is preoccupied with the SPIDER struggling to get 
[action] out of his fist, he can't help reacting to the word, "germs"! 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Germs?! 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] In the 18th century there was no such 
[dialogue] thing!  Nobody'd ever imagined such a 
[dialogue] thing -- no sane person anyway.  Along 
[dialogue] comes this doctor...Semmelweiss, I 
[dialogue] think.  He tries to convince people... 
[dialogue] other doctors mostly...that there are 
[dialogue] these teeny tiny invisible "bad things" 
[dialogue] called germs that get into your body and 
[dialogue] make you...sick!  He's trying to get 
[dialogue] doctors to wash their hands.  What is 
[dialogue] this guy...crazy?  Teeny tiny invisible 
[dialogue] whaddayou call 'em?..."germs"! 

[action] As JEFFREY warms to his subject, getting excited, COLE tries to 
[action] figure out where to put the SPIDER. 

[character] JEFFREY (cont.) 
[dialogue] So cut to the 20th century!  Last week 
[dialogue] in fact, right before I got dragged 
[dialogue] into this hellhole.  I order a burger 
[dialogue] in this fast food joint.  The waiter 
[dialogue] drops it on the floor.  He picks it up, 
[dialogue] wipes it off, hands it to me...like it 
[dialogue] was all okay. 

[action] No alternative.  COLE pops the SPIDER in his mouth and swallows 
[action] it as JEFFREY prattles on... 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] "What about the germs?"  I say.  He 
[dialogue] goes, "I don't believe in germs.  Germs 
[dialogue] are just a plot they made up so they 
[dialogue] can sell you disinfectants and soap!" 
[dialogue] Now, he's crazy, right?  Hey, you 
[dialogue] believe in germs, don't you? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I'm not crazy. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Of course not, I never thought you were. 
[dialogue] You want to escape, right?  That's very 
[dialogue] sane.  I can help you.  You want me to, 
[dialogue] don't you?  Get you out? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] If you know how to escape, why don't you...? 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Why don't I escape, that's what you 
[dialogue] were going to ask me, right?  'Cause 
[dialogue] I'd be crazy to escape!  I'm all taken 
[dialogue] care of, see?  I've sent out word. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] What's that mean? 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] I've managed to contact certain underlings, 
[dialogue] evil spirits, secretaries of secretaries, and 
[dialogue] assorted minions, who will contact my father. 
[dialogue] When he learns I'm in this kind of place, 
[dialogue] he'll have them transfer me to one of those 
[dialogue] classy joints where they treat you...properly. 
[dialogue] LIKE A GUEST!  LIKE A PERSON!  SHEETS! 
[centered] TOWELS!  LIKE A BIG HOTEL WITH GREAT DRUGS 
[centered] FOR THE NUT CASE LUNATIC MANIAC DEVILS... 

[action] PATIENTS are waking up as the NURSE and two ORDERLIES burst into the 
[action] dorm and head straight for JEFFREY who's struggling to calm himself. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Sorry.  Really sorry.  Got a little 
[dialogue] agitated.  The thought of escaping 
[dialogue] crossed my mind and suddenly...suddenly 
[dialogue] I felt LIKE BENDING THE FUCKING BARS 
[dialogue] BACK, RIPPING OFF THE GODDAMN WINDOW 
[dialogue] FRAMES AND...EATING THEM, YES, EATING 
[dialogue] THEM, AND LEAPING, LEAPING... 

[action] COLE watches the ORDERLIES grab JEFFREY and haul him away. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] You dumb assholes!  I'm a mental patient! 
[dialogue] I'm supposed to act out.  Wait til you 
[dialogue] morons find out who I am.  My father's 
[dialogue] gonna be really upset.  AND WHEN MY 
[dialogue] FATHER GETS UPSET, THE GROUND SHAKES! 
[dialogue] MY FATHER IS GOD!  I WORSHIP MY FATHER. 

[scene_heading] INT.  WARD DAYROOM - MORNING 

[action] ANGLE ON TV SCREEN/A VIDEO IMAGE OF A LAB MONKEY, convulsing 
[action] pathetically, a victim of shocks from the numerous wires attached 
[action] to his tiny, restrained body. 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, sitting, writing intensely in a magazine with 
[action] crayon, surrounded by dull-eyed PATIENTS in pajamas and ratty 
[action] robes, staring at the shuddering LAB MONKEY on the TV screen. 

[character] JEFFREY'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Torture!  Experiments!  We're all 
[dialogue] monkeys 

[action] COLE locks up, startled, as JEFFREY, one eye bruised black, takes 
[action] the seat next to him. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] They hurt you! 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Not as bad as what they're doing to 
[dialogue] kitty. 

[action] ANGLE ON TV, showing a laboratory CAT turning in mad circles, 
[action] eating its own tail, while a NEWS REPORTER narrates. 

[character] TV NEWS REPORTER (v.o.) 
[dialogue] These video tapes were obtained by 
[dialogue] animal rights activists who worked 
[dialogue] underground as laboratory assistants 
[dialogue] for several months.  Authorities say 
[dialogue] there is little they can do until... 

[action] The video footage now shows LAB WORKERS watching the results of 
[action] their experiments passively. 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, reacting angrily. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Look at those assholes, they're asking 
[dialogue] for it!  Maybe people deserved to be 
[dialogue] wiped out! 

[character] JEFFREY 
[parenthetical] (startled, turning) 
[dialogue] Wiping cut the human race!  That's a 
[dialogue] great idea!  But it's more of a long 
[dialogue] term thing -- right now we have to 
[dialogue] focus on more immediate goals. 
[parenthetical] (sudden whisper) 
[dialogue] I didn't say a word about "you know 
[dialogue] what". 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] What are you talking about??? 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] You know -- your plan. 

[action] As COLE stares, befuddled, JEFFREY sees COLE'S magazine. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] What're you writing?  You a reporter? 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (shielding the magazine) 
[dialogue] It's private. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] A lawsuit?  You going to sue them? 

[action] Just then BILLINGS looms over COLE, extending a cup full of pills. 

[character] BILLINGS 
[dialogue] Yo, James -- time to take your meds. 

[scene_heading] INT.  DAY ROOM/HOSPITAL - THIRTY MINUTES LATER (MORNING) 

[action] ANGLE ON THE TV, a commercial playing:  a beautiful couple romps 
[action] in the surf in slow motion while an eager NARRATOR encourages... 

[character] NARRATOR (v.o.) 
[dialogue] Take a chance.  Live the moment.  Sunshine. 
[dialogue] Gorgeous beaches.  The Florida Keys! 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, very drugged, seated in front of the TV along with 
[action] other drugged PATIENTS, staring at the screen. 

[action] ANGLE ON THE TV, showing a picture of the Marx Brothers. 

[character] TV AWNOUNCER (v.o.) 
[dialogue] We'll return to the Marx Brothers in 
[dialogue] "Monkey Business" following these 

[dialogue] messages. 

[character] JEFFREY'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Monkey Business!  Monk Key Business. 

[action] COLE sees JEFFREY sliding into the next chair and smirking. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Get it?  Monk - Key.  Monk! 
[parenthetical] (big grin) 
[dialogue] Key! 

[action] JEFFREY flashes his palm open for one quick moment.  A KEY! 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (groggy) 
[dialogue] What....??? 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Wooooo, they really dosed you, bro. 
[dialogue] Major load!  Listen up -- try and get 
[dialogue] it together.  Focus!  Focus!  The plan! 
[dialogue] Remember?  I did my part. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] What...??? 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Not, "what", babe!  When! 

[] "When???" 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] (pressing the key into Cole's hand) 
[dialogue] Now! 

[character] VOICE/TV (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Let us guide you to the stocks and 
[dialogue] bonds that will enhance your portfolio. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[parenthetical] (leaping to his feet) 
[centered] YES -- NOW!  BUY NOW!  STOCKS AND BONDS! 
[dialogue] NO MORE MONKEY BUSINESS -- BUY NOW. 

[action] ANGLE ON TV, almost mimicking JEFFREY with an ad...a BULL and a 
[action] BEAR and a computer screen showing stock prices fluctuating. 

[character] VOICE/TV (v.o.) 
[dialogue] A portfolio tailored to your specific 
[dialogue] needs and the needs of your loved ones... 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, dumbfounded, watching JEFFREY dance crazily. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] YES, YES.  ENHANCE YOUR PORTFOLIO NOW! 

[action] ANGLE ON BILLINGS, across the ward, reacting to JEFFREY, lets go 
[action] of the OLD MAN he's helping as another orderly, TERRY, presses a 
[action] beeper, calling for help. 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, flabbergasted, as JEFFREY cavorts around the room. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] BUY!  SELL!  SEIZE THE OPPORTUNITY! 

[action] ANGLE ON A HAND, inserting the last piece into the PEACEABLE 
[action] KINGDOM JIGSAW PUZZLE.  Just then, JEFFREY'S HAND sweeps the 
[action] puzzle off the table, scattering it into a thousand pieces. 

[action] ANGLE ON JEFFREY, dancing away while the PATIENT who just 
[action] completed the puzzle stares, very upset. 

[action] Other PATIENTS are getting agitated, too, as JEFFREY avoids a 
[action] lunge by BILLINGS and dances off, using PATIENTS as a shield. 

[character] HEAVY WOMAN PATIENT 
[dialogue] I'M GETTING DIZZY.  MAKE HIM STOP! 

[character] SKINNY MAN PATIENT 
[dialogue] HERE THEY COME!  THEY'RE COMING! 

[character] OLD MAN PATIENT 
[dialogue] FIVE HUNDRED DOLLARS!  I GOT FIVE 
[dialogue] HUNDRED DOLLARS!  I'M INSURED! 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] OPPORTUNITY!  DEFINITELY!  A WINDOW OF 
[centered] OPPORTUNITY!  OPENING NOW!  NOW'S THE TIME 
[centered] FOR ALL GOOD MEN TO SEIZE THE MOMENT!  YES! 
[centered] YES!  MASTERCARD!  VISA!  THE KEY TO HAPPINESS! 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, realizing through the haze of drugs that JEFFREY 
[action] is sending a message to him.  COLE looks at the ward door. 

[action] COLE'S POV:  the WARD DOOR opens and two husky ORDERLIES enter. 
[action] One locks the door with a key, one of many on a key ring attached 
[action] to his belt, as the other ORDERLY rushes to join the pursuit. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] SEIZE THE MOMENT!  GET RICH!  NOW'S THE 
[dialogue] TIME!  GO FOR IT! 

[character] BILLINGS 
[parenthetical] (missing a tackle) 
[dialogue] God damn you, Jeffrey, quit playing the fool. 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, hesitating.  He locks at the door...blurring in 
[action] and out of focus.  He looks down at the key in his hand. 

[action] ANGLE ON JEFFREY, being grabbed by the ORDERLIES.  JEFFREY 
[action] resists wildly as they struggle to overpower him. 

[character] JEFFREY 

[dialogue] LAST CHANCE!  LAST CHANCE!  HEY -- OW! 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, moving to the door.  He reaches it and tries to 
[action] insert the key in the lock. 

[action] ANGLE ON LOCK, as the key keeps missing the hole. 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, glancing nervously over his shoulder. 

[action] COLE'S POV:  ORDERLIES swarm over JEFFREY, don't notice COLE. 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, managing to insert the key.  It won't turn. 

[action] A PATIENT, close at hand, startles COLE, speaking into his ear. 

[character] PATIENT 
[dialogue] Place to go would be...Florida.  The 
[dialogue] keys are lovely this tine of year. 

[action] COLE, unnerved, desperate tries the key again.  It turns. 

[character] PATIENT 
[dialogue] Be careful.  J. Edgar Hoover isn't really dead. 

[action] COLE pauses, stares, not understanding.  Then, he opens the door. 

[scene_heading] INT.  CORRIDOR/COUNTY HOSPITAL 

[action] Stepping through the door, COLE finds himself in an ante-room 
[action] facing several elevators. 

[action] A uniformed SECURITY MAN sitting at a near-by desk doesn't even 
[action] lock up from the magazine he's reading. 

[action] Barely daring to breathe, COLE steps toward the elevators so his 
[action] back is to the SECURITY MAN.  But he doesn't know how to control 
[action] this elevator.  What should he do? 

[character] SECURITY MAN'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Two's not working today.  Use one. 

[action] COLE freezes, sneaks a glance over his shoulder. 

[action] COLE'S POV:  the SECURITY MAN continues his reading.  He's a big 
[action] guy with reading glasses perched on his nose.  He looks exactly 
[action] like the MENACING GUARD IN THE FUTURE...SCARFACE! 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, stunned! 

[action] Just then, an elevator door slides open.  The elevator's empty. 

[action] COLE steps into it. 

[scene_heading] INT.  ELEVATOR/COUNTY HOSPITAL 

[action] The door closes, isolating COLE in the elevator. 

[action] COLE finds the down button, is about to push it when the elevator 
[action] springs to life.  The numbers on the indicator over the door 
[action] start to rise.  7...8...9. 

[action] Then, the elevator stops and the door opens. 

[action] Two DOCTORS and an AIDE stand in front of the door, waiting. 

[action] COLE hesitates. 

[action] They look at him.  They seem to expect him to exit. 

[action] Avoiding eye contact, COLE exits the elevator. 

[action] As they enter the elevator, the DOCTORS look back at COLE and frown. 

[scene_heading] INT.  RAILLY'S OFFICE - MORNING 

[action] RAILLY has just arrived for work.  She's slipping on her white 
[action] doctor's coat when... 

[action] DR. CASEY, one of the other residents, sticks his head in the 
[action] door waving a crayoned message on a page torn from a magazine. 

[character] DR. CASEY 
[dialogue] This was in my box, but I have a slight 
[dialogue] suspicion it wasn't meant for me. 

[action] CASEY enters the room, reading the scrawled words dramatically. 

[character] DR. CASEY 
[dialogue] "You are the most beautiful woman I have 
[dialogue] ever seen.  You live in a beautiful 
[dialogue] world.  But you don't know it.  You have 
[dialogue] freedom, sunshine, air you can breathe." 

[character] RAILLY 
[parenthetical] (smiling) 
[dialogue] Cole.  James Cole -- right? 

[action] She reaches for the note but CASEY moves it out of her grasp. 

[character] DR. CASEY 
[dialogue] "I would do anything to stay here, but 
[dialogue] I must leave.  Please, help me." 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Poor man... 

[action] CASEY is handing her the note when another resident, DR. GOODINS, 
[action] sticks his head in the door.  He's upset. 

[character] DR. GOODINS 
[dialogue] Hey, Kathryn, James Cole is one of 
[dialogue] yours, right? 

[action] RAILLY and CASEY stare at him. 

[character] DR. GOODINS 
[dialogue] He got out.  Took off.  Last seen, he 
[dialogue] was up on nine. 

[scene_heading]     INT.  X-RAY DEPARTMENT/BASEMENT - DAY 

[action]     A PATIENT is being swallowed by a large tube, a CAT SCANNER, 
[action]     while a DOCTOR in a white coat speaks reassuringly. 

[character] DOCTOR 
[dialogue] Just relax -- don't fight it.  We have 
[dialogue] to know exactly what's there so we can... 

[action] The DOCTOR stops, astonished, as the door bursts open. 

[action] It's COLE!  He stares at the PATIENT and the Cat Scanner. 

[action] The PATIENT lifts his head up and stares at COLE. 

[character] DOCTOR 
[dialogue] Eh, excuse me.  Can I help you? 

[action] COLE turns and rushes back out the door. 

[scene_heading] INT.  CORRIDOR/COUNTY HOSPITAL 

[action] COLE steps into the corridor, turns to his right, freezes. 

[action] A POSSE of SECURITY GUARDS is headed in his direction. 

[action] COLE turns to his left. 

[action] Four ORDERLIES are coming that way. 

[action] COLE'S trapped.  A beat.  He attacks the nearest man.  BILLINGS. 

[scene_heading] INT.  TECH ROOM/PSYCH WARD - SHORTLY (DAY) 

[action] RAILLY prepares a hypo, turns to COLE who is strapped tightly on 
[action] a gurney with BILLINGS and an RN standing on either side, tense 
[action] for more trouble.  One of BILLINGS' eyes is starting to swell shut. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] It's just a shot to calm you. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] No more drugs.  Please... 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] I have to do this, James.  You're very 
[dialogue] confused. 

[action] RAILLY pushes the needle into COLE'S skin. 

[scene_heading] INT.  CONFERENCE ROOM/PSYCH WARD - LATER (DAY) 

[action] DR. FLETCHER faces RAILLY across the conference table.  DR. 
[action] CASEY, DR. GOODINS, DR. MARILOU MARTIN are also there. 

[character] DR. FLETCHER 
[dialogue] Don't be defensive, Kathryn, this isn't 
[dialogue] an inquisition. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] I didn't think I was being defensive. 
[dialogue] I was just... 

[character] DR. FLETCHER 
[dialogue] He should have been in restraints.  It 
[dialogue] was bad judgment on your part, plain 
[dialogue] and simple.  why not just cop to it? 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Okay, it was bad judgment.  But I have 
[dialogue] the strangest feeling about him -- I've 
[dialogue] seen him somewhere and... 

[character] DR. FLETCHER 
[dialogue] (impatient, not interested) 
[dialogue] Two policemen were already in the 
[dialogue] hospital and now we have an orderly 
[dialogue] with a broken arm and a Security 
[dialogue] Officer with a fractured skull. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] I said it was bad judgment!  What else 
[dialogue] do you want me to say? 

[character] DR. FLETCHER 
[dialogue] You see what I mean?  You're being defensive. 
[parenthetical] (to Dr. Casey) 
[dialogue] Isn't she being defensive, Bob? 

[action] But just then, BILLINGS sticks his head in the door. 

[character] BILLINGS 
[dialogue] Uh, Dr. Fletcher -- we got another... 
[dialogue] situation. 

[scene_heading] INT.  CORRIDOR/PSYCH WARD - MOMENTS LATER (DAY) 

[action] DR. FLETCHER looks into an empty padded cell as RAILLY, MARTIN, 
[action] GOODIN, BILLINGS, PALMER and the NURSE crowd behind him. 

[character] DR. FLETCHER 
[dialogue] He was in full restraints?  And the 
[dialogue] door was locked? 

[character] BILLINGS 
[dialogue] Yes, sir.  Did it myself. 

[character] DR. FLETCHER 
[dialogue] And he was fully sedated? 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] He was fully sedated! 

[character] DR. FLETCHER 
[dialogue] Then are you trying to tell me that a 
[dialogue] fully sedated, fully restrained patient 
[dialogue] somehow slipped out that vent, replaced 
[dialogue] the grill behind him and that he's wriggling 
[dialogue] through the ventilation system right now? 

[action] DR. FLETCHER indicates an impossibly tiny vent high in the wall. 

[scene_heading] INT.  CONCOURSE/AIRPORT - DAY (THE DREAM) 

[action] Seen through the glass windows, a 747 takes off, climbing into 
[action] the sky as the airport P.A. System drones... 

[character] P.A. SYSTEM 
[dialogue] Flight 784 to San Francisco now 
[dialogue] boarding at Gate 38... 

[action] YOUNG COLE, watching the 747, whirls at the SOUND of a COMMOTION. 

[action] MR. PONYTAIL bumps him. 

[action] The BLONDE MAN sprints past.  The WOMAN'S VOICE calls out! 

[character] WOMAN'S VOICE 
[dialogue] NOOOOOOOOOO! 

[action] TRAVELERS dive for cover briefly revealing the mysterious BRUNETTE 
[action] running after the BLONDE MAN!  But this time, YOUNG COLE catches 
[action] just a glimpse of her face.  She looks a little like RAILLY except 
[action] for the dark hair, the make-up. and the flashy earrings.  She 
[action] calls out, her VOICE blending weirdly with the P.A. SYSTEM... 

[character] BRUNETTE/P. A. SYSTEM 
[dialogue] The Freedom For Animals Headquarters 
[dialogue] now boarding on Second Avenue.  The 
[dialogue] Army of the Twelve Monkeys... 

[character] ENGINEER'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Cole, you moron -- wake up! 

[scene_heading] INT.  ENGINEERING OFFICE - ETERNAL NIGHT OF THE FUTURE 

[action] As COLE blinks awake, the digitized monotone of the P.A. SYSTEM 
[action] continues to drone in an unearthly VOICE... 

[character] UNEARTHLY VOICE/P.A. SYSTEM 
[dialogue] -- they're the ones who are going to do it... 

[action] COLE'S eyes seek the source of the sound and find it on the table 
[action] in front of the panel of disapproving SCIENTISTS facing him.  It's 
[action] a beat-up old tape recorder. 

[character] UNEARTHLY VOICE/TAPE RECORDER 
[dialogue] I can't do anything more.  The Police 
[dialogue] are after me. 

[action] The tape ends, runs off the reel, flap...flap...flap... 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 
[dialogue] Well? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Uh, what? 

[character] ENGINEER 
[dialogue] He's drugged out of his mind!  He's 
[dialogue] completely zoned out. 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 
[dialogue] Cole, did you or did you not record 
[dialogue] that message? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Uh, that message...me? 

[character] MICROBIOLOGIST 
[dialogue] It's a digital reconstruction of a 
[dialogue] message, Cole, from a weak signal on our 
[dialogue] contact number.  Did you make that call? 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (angrily) 
[dialogue] I couldn't call!  You sent me to the 
[dialogue] wrong year!  It was 1989. 

[character] SCIENTISTS 
[dialogue] 1989! 

[action] The SCIENTISTS react, exchanging looks, whispers.  Then, 

[character] ZOOLOGIST 
[dialogue] You're certain of that? 

[character] GEOLOGIST 
[parenthetical] (before Cole can answer) 
[dialogue] What did you do with your time, Cole? 
[dialogue] Did you waste it on drugs?  Women? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] They forced me to take drugs. 

[character] BOTANIST 
[dialogue] Forced you!  Why would someone force 
[dialogue] you to take drugs? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I got into trouble.  I got arrested. 
[dialogue] But I still got you a specimen -- a 
[dialogue] spider -- but I didn't have anyplace to 

[dialogue] put it, so I ate it.  It was the wrong 
[dialogue] year anyway, so I guess it doesn't matter. 

[action] The SCIENTISTS stare incredulously, then turn, exchange knowing 
[action] looks, huddle, start whispering to one another. 

[action] Struggling to stay awake COLE sees, blurrily, the MICROBIOLOGIST 
[action] staring at COLE intently.  For one moment, the face belongs to 
[action] DR. FLETCHER! 

[action] COLE blinks hard...and the MICROBIOLOGIST has his own face, again. 

[action] COLE'S head slumps forward now...and everything goes dark. 

[character] GEOLOGIST'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Cole! 

[scene_heading] INT.  ENGINEERING OFFICE - ETERNAL NIGHT OF THE FUTURE 

[action] COLE comes awake with a start.  The room is dark now, except... 

[action] a slide is being projected on a torn screen.  It's a picture of a 
[action] stenciled graffiti...the logo of The Army of the 12 Monkeys. 

[character] ENGINEER 
[dialogue] What about it, Cole? 

[character] ZOOLOGIST 
[dialogue] Did you see it? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Uh, no, sir.  I... 

[action] Another slide CLICKS into place.  Youthful PROTESTERS, their 
[action] placards featuring slogans and images of Animal Atrocities, 
[action] confront POLICE in riot gear. 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 
[dialogue] What about these people?  Did you see 
[dialogue] any of these people? 

[action] Zooming in, panning, the SCIENTISTS emphasize the FACES of the 
[action] PROTESTERS.  The FACES are unfamiliar to COLE (though WE will 
[action] recognize some of them later on). 

[character] COLE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Uh, no, sir, I...wait! 

[action] The image pans back to a much enlarged blurry FACE among the 
[action] PROTESTERS.  In spite of the poor image, the expression of rage 
[action] is clear, and it seems to resemble a somewhat older JEFFREY MASON. 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 
[dialogue] Him?  You saw that man? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Uh, I think so.  In the mental hospital. 

[character] MICRO3IOLOGIST 
[parenthetical] (switching on the light) 
[dialogue] You were in a mental institution?! 

[action] The SCIENTISTS MUTTER disapprovingly among themselves. 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 
[dialogue] You were sent to make very important 
[dialogue] observations! 

[character] BOTANIST 
[dialogue] You could have made a real contribution. 

[character] GEOLOGIST 
[dialogue] Helped to reclaim the planet... 

[character] ZOOLOGIST 
[dialogue] As well as reducing your sentence. 

[character] MICROBIOLOGIST 
[dialogue] The question is, Cole -- "Do you want 
[dialogue] another chance?" 

[action] COLE stares at them, trying to figure out what they mean. 

[scene_heading] INT.  CONCOURSE/AIRPORT - DAY (THE DREAM) 

[action] The BRUNETTE runs up the concourse, her back to YOUNG COLE, as 
[action] frightened PASSENGERS duck for cover, SHOUTING! 

[character] RASPY VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Hey!  Who's that? 

[scene_heading] INT.  CELL - ETERNAL NIGHT 

[action] COLE opens his eyes.  Where is he?  Silence as he examines the 
[action] tiny cell.  Bare cement walls.  High ceiling.  Same color and 
[action] size as the isolation room at the county hospital. 

[character] RASPY VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Hey, Bob...what's your name? 

[action] COLE looks around frantically.  Up, down.  Where is the VOICE 
[action] coming from?  Maybe from that tiny vent high in the wall... 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Where are you? 

[character] RASPY VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] You can talk!  Wah'dja do, Bobby boy? 
[dialogue] Volunteer? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] My name's not "Bob". 

[character] RASPY VOICE (o.s.) 

[dialogue] Not a prob, Bob.  Where'd they send you? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Where are you? 

[character] RASPY VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Another cell. ...  Maybe. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] What do you mean, "maybe"?  What's that 
[dialogue] supposed to mean? 

[character] RASPY VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Maybe.  Means "maybe" I'm in the next cell, 
[dialogue] another "volunteer" like you -- or "maybe" 
[dialogue] I'm in the Central Office spying on you 
[dialogue] for all those science bozos.  Or, hey, "maybe" 
[dialogue] I'm not even here.  "Maybe" I'm just in 
[dialogue] your head.  No way to confirm anything. 
[dialogue] Ha Ha.  Where'd they send you? 

[action] COLE doesn't answer. 

[character] RASPY VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Not talking, huh, Bob?  That's okay 
[dialogue] I can handle that. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] 1989. 

[character] RASPY VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] 89!  How was it?  Good drugs?  Lotsa 
[dialogue] pussy?  Hey, Bob, you do the job?  D'ju 
[dialogue] find out the "big info"?...Army of the 
[dialogue] Twelve Monkeys...where the virus was 
[dialogue] prior to mutation? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] It was supposed to be 1995. 

[character] RASPY VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Science isn't an exact science with 
[dialogue] these clowns.  You're lucky you didn't 
[dialogue] end up in ancient Egypt! 

[scene_heading] INT.  LAB - ETERNAL NIGHT OF THE FUTURE 

[action] COLE is strapped on a gurney.  SCIENTISTS hover near-by, 
[action] whispering.  The walls of the gloomy chamber are damp, sweating. 

[character] GEOLOGIST 
[dialogue] No mistakes this time, Cole. 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 
[dialogue] Stay alert.  Keep your eyes open. 

[character] ZOOLOGIST 

[dialogue] Good thinking about that spider, Cole. 
[dialogue] Try and do something like that again. 

[character] MICROBIOLOGIST 
[dialogue] Just relax now -- don't fight it.  We 
[dialogue] have to know exactly what's there so we 
[dialogue] can fix it. 

[action] The gurney is being wheeled into a crudely welded steel tube... 
[action] reminiscent of the cat scanner in County Hospital. 

[action] COLE'S POV:  a last glimpse of anxious FACES, then the chamber 
[action] door is CLANGED shut. 

[action] EVERYTHING IS BLACK.  A HUM BUILDS.  THE BLACKNESS VIBRATES, THE 
[action] HUM REACHES A DEAFENING LEVEL, THEN DIMUENDOS.  WE BEGIN TO HEAR 
[action] BURSTS OF MACHINE GUN FIRE, VOICES SHOUTING IN FRENCH, A SUDDEN 
[action] HUGE EXPLOSION!  THEN... 

[scene_heading] EXT.  TRENCH/FRANCE - DAY 

[action] DRIZZLING RAIN.  And SCREAMS.  COLE'S in a deep trench, naked, 
[action] eyes wide with terror. What's going on? Where is he?  SOLDIERS 
[action] in gas masks push urgently past him rushing toward their injured 
[action] COMRADES who've been ripped apart by the shell that just hit 
[action] fifteen yards away.  Muffled VOICES shout through gas masks... 
[action] in FRENCH.  COLE doesn't know it, but this is World War I! 
[action] Suddenly, a SERGEANT confronts him, shouting in French. 

[character] SERGEANT 
[parenthetical] (FRENCH, subtitled) 
[dialogue] Where's your mask?!  And your clothes... 
[dialogue] and your weapon, you idiot?! 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] What?  What?? 

[action] COLE looks around desperately.  A horribly WOUNDED MAN is being 
[action] stretchered past them in the narrow trench.  Machine guns chatter 
[action] close at hand.  AAK AAK AAK.  A grenade EXPLODES.  Reacting to the 
[action] foreign word, the SERGEANT jams his bayonet into COLE'S ribs... 

[character] SERGEANT 
[parenthetical] (FRENCH, subtitled) 
[dialogue] Captain!  A Kraut!  We got a Kraut! 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I don't understand.  Where am I? 

[action] The CAPTAIN hurries over, snapping at COLE in German. 

[character] CAPTAIN 
[parenthetical] (GERMAN, subtitled) 
[dialogue] How'd you get here, soldier?  What's 
[dialogue] your rank?  Where are your clothes? 

[character] COLE 

[dialogue] I...don't understand. 

[character] CAPTAIN 
[dialogue] (frowning, GERMAN, subtitled) 
[dialogue] German!  Speak German!  What are you 
[dialogue] doing here? 

[character] VOICE (o.s.) 
[parenthetical] (pleading in English) 
[dialogue] I gotta find 'em.  I gotta find 'em. 
[dialogue] Please, you gotta help me! 

[action] COLE turns, sees... 

[action] It's his friend, JOSE, the Puerto Rican kid from the next cell in 
[action] the "underground" time.  He's being carried past COLE now on a 
[action] stretcher, blood all over his torso, horribly wounded. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] JOSE! 

[character] JOSE 
[dialogue] Cole!  Oh, God, Cole, where are we? 

[action] JOSE reaches out to COLE just as a PHOTOGRAPHER takes a FLASH 
[action] PICTURE of the kid being carried off on the stretcher.  SUDDENLY, 
[action] SHOTS RING OUT.  COLE goes down.  Hit in the leg! 

[action] SOLDIERS in gas masks rush past him like giant insects. 

[action] Looking to his left, COLE sees the CAPTAIN lying beside him, dead 
[action] from a chest wound, his gas mask half off. 

[action] COLE is reaching for the mask when... 

[action] A SHELL HITS CLOSE BY WITH AN ENORMOUS EXPLOSION. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  COLLEGE CAMPUS - NIGHT 

[action] Stunningly quiet.  We are on a placid campus looking at the 
[action] dignified architecture of Breitrose Hall.  MOVING IN we FOCUS ON 
[action] a large poster advertising "The Alexander Lectures, Spring 1995". 
[action] WE SKIM the listings (Jon Else on The Nuclear Agony, Dr. Andrew 
[action] Miksztal on Biological Ethics, etc.) until we SETTLE ON... 

[character] DR. KATHRYN RAILLY 
[centered] MADNESS AND APOCALYPTIC VISIONS 
[centered] MAY 17 

[scene_heading] INT.  AUDITORIUM/BREITROSE HALL - NIGHT 

[action] A large screen dominates the auditorium stage.  On the screen is 
[action] a slide of an engraving from the Middle Ages showing a MADMAN in 
[action] apparent agony, his mouth shaped to a scream, as he is restrained 
[action] by PEASANTS.  The projector ZOOMS slowly in on the agonized FACE 
[action] of this MADMAN as we HEAR RAILLY'S VOICE lecturing. 

[character] RAILLY'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] According to the accounts of local 
[dialogue] officials at that time, this gentleman, 
[dialogue] judged to be about forty years of age, 
[dialogue] appeared suddenly in the village of Wyle 
[dialogue] near Stonehenge in the West of England in 
[dialogue] April of 1162.  Using unfamiliar words and 
[dialogue] speaking in a strange accent, the man made 
[dialogue] dire prognostications about a pestilence 
[dialogue] which he predicted would wipe out humanity 
[dialogue] in approximately 8OO years.  Deranged and 
[dialogue] hysterical, the man raped a young woman of the 
[dialogue] village, was taken into custody, but then 
[dialogue] mysteriously escaped and was not heard of again. 

[action] WE DISCOVER RAILLY, six years older now, standing at a lectern 
[action] in a pool of light.  She's dwarfed by the giant screen where the 
[action] engraving is replaced by a series of slides of woodcuts showing 
[action] scenes of pestilence in the Middle Ages as she lectures to an 
[action] audience of mostly SCHOLARLY TYPES. 

[character] RAILLY (cont.) 
[dialogue] In 1841, Mackay wrote, "During seasons 
[dialogue] of great pestilence, men have often 
[dialogue] believed the prophecies of crazed 
[dialogue] fanatics, that the end of the world was 
[dialogue] come."  Obviously, this plague/doomsday 
[dialogue] scenario is considerably more compelling 
[dialogue] when reality supports it in some form, 
[dialogue] whether it's the Bubonic Plague, smallpox, 
[dialogue] or AIDS.  In addition to these "natural" 
[dialogue] contagions, there are now technological 
[dialogue] horrors as well:  besides radiation, 
[dialogue] consider our lurking fear of germ 
[dialogue] warfare and its close approximation, 
[dialogue] chemical warfare, which first reared 
[dialogue] its ugly head in the deadly mustard 
[dialogue] gas attacks during the First World War. 

[action] ON THE SCREEN, a SERIES of SLIDES show images of WORLD WAR I 
[action] SOLDIERS in gas masks, in death throes, etc.. 

[character] RAILLY'S VOICE (cont. o.s.) 
[dialogue] During such an attack in the French 
[dialogue] trenches in October, 1917, we have an 
[dialogue] account of this soldier... 

[action] ON THE SCREEN, a slide of an old deteriorated photograph shows 
[action] JOSE, the Puerto Rican kid, strapped to a stretcher, being carried 
[action] by SOLDIERS through the trenches during an attack.  JOSE appears 
[action] to be ranting madly as the projector ZOOMS CLOSER on his face until 
[action] the image approximates Munch's famous painting. 

[character] RAILLY'S VOICE (cant. o.s.) 
[dialogue] -- who, during an assault, was wounded 
[dialogue] by shrapnel and hospitalized behind the 
[dialogue] lines where Doctors discovered he had 

[dialogue] lost all comprehension of French but 
[dialogue] spoke English fluently, albeit in a 
[dialogue] regional dialect they didn't recognize. 
[dialogue] The man, although physically unaffected 
[dialogue] by the gas, was hysterical.  He claimed 
[dialogue] he had come from the future, that he was 
[dialogue] looking for a pure germ that would 
[dialogue] ultimately wipe mankind off the face of 
[dialogue] the earth in the year... 1995! 

[action] The AUDIENCE gives a nervous CHUCKLE. 

[action] ON THE SCREEN, a different old photograph of JOSE.  This time 
[action] he's in a military hospital, gaunt, haunted, very ill. 

[character] RAILLY'S VOICE (cont. o.s.) 
[dialogue] Although seriously injured, the young 
[dialogue] soldier disappeared from the hospital 
[dialogue] before more data could be gathered.  No 
[dialogue] doubt, he was trying to carry on his 
[dialogue] mission to warn others, substituting 
[dialogue] for the agony of war...a self-inflicted 
[dialogue] agony we call the "Cassandra Complex".
