[action] As RAILLY continues, we SCAN the AUDIENCE and DISCOVER MARILOU MARTIN, 
[action] RAILLY'S friend, and MARILOU'S HUSBAND, WAYNE CHANG, both listening 
[action] attentively.  Further away, another MAN listens intently.  A MAN with 
[action] shoulder-length carrot-colored hair.  His name is DR. PETERS. 

[character] RAILLY (cont.) 
[dialogue] Cassandra, in Greek legend you will recall, 
[dialogue] was condemned to know the future but to be 
[dialogue] disbelieved when she foretold it.  Hence, 
[dialogue] the agony of foreknowledge combined with 
[dialogue] impotence to do anything about it. 

[scene_heading] INT.  RECEPTION ROOM - AN HOUR LATER (NIGHT) 

[action] A stack of new books.  THE DOOMSDAY SYNDROME, Apocalyptic 
[action] Visions of the Mentally Ill by Dr. Kathryn Railly 

[action] Surrounded by enthusiastic members of the audience, RAILLY is 
[action] seated at the table signing books but DR. PETERS has her ear. 

[character] DR. PETERS 
[dialogue] I think, Dr. Railly, you have given 
[dialogue] your alarmists a bad name.  Surely 
[dialogue] there is very real and very convincing 
[dialogue] data that the planet cannot survive the 
[dialogue] excesses of the human race:  proliferation 
[dialogue] of atomic devices, uncontrolled breeding 
[dialogue] habits, the rape of the environment, the 
[dialogue] pollution of land, sea, and air.  In this 
[dialogue] context, isn't it obvious that "Chicken 
[dialogue] Little" represents the sane vision and 
[dialogue] that Homo Sapiens' motto, "Let's go 
[dialogue] shopping!" is the cry of the true lunatic? 

[action] DR. PETERS smiles self-importantly at RAILLY as an elderly 
[action] disheveled PROFESSOR elbows in front of him. 

[character] DISHEVELED PROFESSOR 
[dialogue] Doctor Railly -- please!  I wonder if 
[dialogue] you're aware of my own studies which 
[dialogue] indicate that certain cycles of the 
[dialogue] moon actually impact on the incidence 
[dialogue] of apocalyptic predictions as observed 
[dialogue] in urban emergency rooms and... 

[action] As the PROFESSOR babbles, MARILOU MARTIN and her husband, WAYNE 
[action] CHANG, appear and whisper... 

[character] MARILOU 
[dialogue] You were great. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] You're leaving? 

[character] MARILOU 
[dialogue] The reservation's at nine thirty -- 
[dialogue] it's getting late. 

[character] DISHEVELED PROFESSOR 
[dialogue] Doctor Railly -- please -- this is very 
[dialogue] important! 

[character] WAYNE CHANG 
[parenthetical] (checking the professor) 
[dialogue] You sure you're gonna be all right? 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] (smiles, checks her watch) 
[dialogue] I'll be there in twenty minutes. 

[character] DISHEVELED PROFESSOR 
[dialogue] Dr. Railly, I simply cannot understand 
[dialogue] your exclusion of the moon in relation 
[dialogue] to apocalyptic dementia... 

[scene_heading] EXT.  PARKING LOT/BREITROSE HALL - NIGHT 

[action] A full moon. 

[action] COLLEAGUES in a VOLVO pull out of the parking lot, calling, 
[action] "Congratulations" to RAILLY. 

[action] She waves back as she hurries to her black ACURA, one of the last 
[action] cars left in the lot. 

[action] The outside lights of Breitrose Hall go off. 

[action] RAILLY seems to be alone in the lot as she fishes keys from her 
[action] purse, unlocks her car door, starts to open it when... 

[action] Suddenly, she's grabbed from behind in a choke-hold by a large 
[action] shadowy MAN looming out of the darkness behind her. 

[character] MAN'S VOICE 
[dialogue] Get in! 

[action] Unable to scream, she writhes and kicks as he forces her into the 
[action] front seat. 

[character] MAN'S VOICE 
[dialogue] I've got a gun. 

[action] RAILLY freezes, terrified, as he opens the rear door and 
[action] scrambles in behind her. 

[scene_heading] INT.  ACURA/PARKING LOT 

[action] Fighting to suppress the quaver in her voice, RAILLY says... 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] You can have my purse.  I have a lot of 
[dialogue] cash and credit... 

[character] MAN'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Start the car. 

[action] Glancing in the rear view mirror, RAILLY sees penetrating eyes 
[action] peering out of the shadows, no other features. 

[action] Half-turning in the seat, she holds out the keys to him. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Here!  You can have the keys.  You can... 

[action] He grabs her hair and yanks her head back hard, speaking fiercely 
[action] into her ear, his face last in shadow. 

[character] MAN 
[dialogue] START THE CAR!  NOW! 

[scene_heading] EXT.  ACURA/PARKING LOT 

[action] The engine STARTS, the Acura backs up, then heads for the exit. 

[scene_heading] INT.  ACURA 

[action] Steering fearfully, RAILLY hears him speak more calmly now. 

[character] MAN'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] I don't want to hurt you.  But I will. 
[dialogue] I've hurt people before when...when I 
[dialogue] had no choice.  Turn left. 

[action] As she makes the turn, RAILLY glances in the rear view mirror, 
[action] sees him unfolding a tattered map.  His face is lost in darkness 
[action] but she glimpses ragged, torn clothing as he tries to read the 
[action] map by the intermittent glow of passing street lights. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Where... where are we going' 

[character] MAN 
[dialogue] I need you to drive me to Philadelphia. 

[character] RAILLY 
[parenthetical] (startled, horrified) 
[dialogue] But that's... that's more than 200 miles! 

[character] MAN 
[dialogue] That's why I can't walk there.  Turn 
[dialogue] here... I think... 

[action] RAILLY obeys.  She glances in the mirror again, hesitates, then 
[action] boldly switches on the dome light, holding her breath fearfully 
[action] for his reaction. 

[action] He grunts appreciatively.  Relieved, she looks in the mirror 
[action] again, trying to get a better look at him, but now his features 
[action] are concealed by the map. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] If you make me go with you, it's 
[dialogue] kidnapping.  That's a serious crime. 
[dialogue] If you let me go, you could just take 
[dialogue] the car and... 

[character] MAN 
[dialogue] I don't know how to drive!  We went 
[dialogue] underground when I was nine, I told you 
[dialogue] that.  When you come to the corner, 
[dialogue] turn right. 

[action] Startled, RAILLY whirls, looks right at him. 

[action] He's lowered the map.  It's COLE!  Haggard, unshaven, dirty. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Cole!  James Cole!  You escaped from a 
[dialogue] locked room six years ago. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] 1989.  Six years for you.  There's the 
[dialogue] sign!  Right here! 

[action] COLE is indicating a freeway entrance. 

[action] RAILLY turns the wheel sharply. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  FREEWAY - NIGHT 

[action] The Acura veers up the ramp and onto the freeway. 

[scene_heading] INT.  ACURA/FREEWAY - NIGHT 

[action] RAILLY glances in the mirror, sees COLE settling back wearily 
[action] against the seat.  She says carefully... 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] I can't believe this is a coincidence, 
[dialogue] Mr. Cole.  Have you been...following me? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] You told me you'd help me.  I know this 
[dialogue] isn't what you meant, but...I was desperate... 
[dialogue] no money...bum leg... sleeping on the streets. 
[dialogue] I probably smell bad.  Sorry about that. 
[dialogue] But then I saw your book in a store window 
[dialogue] with a notice about your lecture. 
[parenthetical] (sudden pride) 
[dialogue] I can read, remember? 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Yes, I remember. 
[parenthetical] (a beat, then) 
[dialogue] Why do you want to go to Philadelphia? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] It's the next step.  I checked out the 
[dialogue] Baltimore information, it was nothing. 
[dialogue] It's Philadelphia, that's where they 
[dialogue] are, the ones who killed everyone. 
[dialogue] (pointing suddenly, eagerly) 
[dialogue] Zs that a radio?  Does it play music? 

[action] RAILLY turns on the radio and immediately WE HEAR the SOUND of SURF 
[action] and the CRIES of gulls, background to an oozing baritone COMMERCIAL. 

[character] COMMERCIAL/RADIO (o.s.) 
[dialogue] This is a personal message to you. 

[action] COLE sits up, alert, listening intently. 

[character] COMMERCIAL/RADIO (cont. o.s.) 
[dialogue] Are you at the end of your rope?  Are 
[dialogue] you dying to get away? 

[action] COLE'S eyes narrow, concentrating on this personal message. 

[] COMMERCAIL/RADIO (cont. o.s.} 
[] The Florida Keys are waiting for you. 

[action] COLE frowns as the SOUND of breaking SURF and crying GULLS fills 
[action] the car.  It's confusing!  He blurts out... 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I've never seen the ocean! 

[action] Observing his confusion in the mirror, RAILLY assumes her 
[action] professional tone. 

[character] RAILLY 

[dialogue] It's an advertisement, Mr. Cole.  You 
[dialogue] do understand that, don't you?  It's 
[dialogue] not really a special message to you. 

[action] COLE frowns.  He did think it was for him, but she's probably right. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] You used to call me "James". 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] You'd prefer that? ... James...you 
[dialogue] don't really have a gun, do you. 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (cynical laugh) 
[dialogue] Everybody's got a gun.  In this city... 

[action] He breaks off reacting to the RADIO MUSIC!  FATS DOMINO singing 
[action] "BLUEBERRY HILL"!  COLE grins, mouth agape, eyes wide like a kid's. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Can you...can you make it louder?  I 
[dialogue] love hearing twentieth century music! 
[dialogue] Hearing music and breathing air! 

[action] As RAILLY cranks up the volume, she watches the mirror 
[action] incredulously, sees him stick his head out the window into the 
[action] wind, mouth open, "eating" the air hungrily. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  FREEWAY/ACURA - NIGHT 

[action] "BLUEBERRY HILL" BLARES as the Acura, COLE'S head out the rear 
[action] window, zips past a sign at 65 mph. 

[action] The sign says, "PHILADELPHIA 233 MILES." 

[scene_heading] INT.  ACURA/FREEWAY - NIGHT 

[action] RAILLY glances in the mirror at the nut in the rear seat with his 
[action] head out the window.  what can she do?  Just then, while she's 
[action] trying to figure something out, an ANNOUNCER'S VOICE breaks in... 

[character] ANNOUNCER/RADIO (o.s.) 
[dialogue] This just in from Fresno, California: 
[dialogue] emergency crews are converging on a 
[dialogue] cornfield where playmates of nine year 
[dialogue] old Ricky Neuman say they saw him 
[dialogue] disappear right before their eyes. 

[action] COLE pulls his head back inside with a frown, troubled now. 

[character] ANNOUNCER/RADIO (cont. o.s.) 
[dialogue] Young Neuman apparently stepped into an 
[dialogue] abandoned well shaft and is lodged somewhere 
[dialogue] in the narrow 150 foot pipe, possibly alive, 
[dialogue] possibly seriously injured.  Playmates claim 
[dialogue] they heard him cry out faintly but since then 

[dialogue] there has been no contact with... 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] "Never cry wolf!" 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] What? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] My father told me that.  "Never cry 
[dialogue] wolf."  Then people won't believe you 
[dialogue] if...something really happens. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] "If something really happens"...like 
[dialogue] what, James? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Something bad.  Is that all the music? 
[dialogue] I don't want to hear this stuff... 

[action] RAILLY glances at him as she scans stations. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Did something terrible happen to you when 
[dialogue] you were a child?  Something so bad...? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Ohhhh, that one!  Can we hear that one? 

[action] It's IVORY JOE HUNTER singing, "SINCE I MET YOU, BABY". 

[character] IVORY JOE/RADIO (o.s.) 
[dialogue] "Since I met you, baby, 
[dialogue] My whole life has changed... 

[action] Ecstatic, COLE sticks his head out the window again. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  ACURA/FREEWAY 

[action] COLE'S POV:  the heavens, glittering with a million stars and a 
[action] lover's moon as IVORY JOE croons the achingly romantic lyrics... 

[character] IVORY JOE/RADIO (cont. o.s.) 
[dialogue] "-- cause since I met you, baby. 
[dialogue] All I need is you..." 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, wind in his hair, eyes shining, gulping air blissfully. 

[scene_heading] INT.  RAILLY'S APARTMENT - MORNING 

[action] Two POLICE OFFICERS and an anxious MARILOU MARTIN listen to an 
[action] answering machine's message while a hungry CAT cries plaintively. 

[character] ANSWERING MACHINE 
[dialogue] Dr. Railly -- this is Palmer from Psych 
[dialogue] Admitting.  There was a guy here this 

[dialogue] afternoon looking for you.  He seemed 
[dialogue] very agitated.  We tried to keep him, but 
[dialogue] he refused 'n I kept thinking, I know 
[dialogue] this guy.  Then, just a few minutes ago, 
[dialogue] it came to me.  It's Cole!  James Cole. 
[dialogue] Remember him?  The paranoid who pulled 
[dialogue] the Houdini back in '89.  Well, he's 
[dialogue] back and he's...cuckoo...and he's looking 
[dialogue] for you.  I thought you oughta know. 

[action] The machine switches off.  The POLICE OFFICERS exchange a look. 

[character] MARILOU MARTIN 
[dialogue] It's just as I told you -- my husband 
[dialogue] and I had gone ahead -- she never 
[dialogue] showed.  That's totally unlike her! 

[character] OFFICER TWO 
[parenthetical] (pulls out his notebook) 
[dialogue] Do you happen to know the make of her car? 

[character] MARILOU MARTIN 
[dialogue] Um...Acura...'92 Acura. ... Also, that 
[dialogue] cat's starving!  She would never neglect 
[dialogue] her cat! 

[scene_heading] EXT.  MOTEL - MORNING 

[action] The ACURA is parked outside room 46 of the HIGHWAYS & BYWAYS 
[action] MOTEL, which has definitely seen better days. 

[scene_heading] INT.  MOTEL ROOM 46 

[action] The TV is on.  A commercial is just starting.  A catfood jingle. 

[action] The sound of HEAVY BREATHING. 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, sweating, BREATHING HEAVILY, sprawled on one side 
[action] of the double bed, sound asleep. 

[scene_heading] INT.  CONCOURSE/AIRPORT - DAY (THE DREAM) 

[action] GUNSHOT!  YOUNG COLE glimpses the BLONDE MAN staggering, wounded. 

[action] The mysterious BRUNETTE races past him toward the BLONDE MAN, and 
[action] YOUNG COLE again glimpses the resemblance to RAILLY, in spite of 
[action] the dark hair, the make-up, the flashy earrings. 

[action] Close at hand, YOUNG COLE'S FATHER, his face still out of view, says, 

[character] FATHER'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Son, it's important for your cat to 
[dialogue] have the nourishment necessary for 
[dialogue] healthy bones and a rich coat. 

[scene_heading] INT.  MOTEL ROOM 46 

[action] COLE comes awake with a start.  He stares, disoriented, at the 
[action] CATFOOD COMMERCIAL on the TV. 

[character] RAILLY'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Please untie me.  I'm very uncomfortable. 

[action] COLE turns to RAILLY, beside him on the bed, frightened and 
[action] helpless, her jacket arranged to restrain her like a strait-jacket. 

[action] COLE'S instinct is to free her at once, but he controls his 
[action] impulse.  He looks away, gets up, and, wincing, limps to the 
[action] dresser, stepping around empty fast-food cartons.  He pulls a 
[action] razor and shaving soap from a paper bag, then goes into the 
[action] bathroom, leaving the door open, and starts to shave. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] You were in my dream just now.  Your hair 
[dialogue] was different, but I'm sure it was you. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] We dream about what's important in our lives. 
[dialogue] And I seem to have become pretty important 
[dialogue] in yours.  What was the dream about? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] About an airport...before everything 
[dialogue] happened.  It's the same dream I always 
[dialogue] have -- the only one.  I'm a little kid 
[dialogue] in it. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] And I was in it?  What did I do? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] You were very upset.  You're always 
[dialogue] very upset in the dream, but I never 
[dialogue] knew it was you before. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] It wasn't me before, James.  It's 
[dialogue] become me now because of...what's 
[dialogue] happening.  Please untie me. 

[action] Finished shaving, COLE re-enters the bedroom, toweling his face. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] No, I think it was always you.  It's 
[dialogue] very strange. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] You're flushed.  And you were moaning. 
[dialogue] I think you're running a fever.  What 
[dialogue] are you doing? 

[action] COLE is rummaging through RAILLY'S wallet, pulling out money. 

[character] COLE 

[dialogue] I'll be back in a minute. 

[action] He heads for the door. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] No!  Don't leave me here like this! 

[action] Too late!  He shuts the door behind him, leaving her alone. 

[action] ANGLE ON THE TV SCREEN, where an ANCHORMAN sits at a News Set. 

[character] TV ANCHORMAN 
[dialogue] And in Fresno, California...crews 
[dialogue] continue to attempt to rescue nine year 
[dialogue] old Ricky Neuman. 

[action] ANGLE ON RAILLY, twisting and struggling on the bed, trying to 
[action] get loose, tears welling in her eyes. 

[character] TV ANCHORMAN (cont. o.s.) 
[dialogue] The boy was playing ball with four 
[dialogue] other children when he literally 
[dialogue] disappeared off the face of the earth. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  MOTEL CORRIDOR - MORNING 

[action] COLE puzzles over a junk food vending machine, inserts coins 
[action] tentatively. 

[scene_heading] INT.  MOTEL ROOM 

[action] ANGLE ON TV, the picture of RAILLY filling the screen. 

[action] ----------------------- PAGE 52 MISSING ----------------------- 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] My notes.  Observations.  Clues. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Clues?  What kind of clues? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] A secret army.  The Army of The Twelve 
[dialogue] Monkeys.  I've told you about them. 
[dialogue] They spread the virus.  That's why we 
[dialogue] have to get to Philadelphia.  I have to 
[dialogue] find them -- it's my assignment. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] What will you do...when you find 
[dialogue] this...secret army? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I just have to locate the virus in its 
[dialogue] original form before it mutates.  So 

[dialogue] scientists can come back and study it 
[dialogue] and find a cure.  So that those of us 
[dialogue] who survived can go back to the surface 
[dialogue] of the earth. 

[action] RAILLY maintains a professional deadpan, says nothing as they pass 
[action] a pickup truck with a MOTHER, FATHER, and five KIDS in the back. 

[action] COLE stares at the KIDS, a sad look in his eyes. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] You won't think I'm crazy next month. 
[dialogue] People are going to start dying.  At 
[dialogue] first the papers will say it's some 
[dialogue] weird fever, some virus.  Then they'll 
[dialogue] begin to catch on.  They'll get it. 

[character] RADIO NEWSCASTER (o.s.) 
[dialogue] We interrupt this program with a 
[dialogue] special bulletin... 

[action] RAILLY and COLE both react to the radio, suddenly alert. 

[character] RADIO NEWSCASTER (o.s.) 
[dialogue] This report just in from Fresno, 
[dialogue] California.  Naval sonar specialists 
[dialogue] who were flown to the site... 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I thought it was about us.  I thought 
[dialogue] maybe they'd found us and arrested me 
[dialogue] or something. 

[action] RAILLY stares at COLE. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Just a joke. 

[character] RADIO NEWSCASTER (o.s.) 
[dialogue] -- an hour ago have been unable to 
[dialogue] determine the location of the boy in the 
[dialogue] 150 foot shaft...but a TV sound man who 
[dialogue] lowered an ultra-sensitive microphone into 
[dialogue] the narrow tube claims he heard breathing 
[dialogue] sounds coming from approximately seventy 
[dialogue] feet down... 

[action] COLE reaches over and changes stations.  MUSIC again. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Does that disturb you, James?  Thinking 
[dialogue] about that little boy in the well? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] When I was a kid I identified with that 
[dialogue] kid, down there alone in that pipe...a 
[dialogue] hundred feet down -- doesn't know if 

[dialogue] they're going to save him. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] What do you mean -- when you were a kid? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Nevermind.  It's not real -- it's a 
[dialogue] hoax.  A prank.  He's hiding in a barn. 
[dialogue] Hey, turn left here.  Left! 

[action] COLE quickly checks the map as RAILLY stares, then turns left. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  SKID ROW STREET/PHILADELPHIA - DAY 

[action] An elderly EVANGELIST with long stringy hair, wearing a tattered 
[action] bathrobe, stands on a Skid Row corner WAVING a worn Bible as he 
[action] rants at disinterested DERELICTS, WINOS, and BAG LADIES. 

[character] EVANGELIST 
[dialogue] "And the wild beasts of the islands 
[dialogue] shall cry in their desolate houses and 
[dialogue] dragons in their pleasant palaces:  and 
[dialogue] her time is near to come, and her days 
[dialogue] shall not be prolonged." 

[action] ANGLE ON RAILLY'S ACURA, crawling down the street, RAILLY driving, 
[action] COLE, beside her, staring out the window. 

[scene_heading] INT.  ACURA/SKID ROW STREET 

[action] COLE is scrutinizing the crumbling walls, boarded-up store 
[action] fronts, tattered posters, decaying signs, miserable "RESIDENTS". 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Where I come from we think of this as Eden. 
[dialogue] If we could just see the sun, eat sun-grown 
[dialogue] food.  Eden!  Look at them!  They donut 
[dialogue] know what they have.  They don't see the 
[dialogue] sky.  They don't feel the air! 

[action] COLE'S POV:  a BMW speeds toward them, passes, its radio BLARING! 

[character] COLE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] And the ones who aren't hungry are so smug 
[dialogue] they haven't a clue.  WAIT!  STOP! 

[scene_heading] EXT.  ACURA/SKID ROW 

[action] On foot now, COLE pulls an astonished RAILLY to a wall covered with 
[action] graffiti, a hopeless tangle of symbols, words, and crude pictures. 

[action] Clueless, RAILLY stares at the wall, then at COLE. 

[action] COLE touches a bit of red-stenciled graffiti hidden under gang 
[action] insignias.  We can just see TWELVE MONKEYS holding hands in a circle. 

[character] COLE 

[dialogue] The Twelve Monkeys!!!  They're here. 
[parenthetical] (looks around) 
[dialogue] Somewhere.  Come on! 

[action] He pulls her along the sidewalk.  No question, he's insane. 

[action] At the next alley entrance, COLE stops abruptly.  Then, still 
[action] keeping a firm grip on RAILLY'S arm, he starts ripping down newly 
[action] tacked-up posters announcing a Rap concert. 

[action] RAILLY stares at him, then turns and is looking all around when, 
[action] suddenly, COLE pulls her up tight and threatens... 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Look, I'm warning you.  You do anything, 
[dialogue] I'm going to go crazy -- hurt people! 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] I'm not going to "do" anything, I 
[dialogue] promise.  But you need help, James. 
[dialogue] None of this is what you think it is. 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, not listening, staring triumphantly!  He's found 
[action] another partially obscured stencil of THE TWELVE MONKEYS! 

[action] But just then, a raspy VOICE startles COLE. 

[character] RASPY VOICE (o.s) 
[dialogue] You can't hide from them, Bob. 

[action] COLE whirls, sees a derelict, LOUIE, leering at him, speaking in a 
[action] voice eerily like the RASPY VOICE from the next cell in the future. 

[character] LOUIE 
[dialogue] No, sir, Old Bob -- don't even try. 
[parenthetical] (conspiratorially) 
[dialogue] They hear everything.  They got that 
[dialogue] tracking device on you.  They can find 
[dialogue] you anywhere.  Anytime.  Ha Ha! 

[action] RAILLY looks from LOUIS to COLE, sees COLE'S stunned reaction. 

[character] LOUIE 
[parenthetical] (touches his back jaw) 
[dialogue] In the tooth, Bob!  Right? 
[parenthetical] (sudden triumphant grin) 
[dialogue] But I fooled 'em, old buddy! 

[action] He opens his mouth wide.  NO TEETH' 

[action] COLE grabs RAILLY and pulls her into the alley and down it. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] They're keeping an eye on me. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Who's keeping an eye on you? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] The man...with the voice.  I recognized 
[dialogue] him.  He's from the present.  He... 

[action] COLE breaks off, freezes as he sees... 

[action] there on a brick wall is a stencil of the DANCING MONKEYS 

[action] And further on, another red stencil! 

[scene_heading] EXT.  VACANT LOT - MOMENTS LATER (DAY) 

[action] CRACKHEADS huddle against a building, sucking their pipes, 
[action] oblivious to COLE pulling RAILLY past. 

[action] COLE scans the walls for messages in the confusion of graffiti. 

[action] RAILLY is considering her surroundings dubiously when, suddenly, 
[action] COLE pulls her toward the mouth of a dark and forbidding alley. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] James, no -- we shouldn't be here! 

[action] COLE ignores her, yanking her after him into the alley. 

[scene_heading] INT.  DARK ALLEY - DAY 

[action] Two TOM CATS face off, arching their backs and HISSING menacingly. 

[action] COLE avoids them as he pulls RAILLY into the gloom. 

[action] ANGLE ON RAILLY, seeing something alarming twenty yards ahead! 

[action] RAILLY'S POV:  TWO THUGS, standing over a MAN, kicking him. 

[action] RAILLY tries to stop, but COLE, intent on the wall messages, 
[action] doesn't notice the THUGS. 

[action] The TWO THUGS turn and spot COLE and RAILLY moving toward them. 

[action] These creeps have mean eyes, predator faces. 

[action] RAILLY digs her heels in, forcing COLE to stop. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] James!  We have to go back.  Those men... 

[action] Too late.  While COLE turns and stares at her, uncomprehending, 
[action] the TWO THUGS are approaching. 

[character] FIRST THUG 
[dialogue] Hey, buddy. 

[action] Startled, COLE turns to face them. 

[action] The SECOND THUG lunges for RAILLY'S purse, yanks it from her. 

[action] COLE reaches to grab it back, but...WHACK!  The FIRST THUG smacks 
[action] COLE hard across the face with something metallic. 

[action] Bloody-faced, dazed, COLE doesn't even have a chance to clear his 
[action] head as the FIRST THUG shoves the hard object against COLE'S 
[action] cheek.  It's a cheap thirty-eight pistol. 

[action] RAILLY turns to run, gets two steps before the SECOND THUG knocks 
[action] her roughly to the ground. 

[character] SECOND THUG 
[dialogue] Stick around, bitch. 

[action] Looming over her, the SECOND THUG starts to unzip his fly. 
[action] RAILLY looks over to COLE, SEES... 

[action] COLE dropping to his knees, groveling at the FIRST THUG'S feet. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Please!  Please don't hurt me! 

[action] The FIRST THUG steps close, kicks COLE contemptuously, cocks his 
[action] foot for a second kick when... 

[action] COLE uncoils, lunging, rising, his strong arms around the bigger 
[action] man's calves, lifting him mightily, high off the ground. 

[action] The gun FIRES wildly as COLE staggers forward with the FIRST THUG 
[action] in his arms and smashes the man into the brick wail behind him. 
[action] The FIRST THUG goes down in a heap, dropping the pistol. 

[action] Zipping his fly hastily, the SECOND THUG turns to deal with COLE 
[action] but COLE attacks him....rocking him again and again with savage 
[action] blows that come one after another with lightning speed.  The SECOND 
[action] THUG staggers back, bloody and dazed as RAILLY watches, amazed. 

[action] Turning back to the FIRST THUG, COLE sees the MAN reaching for 
[action] the dropped pistol. 

[action] COLE kicks him viciously in the jaw.  The FIRST THUG'S head whips 
[action] back.  SNAP!  He collapses against the brick wall. 

[action] COLE turns back to see the SECOND THUG retreating down the alley 
[action] as fast as he can stagger. 

[action] RAILLY stares up at COLE.  He looks very dangerous.  He glances 
[action] in her direction as he pockets the pistol. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Are you hurt? 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Uh, no.  Yes.  I mean, just some scrapes... 

[action] As RAILLY gets to her feet, she sees COLE bend over the 
[action] motionless THUG and quickly go through his pockets. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Is he...alive? 

[action] COLE ignores the question as he pockets the man's wallet and a 
[action] handful of bullets, then turns and snaps at RAILLY. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Come an.  We're running out of time. 
[dialogue] You can't help him. 

[action] As COLE yanks her roughly away, she looks back, sees the FIRST 
[action] THUG'S sightless eyes, wide open...staring blankly. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Oh, Jesus, James!  You killed him! 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I did him a favor.  Now come on. 

[action] COLE, pulling her again, sees more "12 MONKEYS" on the wall. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] You didn't have a gun before, did you? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I've got one now. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  SECOND AVE - DAY 

[action] The EVANGELIST, spotting COLE and RAILLY hurrying past him, 
[action] points urgently at COLE. 

[character] EVANGELIST 
[dialogue] You!  You!  You're one of us, aren't you? 

[action] But COLE has stopped and is staring at... 

[action] A STOREFRONT OFFICE...its windows covered with posters.  The sign 
[action] over the office says, FREEDOM FOR ANIMALS ASSOCIATION. 

[scene_heading] INT.  FAA STORE - MOMENTS LATER (DAY) 

[action] Earnest young activists, FALE, deathly pale, BEN, long haired, 
[action] and TEDDY, muscular, are gathered around a counter collating 
[action] leaflets that demand an END TO SPECIEISM.  Behind them, a large 
[action] poster proclaims, "ANIMALS HAVE SOULS, TOO".  Just then, there's 
[action] a tremendous CLAP OF THUNDER as the ACTIVISTS look up and see 
[action] COLE and RAILLY enter. 

[action] COLE looks startled.  It sounds like torrential RAIN POURING in 
[action] here.  Maintaining a tight grip on RAILLY'S wrist, he looks 
[action] around frantically for an explanation for the tropical downpour. 

[action] Bookshelves line two walls.  The front window is blanked cut with 
[action] posters of Animal Rights demonstrations, newspaper clippings, 
[action] photos of animal atrocities.  The fourth wall features the 

[action] counter where the three ACTIVISTS face COLE as a JUNGLE BIRD 
[action] SCREAMS in the DOWNPOUR. 

[character] FALE 
[dialogue] Uh, can we help you? 

[action] COLE looks confused as the RAIN abates and an ELEPHANT trumpets 
[action] an urgent warning. 

[character] FALE 
[dialogue] Excuse me.  You looking for something 
[dialogue] in particular? 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] It's all right, James -- it's just a tape. 

[action] COLE'S eyes follow her look.  It's a tape recorder underneath a 
[action] sign advertising, "THE TRUE MUSIC OF THE WORLD". 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I, uh, I'm looking for the, ah, the 
[dialogue] Army of the Twelve Monkeys. 

[action] FALE glances at BEN and TEDDY.  "We have a problem!" the look says. 
[action] MONKEYS start CHATTERING on the tape as TEDDY comes around the 
[action] counter, bigger than COLE, physically imposing, menacing. 

[character] TEDDY 
[dialogue] We don't know anything about any "Army 
[dialogue] of the Twelve Monkeys", so why don't 
[dialogue] you and your friend disappear, okay? 

[action] COLE backs away, a firm grip on RAILLY, as a LION ROARS. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I just need some information... 

[character] TEDDY 
[dialogue] Didn't you hear me?  We're not... 

[action] TEDDY breaks off mid-sentence...freezes. 

[action] COLE is pointing a pistol at them.  A TIGER SNARLS. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] James, no -- don't hurt them. 
[parenthetical] (to the activists) 
[dialogue] Please, I'm a psychiatrist -- just do 
[dialogue] whatever he tells you to do.  He's... 
[dialogue] upset -- disturbed.  Please -- he's 
[dialogue] dangerous -- just cooperate. 

[action] MONKEYS CHATTER wildly as TEDDY backs up. 

[character] FALE 
[dialogue] What do you want -- money?  We only 
[dialogue] have a few bucks. 

[action] COLE is suddenly very much in charge and self-confident again.  A 
[action] BABOON HOWLS with laughter. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I told you what I want. 
[parenthetical] (snaps at Railly) 
[dialogue] Lock the door! 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] James, why don't we...? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Lock it now! 

[action] RAILLY hurries to the door to lock it as BEN says to FALE, 

[character] BEN 
[dialogue] I told you that fuckhead Mason would 
[dialogue] get us into something like this. 

[character] FALE 
[dialogue] Shut up! 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Mason??? 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Jeffrey Mason? 

[character] BEN 
[dialogue] Yeah, tucking, crazy Jeffrey Mason. 

[scene_heading] INT.  FAA STORE BASEMENT - TWENTY MINUTES LATER (DAY) 

[action] The three ACTIVISTS are tied tightly together in the middle of 
[action] the floor in this dimly-lit, windowless basement.  They're very 
[action] frightened, eager to cooperate. 

[character] FALE 
[dialogue] Then, Jeffrey becomes like this...big 
[dialogue] star -- the media latch on to him 
[dialogue] because he's picketing his own father, 
[dialogue] a "famous Nobel Prize winning virologist". 
[dialogue] You musta seen all that on TV. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] No, I don't watch TV. 

[action] COLE, the gun next to him, rummages through boxes of papers while 
[action] RAILLY watches helplessly.  Suddenly, COLE finds something he 
[action] thinks he's seen before.  He holds it up. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Is this him -- Dr. Mason? 

[action] It's a photograph of DR. MALCOLM MASON, being escorted by a 

[action] phalanx of riot cops through a mob of raging activists. 

[character] FALE 
[dialogue] That's him. 

[character] BEN 
[parenthetical] (very frightened) 
[dialogue] What are you going to do with us? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] (stares at the photo, then) 
[dialogue] Tell me more about Jeffrey. 

[character] FALE 
[dialogue] (a helpless shrug to his cohorts) 
[dialogue] Jeffrey started getting bored with the 
[dialogue] shit we do...picketing, leafleting, 
[dialogue] letter-writing stuff.  He said we were, 
[dialogue] "ineffectual liberal jerkoffs".  He 
[dialogue] wanted to do guerrilla "actions" to 
[dialogue] "educate" the public. 

[action] COLE holds up a clipping showing horrified SENATORS standing on 
[action] their desks as RATTLESNAKES slither along the Senate Floor. 

[character] FALE 
[dialogue] Yeah, that's when he let a hundred 
[dialogue] snakes loose in the Senate. 

[character] TEDDY 
[dialogue] But we weren't into that kind of stuff. 
[dialogue] It's counter productive, we told him. 

[character] FALE 
[dialogue] So he and eleven others split off and 
[dialogue] became this underground..."army" 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] The Army of The Twelve Monkeys. 

[character] BEN 
[dialogue] They started planning a "Human Hunt". 

[character] TEDDY 
[dialogue] They bought stun guns and nets and bear 
[dialogue] traps.  They were gonna go to Wall 
[dialogue] Street and trap lawyers and bankers... 

[character] BEN 
[dialogue] But they didn't do it.  They didn't do 
[dialogue] any of it. 

[character] TEDDY 
[dialogue] Yeah, just like always, Mr. Big Shot 
[dialogue] sold his friends out! 

[character] COLE 

[dialogue] What's that mean? 

[character] FALE 
[dialogue] He goes on TV, gives a news conference, 
[dialogue] tells the whole world he just realized 
[dialogue] his daddy's experiments are vital for 
[dialogue] humanity and that the use of animals is 
[dialogue] absolutely necessary and that he, Jeffrey 
[dialogue] Mason, from now on, is going to personally 
[dialogue] supervise the labs to make sure all the 
[dialogue] little animals aren't going to suffer. 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (holding up a rolodex) 
[dialogue] What's this? 

[scene_heading] EXT.  FREEWAY - AFTERNOON 

[action] In the crawling traffic, WE FIND a battered FORD covered with 
[action] bumper stickers and painted slogans.  "I BRAKE FOR ANIMALS"... 
[action] "FREE THE ANIMALS"..."WOULD YOU LET A MINK WEAR YOUR SKIN?" 

[character] RAILLY (v.o.) 
[dialogue] You can't just barge in on a famous 
[dialogue] scientist.  They'll have security guards, 
[dialogue] gates, alarm systems.  It's insane, James. 

[scene_heading] INT.  MOVING FORD/FREEWAY 

[action] A ROLODEX CARD with an address on "Outerbridge Road" for "Jeffrey 
[action] Mason c/o Dr. Malcolm Mason" rests on a map spread across COLE'S 
[action] lap.  COLE is in the passenger seat, RAILLY'S at the wheel, 
[action] maneuvering in heavy traffic. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] If those young men don't get loose, 
[dialogue] they could die in that basement. 

[action] COLE glances out the window, indicates the PEOPLE in passing 
[action] cars...COMMUTERS, FAMILIES, TRUCKERS. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] All I see are dead people.  Everywhere. 
[dialogue] What's three more? 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] (a beat, carefully, a new tack) 
[dialogue] You know Dr. Mason's son, Jeffrey 
[dialogue] Mason, don't you, James?  You met him 
[dialogue] in the County Hospital six years ago. 

[action] COLE is studying the map again. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] The guy was a total fruitcake. 

[character] RAILLY 

[dialogue] And he told you then his father was a 
[dialogue] famous virologist. 

[action] COLE is absorbed in the map, his finger tracing "Outerbridge Road". 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] No -- he said his father was "God"! 

[scene_heading] EXT./INT.  FORD/COUNTRY HIGHWAY - LATER (DAY) 

[action] The RADIO BLARES a country song as the Ford zips along an open 
[action] highway.  COLE has his head out the window, sucking air, loving 
[action] the music, but his bliss is feverish now -- he's not well.  As 
[action] the SONG ends, he pulls his head inside.  An ANNOUNCER'S VOICE 
[action] intones over the RADIO... 

[character] RADIO ANNOUNCER (o.s.) 
[dialogue] This just in:  police are widening 
[dialogue] their search for Dr. Kathryn Railly, 
[dialogue] prominent psychiatrist and author. 
[dialogue] Authorities confirm that Dr. Railly has 
[dialogue] been abducted by escaped mental patient, 
[dialogue] James Cole. The two are believed to be 
[dialogue] traveling in Railly's 1992 black Acura, 
[dialogue] license plate H-E-A-D-D-R. 

[action] RAILLY glances at him, sees he's in pain.  She feels so badly for 
[action] him.  She wants to help him.  She says, tenderly... 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] This can't go on, James.  You're not 
[dialogue] well.  You're burning with fever. 

[action] COLE, refusing to succumb, instead, leans over to check the gas gauge. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] We need gas. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] I thought you didn't know how to drive. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I said I was too young to drive.  I 
[dialogue] didn't say I was stupid. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] What's the matter with your leg? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I got shot.  Look -- there's a gas 
[dialogue] station up ahead. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Shot!  Who shot you? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] It was some kind of...war.  Never mind, 

[dialogue] you wouldn't believe me.  Turn off here. 

[scene_heading] INT.  PARKED FORD/GAS STATION - MINUTES LATER (AFTERNOON) 

[action] The GAS STATION ATTENDANT checks the oil while COLE and RAILLY 
[action] remain in the car.  She's pulling a gas card from her wallet. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] You were going to run out off gas on 
[dialogue] purpose, weren't you? 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] No.  I want you to turn yourself in, James -- 
[dialogue] It'll go much better for you if you do 
[dialogue] -- but I'm not going to trick you. 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (sees the credit card) 
[dialogue] That has your name on it.  Give him cash. 

[action] RAILLY puts the card back into her wallet and pulls out cash as 
[action] the GAS STATION ATTENDANT slams the hood down. 

[action] RAILLY starts opening the door.  Alarmed, COLE tries to stop her. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Where are you going? 

[action] She looks him in the eye, then indicates the tiny Convenience 
[action] Store appended to the Gas Station. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] You can come with me.  I have to get 
[dialogue] some things.  Scissors, bandages, some 
[dialogue] alcohol or whiskey. ... I have to look 
[dialogue] at your leg, James.  I'm a doctor. 

[action] COLE looks helpless, hesitant.  She's in charge...for the moment. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  CLEARING/WOODS - AN HOUR LATER (AFTERNOON) 

[action] The sun dazzles through the canopy of leaves.  We HEAR the CAR 
[action] RADIO but not the engine. 

[character] RADIO ANNOUNCER (v.o.) 
[dialogue] Meanwhile in Fresno, where mining 
[dialogue] engineers continue their desperate 
[dialogue] attempt to sink a shaft parallel to the 
[dialogue] ant in which nine year old... 

[action] COLE, in his underwear, leans back on a large rock beside the 
[action] Ford, his pants hanging on the car's open door.  He's staring up 
[action] at the sun and the sky.  RAILLY finishes bandaging his thigh. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] You shouldn't put your weight on it. 
[dialogue] You need stitches and antibiotics. 

[dialogue] Lucky for you it was near the surface. 

[action] RAILLY wraps the bullet in some gauze and sticks it in her pocket 
[action] while COLE continues staring up at the sky. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I love seeing the sun. 

[action] A beat.  COLE tries to stand up. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Wait -- let me help you. 

[action] RAILLY puts an arm around him and helps him to his feet.  A beat. 
[action] They're very close.  They don't move.  RAILLY looks like she can 
[action] barely breathe. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] (leans closer, shuts his eyes) 
[dialogue] You smell so good. 

[character] RAILLY 
[parenthetical] (trying to concentrate) 
[dialogue] You have to give yourself up, you know. 

[action] A beat.  The spell is broken.  He reaches for his pants, then 
[action] turns back to her, suddenly grim. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I have to do something now.  Something 
[dialogue] I don't want to do.  I'm so sorry. 

[action] RAILLY reacts, sudden fear in her eyes.  He looms over her.  He's 
[action] cold now, steeled. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I have a mission.  It's important. 

[action] RAILLY steps back...horrified, realizing she's going to die. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  MASON MANSION - NIGHT 

[action] A SECRET SERVICE AGENT ambles vigilantly among the rows of luxury 
[action] cars parked beside the brightly-lit rural mansion.  Encountering 
[action] another AGENT, he pauses. 

[character] FIRST AGENT 
[dialogue] They find him? 

[character] SECOND AGENT 
[dialogue] Who?? 

[character] FIRST AGENT 
[dialogue] That kid.  The one in the pipe. 

[character] SECOND AGENT 
[dialogue] You believe this?  They're dropping a monkey 

[dialogue] down there with a miniature infra-red camera 
[dialogue] strapped on him and a roast beef sandwich 
[dialogue] wrapped in tinfoil. 

[character] FIRST AGENT 
[dialogue] You're making that up! 

[action] ANGLE UNDER A PARKED MERCEDES, where COLE is hiding, listening to 
[action] the receding VOICES of the AGENTS. 

[character] SECOND AGENT (o.s.) 
[dialogue] I shit you not. ... Man, life is weird! 
[dialogue] A monkey and a sandwich.  Wonder who 
[dialogue] thought that one up. 

[character] FIRST AGENT (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Probly give the sonafabitch a Nobel 
[dialogue] Prize! 

[action] Quickly, COLE rolls to the next car and under it.  He doesn't 
[action] see...the pistol fell out of his pocket, under the Mercedes. 

[scene_heading] INT.  MASON MANSION/DINING ROOM - NIGHT 

[action] A formal dinner for forty.  Desert has been served.  DR. MALCOLM 
[action] MASON rises to the enthusiastic applause of the GUESTS. 

[character] DR. MASON 
[dialogue] Would that I could enjoy this opulent 
[dialogue] dinner and this excellent and 
[dialogue] stimulating company for itself, with no 
[dialogue] sense of purpose.  But alas, I am 
[dialogue] "burdened" with the sense that with all 
[dialogue] this excess of public attention and 
[dialogue] this cacophony of praise, there comes 
[dialogue] great responsibility.  Indeed, I 
[dialogue] practically feel a soapbox growing 
[dialogue] under my feet whenever I stand for more 
[dialogue] than a few seconds. 

[action] While GUESTS laugh at DR. MASON'S last remark, SECRET SERVICE 
[action] AGENT #3 enters the room, scowling, looking for someone. 

[character] DR. MASON (o.s.) 
[dialogue] The dangers of science are a time worn 
[dialogue] threat, from Prometheus stealing fire 
[dialogue] from the Gods to the Cold War era of 
[dialogue] the Dr. Strangelove Terror. 

[action] AGENT #3 spots who he's looking for.  JEFFREY MASON! 

[character] DR. MASON (cont.) 
[dialogue] But never before, not even at Los Alamos 
[dialogue] when the scientists made bets on whether 
[dialogue] their first atomic bomb test would wipe out 
[dialogue] New Mexico, has science given us so much 
[dialogue] reason to fear the power we have at hand. 

[action] ANGLE ON JEFFREY, as AGENT #3 whispers in his ear. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] What are you talking about?  What 
[dialogue] friend?  I'm not expecting anyone. 

[action] ANGLE ON DR. MASON, reacting with irritation to the disturbance. 

[character] DR. MASON 
[dialogue] Current genetic engineering as well as 
[dialogue] my own work with viruses has presented 
[dialogue] us with powers as terrifying as any... 

[action] ANGLE ON JEFFREY, following the AGENT out of the dining room, 
[action] grumbling loudly enough to disturb his father's audience. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] This is ridiculous.  My father is 
[dialogue] making a major address. 

[scene_heading] INT.  HALLWAY/MASON MANSION 

[action] The conversation continues as JEFFREY and AGENT #3 enter the hall. 

[character] AGENT #3 
[dialogue] Normally if we caught a guy sneaking 
[dialogue] around like this with no I.D., we'd 
[dialogue] bust his ass, excuse the French, but 
[dialogue] this one said he knows you... 
[parenthetical] (smirk, smirk) 
[dialogue] -- and, since you seem to have had 
[dialogue] some...uh...unusual...uh..."associates", 
[dialogue] we certainly didn't want to arrest one 
[dialogue] of your, uh, closest...pals. 

[scene_heading] INT.  LIBRARY/MASON MANSION 

[action] COLE, smudged with dirt and car grease, sitting in the shadows in 
[action] a wingback chair, looks up as JEFFREY and AGENT #3 enter the 
[action] room.  A FOURTH AGENT looms beside the wingback chair. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] (dismissing Cole casually) 
[dialogue] Never saw him before in my life.  Go 
[dialogue] ahead and shoot him or torture him or 
[dialogue] whatever it is you do. 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (rising) 
[dialogue] You do know me.  You helped me once. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[parenthetical] (turning to leave) 
[dialogue] That would be totally out of character. 
[dialogue] Helping people is against my principles. 
[parenthetical] (to the Agents) 

[dialogue] See, he definitely doesn't know me.  Now, 
[dialogue] I'm going to go back and listen to my 
[dialogue] father's very eloquent discourse on the 
[dialogue] perils of science WHILE YOU TORTURE THIS 
[dialogue] INTRUDER TO DEATH. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I'm here about some monkeys. 

[action] Halfway out the door, JEFFREY freezes.  A beat. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Excuse me -- what did you say? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Monkeys.  Twelve of them. 

[action] JEFFREY frowns, turns, considers COLE.  Then, suddenly, JEFFREY 
[action] rushes to COLE and embraces him. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Arnold...Arnold. 

[action] COLE is astonished.  The AGENTS are, too. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[parenthetical] (stepping back) 
[dialogue] My God, Arnie, what's happened to you? 
[dialogue] You look like shit 

[character] AGENT #3 
[parenthetical] (dubious) 
[dialogue] You know this man? 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Of course I know him.  What do you 
[dialogue] think -- I act like this to strangers? 
[dialogue] Listen -- you fellas are terrific.  I 
[dialogue] thought you were pulling a number on 
[dialogue] me.  What a terrible thing if you'd 
[dialogue] thrown old Arnie out.  I owe you guys 
[dialogue] the big apologia!  Mea culpa, fellas. 
[parenthetical] (turning to Cole) 
[dialogue] Christ, Arnie, it's black tie!  I mean, 
[dialogue] I said, "drop by," but, like, this is 
[dialogue] Dad's big "do"...vips, senators, secret 
[dialogue] service, and...and everything. 

[action] JEFFREY throws an arm around COLE'S shoulder and starts leading 
[action] him toward the door as the two AGENTS exchange narrow-eyed looks. 

[character] AGENT #4 
[dialogue] "Arnie?" 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Arnold Pettibone.  Old Arnie Pettibone. 
[dialogue] Used to be my best friend.  Still is. 

[dialogue] What've you lost, Arnie -- forty pounds? 
[dialogue] No wonder I didn't know you. You hungry? 
[dialogue] Lots of dead cow, dead lamb, dead pig. 
[dialogue] Real killer feast we're putting on tonight. 

[action] The AGENTS watch JEFFREY lead the limping, disheveled COLE out. 

[character] AGENT #4 
[dialogue] These people -- all of 'em -- are true 
[dialogue] weirdoes! 

[character] AGENT #3 
[parenthetical] (moving to the phone) 
[dialogue] I'm gonna call in a description of this 
[dialogue] "Pettibone" character.  You go keep an 
[dialogue] eye on him.  Make sure he doesn't do 
[dialogue] one of the guests with a fork. 

[scene_heading] INT.  HALLWAY/MASON MANSION 

[action] GUESTS pouring from the dining room into the hall meet JEFFREY 
[action] and a very disconcerted COLE. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Hey, nice ta see ya.  Lookin' good!  Hi, 
[dialogue] there.  Yes, it has been a long time. 

[action] In the b.g., too far away to hear them, AGENT #4 trails JEFFREY and 
[action] COLE as they maneuver through the GUESTS toward the grand staircase. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[parenthetical] (whispers to Cole) 
[dialogue] County Hospital, right?  1989.  The 
[dialogue] "Immaculate Escape" -- am I right? 
[parenthetical] (smiling to guests) 
[dialogue] Why, thank you -- you look wonderful, too. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Listen to me -- I can't do anything 
[dialogue] about what you're going to do. I can't 
[dialogue] change anything.  I can't stop you.  I 
[dialogue] just want some information... 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] We need to talk.  Come on.  Upstairs. 
[parenthetical] (to a guest) 
[dialogue] I am a new person!  I'm completely 
[dialogue] adjusted.  Witness the tux.  It's Armani. 
[parenthetical] (whispers to Cole) 
[dialogue] Who chattered?  Goines?  Weller? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I just need to have access to the pure 
[dialogue] virus, that's all!  For the future! 

[action] JEFFREY studies COLE.  COLE doesn't just talk crazy.  He looks crazy! 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Come on, follow me.  You don't lock so good. 

[action] JEFFREY starts guiding COLE up the grand staircase as COLE, glancing 
[action] back, spots AGENT #3 and AGENT #4, both keeping an eye on him now. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I don't have time to go upstairs.  The 
[dialogue] police are looking for me.  I need to 
[dialogue] know where it is and exactly what it is. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[parenthetical] (brightening suddenly) 
[dialogue] I get it!  This is your old plan, right? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Plan?  What are you talking about? 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Remember?  We were in the dayroom, 
[dialogue] watching TV, and you were all upset 
[dialogue] about the...desecration of the planet. 
[dialogue] And you said to me, "Wouldn't it be 
[dialogue] great if there was a germ or a virus 
[dialogue] that could wipe out mankind and leave 
[dialogue] the plants and animals just as they 
[dialogue] are?"  You do remember that, don't you? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Bulishit!  You're fucking with my head! 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] And that's when I told you my father was 
[dialogue] this famous virologist and you said, "Hey, 
[dialogue] he could make a germ and we could steal it!" 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (grabbing Jeffrey) 
[dialogue] Listen, you dumb fuck!  The thing mutates 
[dialogue] We live underground!  The world belongs 
[dialogue] to the fucking dogs and cats.  We're 
[dialogue] like moles or worms.  All we want to do 
[dialogue] is study the original... 

[character] AGENT #4'S VOICE 
[dialogue] Okay -- take it easy.  We know who you 
[dialogue] are, Mr. Cole. 

[action] COLE feels a firm grip on his shoulder, turns and sees AGERT #4 

[character] AGENT #4 
[dialogue] Let's go somewhere and talk this thing 
[dialogue] over. Okay? Just come with me... 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] You're right!  Absolutely right.  Me's 
[dialogue] a nut case, totally deranged.  Delusional! 

[dialogue] Paranoid.  HIS PROCESSOR'S ALL FUCKED 
[dialogue] UP, HIS INFORMATION TRAY IS JAMMED. 

[action] AGENT #4 is wishing JEFFREY would chill out even as the THIRD 
[action] AGENT is climbing up the staircase to help. 

[action] COLE is like a trapped animal. He's being led down the staircase 
[action] now with JEFFREY, right on his heels, yelling so EVERYONE can hear. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] YOU KNOW WHAT IT IS, THE "ARMY OF THE 
[dialogue] TWELVE MONKEYS"?  IT'S A COLLECTION OF 
[dialogue] NATURE KOOKS WHO RUN A STORE DOWNTOWN. 
[dialogue] SPACE-CASE DO-GOODERS SAVING RAIN 
[dialogue] FORESTS. I HAVE NOTHING TO DO WITH 
[dialogue] THOSE BOZOS ANYMORE. I QUIT BEING THE 
[dialogue] RICH KID FALL GUY FOR A BUNCH OF 
[dialogue] INEFFECTUAL BANANAS. SO MUCH FOR YOUR 
[dialogue] GRAND PLOT! 

[action] COLE stares back at JEFFREY as both AGENTS hustle COLE down the 
[action] stairs.  It sounds true!  JEFFREY'S so confident. 

[character] AGENT #3 
[dialogue] Take it easy, Mr. Mason, we've got him. 
[dialogue] Everything's... 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] MY FATHER HAS BEEN WARNING PEOPLE ABOUT 
[dialogue] THE DANGERS OF EXPERIMENTATION WITH 
[dialogue] VIRUSES AND DNA FOR YEARS. YOU'VE 
[dialogue] "PROCESSED" THAT INFORMATION THROUGH 
[centered] YOUR ADDLED PARANOID INFRA-STRUCTURE AND 
[dialogue] LO AND BEHOLD, I'M FRANKENSTEIN AND THE 
[dialogue] "ARMY OF THE TWELVE MONKEYS" BECOMES 
[dialogue] SOME SORT OF SINISTER REVOLUTIONARY 
[dialogue] CABAL. THIS MAN IS TOTALLY BATSHIT! YOU 
[dialogue] KNOW WHERE HE THINKS HE COMES FROM??? 

[action] Suddenly, COLE, catching the AGENTS by surprise, wrenches free, 
[action] shoves them aside, and stumbles down the rest of the staircase. 

[scene_heading] INT.  FOYER/MASON MANSION 

[action] COLE heads for the front door, but there's an AGENT there!  COLE 
[action] turns and limps madly toward the dining room, pushing his way 
[action] through the crowd of amazed GUESTS. 

[scene_heading] INT.  DINING ROOM/MASON MANSION 

[action] SERVANTS, clearing the table, look up astonished as two AGENTS 
[action] burst into the room. 

[character] AGENT #4 
[dialogue] Did a man just come through here...limping? 

[scene_heading] INT.  KITCHEN/MASON MANSION 

[action] COOKS stare, amazed, as two OTHER AGENTS burst into the kitchen 
[action] and look about urgently. 

[scene_heading] INT.  DEN/MASON MANSION 

[action] A large projection TV is on and a knot of GUESTS is gathered in 
[action] front of it...watching the spooky VIDEO IMAGES. 

[character] TV REPORTER (v.o.) 
[dialogue] These pictures we are seeing are coming 
[dialogue] to us live from deep inside the pipe. 
[dialogue] You can just make out the metal wall 
[dialogue] behind those roots and I guess those 
[dialogue] must be spider webs. 

[action] MRS. McCANN, a guest, watching the TV, expresses concern... 

[character] MRS. McCANN 
[dialogue] Well, if you ask me, I think that monkey 
[dialogue] is going to eat the sandwich himself. 

[action] Just then, two AGENTS burst into the den. 

[action] The GUESTS turn from the TV, startled, stare open-mouthed, but 
[action] the AGENTS have spotted an open window and are hurrying to it. 

[action] AGENTS POV OUT THE WINDOW:  the rows of expensive parked CARS. 

[action] ON THE TV SCREEN, RAILLY'S photograph appears. 

[character] TV ANCHOR (v.o.) 
[dialogue] This just in:  Police say that the body of 
[dialogue] a woman found strangled in the Knutson state 
[dialogue] Park could be kidnap victim, Dr. Kathryn Railly. 

[action] As the AGENTS run out of the room, a photo of RAILLY'S abandoned 
[action] Acura comes up on the TV screen. 

[character] TV ANCHOR (v.o.) 
[dialogue] Earlier in the day, police located 
[dialogue] Railly's abandoned car not far from a 
[dialogue] building where three animal rights 
[dialogue] activists were found bound and gagged... 

[scene_heading] EXT.  MASON MANSION - MINUTES LATER (NIGHT) 

[action] Pistols drawn, AGENTS move cautiously among the rows of parked 
[action] luxury cars, checking inside and under the vehicles. 

[character] AN AGENT'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] COME ON OUT, MR COLE -- WE'RE NOT GOING 
[dialogue] TO HURT YOU. 

[scene_heading] INT.  PARKED GREEN JAGUAR 

[action] COLE, scrunched down on the floor next to the driver's seat, 

[action] spots the key dangling from the ignition, then lifts his head 
[action] slightly to study the shift mechanism, trying to figure it out. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  PARKED CARS/MASON MANSION 

[action] AGENTS continue to move cautiously among the parked cars. 

[scene_heading] INT.  PARKED GREEN JAGUAR 

[action] COLE eases himself into the driver's seat, tentatively slides the 
[action] shift into "Drive", then turns the key.  Nothing happens. 

[action] Panic.  COLE studies the shift again. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  PARKED CARS/MANSION 

[action] AGENT #5 approaches the row where the Jaguar is parked. 

[scene_heading] INT.  PARKED GREEN JAGUAR 

[action] COLE slides the shift from D (Drive) to N (Neutral).  He twists 
[action] the key again.  The engine ROARS...SEVEN THOUSAND RPM! 

[scene_heading] EXT.  LAWN/MANSION 

[action] AGENT #5 whirls at the sound. 

[action] SMASH.  The JAGUAR clips the Mercedes parked in front of it and 
[action] accelerates right at him! 

[action] AGENT #5 dives aside just as the speeding JAGUAR whizzes past 
[action] him, slams into a parked Cadillac, bounces off, grinds between 
[action] two other parked vehicles with a fierce scream of tearing metal. 

[scene_heading] INT.  MOVING JAGUAR 

[action] Caught between two cars, COLE can only lean on the gas pedal. 

[action] The JAGUAR comes free with a great SCCCREEEEECH... 

[action] COLE sees the driveway ahead in the moonlight.  Steering madly, 
[action] he plows through shrubs and gardens heading for the driveway. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  MASON MANSION - NIGHT 

[action] Lights off, veering wildly, the JAGUAR reaches the driveway. 

[action] AGENTS are leaping into cars and a HELICOPTER is coming to life, 
[action] its rotors whipping around. 

[scene_heading] INT.  SPEEDING JAGUAR/OUTERHRIDGE ?OAD - NIGHT 

[action] COLE turns onto the road, careening crazily from one side to the 
[action] other, unable to see ahead with no headlights. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] LIGHTS!  LIGHTS! 

[action] He starts hitting switches.  The wipers come on, the RADIO BLARES. 

[character] RADIO REPORTER'S VOICE/RADIO (o.s.) 
[dialogue] ---when they pulled the monkey out, it 
[dialogue] was still clutching the tinfoil wrapped 
[dialogue] sandwich. 

[action] Rounding a bend, an ONCOMING CAR heads straight at COLE. 

[action] COLE yanks the wheel as the OTHER CAR, horn BLARING, just misses him. 

[action] Recovering, COLE loses the road, speeds crazily along the shoulder. 

[scene_heading] INT.  FLYING HELICOPTER - NIGHT 

[action] The PILOT, an agent, steers the chopper while the CO-PILOT pans a 
[action] spotlight over the two lane road beneath them. 

[action] Just then, the PILOT sees headlights below. 

[character] PILOT 
[dialogue] There!  He's showing lights. 

[scene_heading] INT.  SPEEDING JAGUAR - NIGHT 

[action] COLE can see the road now in his headlights as the windshield 
[action] wipers scrape frantically and the RADIO BLARES... 

[character] RADIO REPORTER'S VOICE/RADIO (o.s.) 
[dialogue] We don't know what to think.  They 
[dialogue] didn't locate him and they don't know 
[dialogue] how much longer he can last, that's 
[dialogue] assuming the boy is still alive. 

[action] A spotlight hits the car and COLE hears the sound of the 
[action] HELICOPTER as it lowers over him! 

[scene_heading] EXT.  OUTERBRIDGE ROAD 

[action] The HELICOPTER maneuvers over the speeding JAGUAR. 

[scene_heading] INT.  SPEEDING JAGUAR 

[action] COLE can see the underbelly of the HELICOPTER a few feet above 
[action] his front windshield. 

[action] Desperate, he yanks the wheel hard, veering off the road. 

[action] COLE'S POV THROUGH THE WINDSHIELD:  dense woods ahead. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  HELICOPTER/WOODS 

[action] The chopper pulls up sharply, avoiding the trees, then levels out. 

[scene_heading] INT.  HELICOPTER 

[action] The PILOT skims the top of the trees while the CO-PILOT rakes the 
[action] forest below with his spotlight. 

[character] PILOT 
[dialogue] Goddamnit!  Where is he? 

[action] The CO-PILOT gets a brief glimpse of headlights through the leaves. 

[character] CO-PILOT 
[dialogue] There!  Over there. 

[character] PILOT 
[dialogue] Where? 
[dialogue] CO-PILOT 
[dialogue] Eight o'clock!  He was right there. 

[action] The headlights are gone.  Nothing but darkness below. 

[character] PILOT 
[dialogue] He musta turned his lights of if. 

[character] CO-PILOT 
[dialogue] Couldn't drive down there without 
[dialogue] lights.  We just can't see 'em. 

[character] PILOT 
[dialogue] Maybe he's not driving! 

[scene_heading] EXT.  WOODS - LATER (NIGHT) 

[action] A weather forecast BLARES from the radio of the steaming Jaguar, 
[action] crumpled into a tree, the driver's door open. 

[action] A POLICE OFFICER, pistol drawn, approaches the car cautiously, as 
[action] OTHER OFFICERS and AGENTS stay behind trees, weapons ready. 

[action] The POLICE OFFICER lunges forward, pointing his weapon into the 
[action] Jaguar.  He inspects the car, then turns and calls out... 

[character] POLICE OFFICER 
[dialogue] He's not in here. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  WOODS/CLEARING - NIGHT 

[action] Limping, bleeding from various cuts, COLE CRASHES through 
[action] underbrush as he follows a stream through the woods. 

[action] Suddenly, he sees what he's looking for. 

[action] The FORD...barely visible in the moonlight, parked in the trees 
[action] beside the stream.  The car looks empty. 

[scene_heading] INT.  TRUNK/FORD 

[action] Total blackness!  The sound of keys in the lock. 

[action] Then, the trunk swings open and COLE stands in the moonlight, 

[action] looking down 

[action] RAILLY is in the trunk, tears of rage and frustration in her eyes. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] You bastard!  You total bastard! 

[scene_heading] EXT.  FORD 

[action] COLE backs away, as RAILLY scrambles out of the trunk, swinging. 

[action] He slips, falls, and she starts kicking him as she rants hysterically. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] I could have died in there.  If something 
[dialogue] had happened to you I would have died. 

[action] COLE is lying on the ground, looking up, his lip caked with blood. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I...I...I'm really sorry. 

[action] Noticing his cuts and torn clothes, she stops kicking him. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] What have you done?  Did you...kill someone? 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (getting to his feet) 
[dialogue] No!  I...don't think so.  I stole a car 
[dialogue] and they chased me.  I hit a tree. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] See -- you can drive after all! 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Yeah, sort of, I guess.  I...I'm sorry 
[dialogue] I locked you up.  I thought...I thought... 
[dialogue] I think maybe I am crazy! 

[action] She looks at him.  Breakthrough?  Very calm now, the doctor. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] What made you think that? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Jeffrey Mason said it was my idea about 
[dialogue] the virus.  And suddenly, I wasn't 
[dialogue] sure.  We talked when I was in the 
[dialogue] institution, and it was all...fuzzy. 
[dialogue] The drugs and stuff. 
[parenthetical] (horrified) 
[dialogue] You think maybe I'm the one who wiped 
[dialogue] out the human race?  It was my idea? 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Nobody is going to wipe out the human 

[dialogue] race.  Not you or Jeffrey or anybody 
[dialogue] else.  You've created something in your 
[dialogue] mind, James -- a substitute reality. 
[dialogue] In order to avoid something you don't 
[dialogue] want to face. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I'm..."mentally divergent".  I would 
[dialogue] love to believe that. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] It can be dealt with, but only if you 
[dialogue] want to.  I can help you. 

[action] COLE reacts to the sound of VOICES in the woods, dogs BARKING. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I need help all right.  They're coming 
[dialogue] after me. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] First, it's important that you 
[dialogue] surrender to them instead of them 
[dialogue] catching you running.  Okay? 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (brightening) 
[dialogue] It would be great if I'm crazy.  If I'm 
[dialogue] wrong about everything...the world will be 
[dialogue] okay.  I'll never have to live underground. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Give me the gun. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] The gun! ... I lost it 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] You're sure? 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (showing her) 
[dialogue] No gun! 
[parenthetical] (looking up) 
[dialogue] Stars!  Air!  I can live here.  Breathe! 

[action] RAILLY starts around to the front of the car. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] I'm going to attract their attention, 
[dialogue] let them know where we are, okay? 

[action] RAILLY gets in the driver's seat...and starts to HONK the horn. 

[character] RAILLY 
[parenthetical] (calling out) 
[dialogue] They'll tell you to put your hands on top 

[dialogue] of your head.  Do what they tell you.  You're 
[dialogue] going to get better, James -- I know it! 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, spotting something on the ground.  An insect!  He 
[action] reaches down to grab it, but, instead, grins, grabs some grass, 
[action] stands, and starts rubbing it happily all over his face.  The 
[action] HORN BLARES as COLE looks up at the sky. 

[action] ANGLE ON THE NIGHT SKY, the moon full, the sky rich with stars. 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, tears of joy running down his face. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I love this world! 

[action] ANGLE ON RAILLY, in the driver's seat, hearing near-by SHOUTS 
[action] from the woods.  The police are almost here.  She gets out of the 
[action] car, starts around toward COLE. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Remember, I'm going to help you.  I'll 
[dialogue] stay with you.  I won't let them... 

[action] She breaks off mid-sentence...stares, stunned! 

[action] COLE is gone.  Disappeared. 

[scene_heading] INT.  POLICE STATION OFFICE - MORNING 

[action] RAILLY is being "debriefed" by POLICE OFFICERS and FBI AGENTS. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Then I said something to him about 
[dialogue] cooperating and he said he would do 
[dialogue] that, so I got in the car and started 
[dialogue] honking the horn.  When I got out, he 
[dialogue] was gone. 

[character] LIEUTENANT HALPERIN 
[dialogue] You lucked out.  For a while we thought 
[dialogue] you were a body they found down state... 
[dialogue] mutilated. 

[action] A COP enters, hands a photo to LIEUTENANT HALPERIN who studies it. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] He wouldn't do something like that -- he... 

[character] LIEUTENANT RALPERIN 
[dialogue] (interrupts, hands her the photo) 
[dialogue] This the man he attacked? 

[action] RAILLY looks at the photo, an 8 x 10 of the FIRST THUG, slumped 
[action] against the alley wall, obviously dead. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] I'd like to be clear about this.  That 

[dialogue] man and the other one were..."severely" 
[dialogue] beating us.  James Cole didn't start 
[dialogue] it.  In fact -- he saved me! 

[character] LIEUTENANT RALPERIN 
[dialogue] Funny thing, Doctor, maybe you can 
[dialogue] explain it to me, you being a psychiatrist 
[dialogue] -- why do kidnap victims almost always 
[dialogue] try to tell us about the guys who grabbed 
[dialogue] 'em and try to make us understand how 
[dialogue] kind these bastards really were? 

[character] RAILLY 
[parenthetical] (as if reciting) 
[dialogue] It's a normal reaction to a life- 
[dialogue] threatening situation. 
[parenthetical] (suddenly animated) 
[dialogue] He's sick.  He thinks he comes from the 
[dialogue] future.  He's been living in a carefully 
[dialogue] constructed fantasy world and that world 
[dialogue] is starting to disintegrate.  He needs 
[dialogue] help! 

[scene_heading] INT.  AIRPORT CONCOURSE/THE DREAM 

[action] YOUNG COLE stares, eyes wide. 

[action] He sees the BRUNETTE, cradling the head of the BLONDE MAN as he 
[action] sprawls on the concourse... 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST'S VOICE (O.S.) 
[dialogue] Wake up! Wake up! 

[character] GEOLOGIST'S VOICE (O.S.) 
[dialogue] I think we gave him too much. 

[character] MICROBIOLOGIST'S VOICE (O.S.) 
[dialogue] WAKE UP, PRISONER! 

[scene_heading] INT.  SCIENTISTS' CHAMBER - ETERNAL NIGHT 

[action] COLE blinks awake. All he can see are blurry faces hovering over 
[action] him, hammering him with questions. 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 
[dialogue] Come on, Cole, cooperate! 

[character] GEOLOGIST 
[dialogue] Spit it out... you went to the home of 
[dialogue] a famous virologist... 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (weakly) 
[dialogue] You...don't...exist!  You're in 
[dialogue] my mind... 

[character] SCIENTISTS (IN RAPID SUCCESSION) 

[dialogue] What?  What's that?  What did he say?  His 
[dialogue] brain's fried.  Give him another shot! 
[dialogue] SPEAK UP, COLE.  WHAT DID YOU DO NEXT? 

[scene_heading] INT.  RAILLY'S APARTMENT - EVENING 

[action] The TV shows film of RAILLY leaving the police station. 

[character] TV REPORTER (v.o.) 
[dialogue] The kidnap victim seemed exhausted but 
[dialogue] apparently unharmed by her 30 hour ordeal 
[dialogue] as she left the police station in 
[dialogue] Philadelphia this morning.  So far she 
[dialogue] has refused to make a public statement. 

[action] RAILLY'S friends, MARILOU and WAYNE, are watching the TV. 

[action] A door opens and KATHRYN RAILLY, wearing a robe, comes out of her 
[action] bedroom.  She still looks exhausted   Followed by her cat, she 
[action] enters the kitchen area and turns on the kettle as WAYNE hastily 
[action] turns down the TV. 

[character] WAYNE 
[dialogue] Sorry. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] No -- I'm in a state of hyper- 
[dialogue] alertness.  I can't sleep. 

[character] MARILOU 
[dialogue] Did you take the sedative? 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] I hate those things.  They mess my head 
[dialogue] up. 

[action] The old mug shot of COLE appears on the screen and RAILLY remotes 
[action] the volume up. 

[character] TV REPORTER (v.o.) 
[dialogue] Along with the kidnapping of the Baltimore 
[dialogue] woman, James Cole is now also wanted in 
[dialogue] connection with the brutal slaying of 
[dialogue] Rodney Wiggins, an ex-convict from... 

[action] RAILLY goes to the window, pushes aside the drape, and sees... 

[action] HER POV:  ACROSS THE STREET...A COP keeps watch. 

[character] RAILLY (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Do they really expect him to come here? 

[action] RAILLY returns to the kitchen area where MARILOU is getting the 
[action] tea things out. 

[character] TV REPORTER on air 
[dialogue] And in Fresno, California... 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] (glances sadly toward the TV) 
[dialogue] He's dead, isn't he -- that little boy? 

[character] WAYNE 
[dialogue] He's fine.  It was just a "prank" he 
[dialogue] and his friends pulled. 

[action] CLOSE ON RAILLY'S FACE... SHOCKED. 

[action] ANGLE ON THE TV SCREEN, showing footage of a sheepish nine year 
[action] old boy being led out of a barn by the police.  The cops look grim. 

[character] TV REPORTER (v.o.) 
[dialogue] Authorities have so far been noncommittal 
[dialogue] about whether they will try to file 
[dialogue] charges against the families of the 
[dialogue] children involved in the hoax. 

[action] RAILLY stares at the TV, stunned. 

[scene_heading] INT.  "HOSPITAL" ROOM - ETERNAL NIGHT 

[action] VOICES!  SINGING!  COLE blinks awake, looks around, confused, then 
[action] stares in disbelief.... 

[action] Crowded around COLE'S bed, the SCIENTISTS are concluding a 
[action] ragged, out of tune, rendition of "BLUEBERRY HILL." 

[character] SCIENTISTS 
[dialogue] ---found my thrills on Blueberry Hill... 

[action] Seeing he's awake, SCIENTISTS break off the song and applaud. 

[character] SCIENTISTS 
[dialogue] Well done, James!  Well done!  Nice 
[dialogue] going!  Congratulations!  Good for you! 

[character] BOTANIST 
[dialogue] During your "interview," while you 
[dialogue] were..."under the influence," you told 
[dialogue] us you liked music! 

[action] COLE, confused, looks around, sees he's in a one-bed windowless 
[action] room adorned with cheap reproductions of 19th and 20th century 
[action] landscapes. 

[action] The BOTANIST responds to COLE'S obvious disbelief with a 
[action] friendly smile and the others join in rapid fire, overlapping. 

[character] ZOOLOGIST 
[dialogue] This isn't the prison, James. 

[character] BOTANIST 
[dialogue] This is a hospital. 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 
[dialogue] But just until you recover your, 
[dialogue] uh,... equilibrium. 

[character] ENGINEER 
[dialogue] You're still a little... disoriented. 

[character] GEOLOGIST 
[dialogue] Stress!  Time travel! 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 
[dialogue] You stood up very well, considering... 

[character] GEOLOGIST 
[dialogue] Superior work!  Superior! 

[character] BOTANIST 
[dialogue] You connected the Army of the 12 
[dialogue] Monkeys to a world famous virologist 
[dialogue] and his son... 

[character] MICROBIOLOGIST 
[dialogue] Others will take over now... 

[character] ZOOLOGIST 
[dialogue] We'll be back on the surface in a 
[dialogue] matter of months.... 

[character] GEOLOGIST 
[dialogue] We'll retake the planet. 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 
[dialogue] We're very close!  Because of you! 

[character] ENGINEER 
[parenthetical] (unrolling a document) 
[dialogue] This is it, James...what you've been 
[dialogue] working for. 

[character] BOTANIST 
[dialogue] A full pardon! 

[character] MICROBIOLOGIST 
[dialogue] You'll be out of here in no time. 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 
[dialogue] Women will want to get to know you... 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I DON'T WANT YOUR "WOMEN," YOU 
[dialogue] BRAINLESS TWIT! I WANT TO BE WELL! 

[action] Unseen until now, two guards, TINY and SCARFACE, suddenly break 
[action] through the ring of SCIENTISTS, push COLE down, and tighten the 
[action] loose restraints, already in place, but unnoticed before. 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 

[] (sympathetically) 
[] Of course you want to be well, James. 
[] And you will be...soon. 

[action] COLE bursts into hysterical laughter. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] YOU DON'T EXIST, YOU SILLY BOZOS! 
[dialogue] YOU'RE NOT REAL! HA HA HA! PEOPLE DON'T 
[dialogue] TRAVEL IN TIME! YOU AREN'T HERE. 
[dialogue] MADE YOU UP!  YOU CAN'T TRICK ME! 
[dialogue] YOU'RE IN MY MIND! I'M INSANE AND 
[dialogue] YOU'RE MY INSANITY! 

[scene_heading] INT.  PSYCHIATRIST'S OFFICE - DAY 

[action] CLOSE ON KATHRYN RAILLY, insisting fiercely to someone, 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] He not only used the word "prank" -- he 
[dialogue] said the boy was hiding in a barn. 

[action] RAILLY's talking to her former boss, DR. OWEN FLETCHER, psychiatrist 
[action] sitting across from her in his office, tapping his pen. 

[character] DR. FLETCHER 
[dialogue] He kidnapped you, Kathryn.  You saw him 
[dialogue] murder someone.  You knew there was a real 
[dialogue] possibility he would kill you, too.  You 
[dialogue] were under tremendous emotional stress. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] For God sakes, Owen, listen to me -- he 
[dialogue] knew about the boy in Fresno and he says 
[dialogue] three billion people are going to die! 

[character] DR. FLETCHER 
[dialogue] Kathryn, you know he can't possibly 
[dialogue] know that.  You're a rational person. 
[dialogue] You're a trained psychiatrist.  You 
[dialogue] know the difference between what's real 
[dialogue] and what's not. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] And what we believe is what's accepted as 
[dialogue] "truth" now, isn't it, Owen?  Psychiatry -- 
[dialogue] it's the latest religion.  And we're the 
[dialogue] priests -- we decide what's right and what's 
[dialogue] wrong --we decide who's crazy and who isn't. ... 
[dialogue] I'm in trouble, Owen.  I'm losing my faith. 

[scene_heading] INT. "HOSPITAL" CELL - ETERNAL NIGHT 

[action] Alone in his "hospital" room, COLE struggles without success to 
[action] free himself from his restraints. 

[character] RASPY VOICE (o.s.) 

[dialogue] You sure fucked up, Bob! 

[action] Startled, COLE freezes, then ignores the RASPY VOICE and 
[action] continues his feverish struggle. 

[character] RASPY VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] But I can understand you don't want 
[dialogue] your mistakes pointed out to you. I can 
[dialogue] relate to that, old Bob. 

[action] COLE looks around in spite of himself.  Nothing to see but the 
[action] walls and the landscape paintings. 

[character] RASPY VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Hey, I know what you're thinking.  You're 
[dialogue] thinking I don't exist except in your 
[dialogue] head.  I can see that point of view.  But 
[dialogue] you could still talk to me, couldn't you? 
[dialogue] Carry on a decent conversation? 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (blurting) 
[dialogue] I saw you! In 1995!  In the real world! 
[dialogue] You were a bum!  You pulled out your teeth. 

[character] RASPY VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Why would I pull out my teeth, Bob? 
[dialogue] They don't like that.  That's a no-no. 
[dialogue] And when did you say you saw me? 
[dialogue] In...1872? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] FUCK YOU! 

[character] RASPY VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Yelling won't get you what you want.  You 
[dialogue] have to be smart to get what you want. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Oh, yeah?  What do I want? 

[character] RASPY VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] You don't know what you want?  Sure you 
[dialogue] do, Bob.  You know what you want. 

[action] COLE, agitated, rocks back and forth.  Then... 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Tell me.  Tell me what I want. 

[character] RASPY VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] To see the sky -- and the ocean -- to 
[dialogue] be topside -- breathe the air -- to be 
[dialogue] with her. ... Isn't that right?  Isn't 
[dialogue] that what you want? 

[action] Completely shaken, COLE hesitates for a long moment.  When he 

[action] speaks, it comes out of him like air...a whisper. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] More...than...anything. 

[scene_heading] INT.  RAILLY'S BEDROOM - MORNING
