[action] RAILLY'S in bed, asleep, having a very bad dream.  Suddenly, the 
[action] bedside phone RINGS.  Her eyes snap open.  A beat to orient 
[action] herself.  RING.  She reaches for the phone. 

[action] INTERCUT LIEUTENANT HALPERIN'S OFFICE/RAILLY'S BEDROOM 

[action] CLOSE ON HALPERIN, at his desk, talking into the phone. 

[character] LIEUTENANT HALPERIN 
[dialogue] Dr. Railly?  Jim Halperin, Philly P.D.. 
[dialogue] Sorry to call so early but... 

[action] CLOSE ON RAILLY, eager, concerned. into the phone, 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] You found him?  Is he all right? 

[action] CLOSE ON HALPEPIN, noting her reaction with raised eyebrows 
[action] giving an "I told you so" look to the BLACK PLAINCLOTHES cop 
[action] across his desk, then continuing into the phone, 

[character] LIEUTENANT HALPERIN 
[dialogue] Au contraire, Doctor.  No sign of your 
[dialogue] good friend, the kidnapper.  However, 
[dialogue] the plot thickens.  I have a ballistic 
[dialogue] report on my desk that says the bullet 
[dialogue] you claim you removed from Mr. Cole's 
[dialogue] thigh is an antique...and all indications 
[dialogue] are it was fired...sometime prior to 1920. 

[action] ANGLE ON RAILLY, reacting, stunned. 

[action] ANGLE ON HALPERIN, continuing soberly now, 

[character] LIEUTENANT RALPERIN 
[dialogue] So what I was thinking was, maybe if I 
[dialogue] sent a detective down there to talk 
[dialogue] with you, you could maybe revise or 
[dialogue] amplify on the circumstances.... 
[dialogue] Hello?  Hello?  Dr. Railly? 

[action] HALPERIN considers the dead phone, glances at the COP again. 

[scene_heading] INT.  RAILLY'S BEDROOM/STUDY 

[action] Her hand still on the receiver, RAILLY looks shocked.  Then, she 
[action] hurries into her study and starts frantically pulling neatly 
[action] arranged piles of papers and books from a bookcase until she 
[action] finds a copy of her book.  She leafs through it hurriedly, 
[action] locates the picture of the Puerto Rican KID (JOSE) in WWI. 

[action] Peering closely, she tries to see everything in the picture. 
[action] Then, she turns and reaches for a research folder of old 
[action] photographs and rummages through it until she finds...!!! 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] No! 

[action] It's an uncropped shot of JOSE being carried on the stretcher in 
[action] the trenches.  And there in the corner with no helmet, no gas 
[action] mask, and just a bit of bare shoulder showing...it's COLE!!! 

[scene_heading] INT.  SCIENTISTS' CHAMBER - ETERNAL NIGHT 

[action] Clean shaven, clear eyed, COLE sits before the frowning SCIENTISTS. 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 
[dialogue] The food, the sky, the certain, uh, 
[dialogue] sexual temptations -- you haven't 
[dialogue] become "addicted" have you, Cole?  To 
[dialogue] that "dying" world' 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] No, sir!  I just want to do my part. 
[dialogue] To get us back on top...in charge of 
[dialogue] the planet.  And I have the experience, 
[dialogue] I know who the people are... 

[character] BIOLOGIST 
[dialogue] He really is the most qualified... 

[character] GEOLOGIST 
[dialogue] But all that..."behavior"... 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 
[parenthetical] (to Cole, a little hurt) 
[dialogue] You said we weren't "real," Cole... 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Well, sir, I don't think the human mind 
[dialogue] was built to exist in two different... 
[dialogue] whatever you call it..."dimensions." 
[dialogue] It's stressful, you said it yourselves, 
[dialogue] it gets you confused.  You don't know 
[dialogue] what's real and what's not. 

[character] MICROBIOLOGIST 
[dialogue] But you know what's real now? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Yes, sir. 

[action] The SCIENTISTS start to confer openly among themselves 

[character] GEOLOGIST 
[dialogue] He'd have to bone up, catch up to our 
[dialogue] research, the latest clues... 

[character] ZOOLOGIST 
[dialogue] He's proved to be a quick study... 

[action] The ASTROPHYSICIST fixes COLE with a sharp, penetrating look. 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 
[dialogue] You can't trick us, you know.  It 
[dialogue] wouldn't work. 

[character] BIOLOGIST 
[dialogue] And why would you want to?  It'll be 
[dialogue] dangerously close to the end. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I understand.  There'd be no point. 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 
[dialogue] We're going to think about it, Cole. 
[dialogue] Among ourselves.  We'll get back to you. 

[scene_heading] INT.  DR. MASON'S OFFICE - DAY 

[action] Standing in front of a wall of glass in his office, overlooking a 
[action] hi-tech lab below where WORKERS in white "space suits" work 
[action] methodically, DR. MASON speaks angrily into a phone.  His male 
[action] ASSISTANT, whose features we don't see, stops writing a formula 
[action] on a blackboard and listens. 

[character] DR. MASON 
[dialogue] You have reason to believe that my son 
[dialogue] may be planning to do what?!!! 

[scene_heading] INT.  RAILLY'S APARTMENT/STUDY 

[action] RAILLY, trying to stay calm, is talking to Dr. Mason on the phone. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Please, I know it sounds insane but... 

[scene_heading] INT.  DR. MASON'S OFFICE 

[action] Dr. Mason on the phone. 

[character] DR. MASON 
[parenthetical] (into the phone) 
[dialogue] I'm afraid this doesn't seem very 
[dialogue] professional to me, in fact it's 
[dialogue] distressingly unprofessional for some- 
[dialogue] one who treated my son briefly (if 
[dialogue] indeed you actually are who you say you 
[dialogue] are) to take a sudden unsolicited interest 
[dialogue] in his mental health six years later, 
[dialogue] and to telephone a parent to express 
[dialogue] opinions that would be inappropriate... 
[dialogue] (breaks off, listens, then) 
[dialogue] I don't know anything about "Monkey 

[dialogue] armies", Doctor.  Nothing whatsoever. 
[dialogue] If my son ever was involved in... 
[parenthetical] (listens, then,) 
[dialogue] It would be doubly inappropriate to 
[dialogue] discuss matters of security with you, 
[dialogue] Dr. Railly, but if it will put you at 
[dialogue] ease, neither my son nor any other 
[dialogue] unauthorized person has access to any 
[dialogue] potentially dangerous organisms in this 
[dialogue] laboratory.  Thank you for your concern. 

[action] DR. MASON hangs up angrily and glares. 

[character] DR. MASON'S ASSISTANT (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Dr. "Kathryn" Railly???? 

[character] DR. MASON 
[dialogue] The psychiatrist who was kidnapped by 
[dialogue] that man who broke into my house.  She 
[dialogue] seems to have been suddenly struck by 
[dialogue] the most preposterous notion about Jeffrey. 

[character] DR. MASON'S ASSISTANT (o.s.) 
[dialogue] I attended a lecture once...Apocalyptic 
[dialogue] visions. 

[action] We see Dr. Mason's ASSISTANT now.  It's DR. PETERS, the red- 
[action] haired man who insisted to Dr. Railly you didn't have to be 
[action] insane to think the world was coming to an end. 

[character] DR. PETERS (cont.) 
[dialogue] Has she succumbed to her own 
[dialogue] theoretical..."disease"? 

[action] But DR. MASON is lost in thought, not listening. 

[character] DR. MASON 
[dialogue] Given the nature of our work, we can't ever 
[dialogue] be careful enough.  I think we should review 
[dialogue] our security procedures, perhaps upgrade them. 

[scene_heading] INT.  SCIENTISTS' CHAMBER - ETERNAL NIGHT 

[action] COLE is facing the BOTANIST who's using a pointer to indicate 
[action] various fading photos and newspaper clippings tacked on the wall. 

[character] BOTANIST 
[dialogue] Let's consider again our current 
[dialogue] information -- if the symptoms were 
[dialogue] first detected in Philadelphia on June 
[dialogue] 28, 1995, that makes us know that...? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] It was released in Philadelphia, 
[dialogue] probably on June 14, 1995. 

[character] BOTANIST 

[dialogue] And it appeared sequentially after that 
[dialogue] in...? 

[action] With a quick glance at the panel of SCIENTISTS staring at him 
[action] from behind the long table, COLE replies like a good pupil, 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] San Francisco, New Orleans, Rio de 
[dialogue] Janeiro, Rome, Kinshasa, Karachi, 
[dialogue] Bangkok, then Peking. 

[character] BOTANIST 
[dialogue] Meaning...??? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] That the virus was taken from Philadelphia 
[dialogue] to San Francisco, then to New Orleans, 
[dialogue] Rio de Janeiro, Rome, Kinshasa, Karachi, 
[dialogue] Bangkok, then Peking. 

[character] BOTANIST 
[dialogue] And your only goal is...??? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] To find out where the virus is so a 
[dialogue] qualified scientist can travel back into 
[dialogue] the past and study the original virus. 

[character] BOTANIST 
[dialogue] So that...??? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Uh, so that a vaccine can be developed 
[dialogue] that will, uh, allow mankind to reclaim 
[dialogue] the surface of the earth. 

[action] COLE glances nervously at the suspicious SCIENTISTS as the 
[action] BOTANIST switches on a slide projector and projects... 

[action] a magazine photo of wall graffiti:  "ATTENTION!!!  POLICE ARE 
[action] WATCHING!  IS THERE A VIRUS?  IS THIS THE SOURCE?  3 BILLION DIE?" 

[character] BOTANIST (o.s.) 
[dialogue] This is from a magazine printed in late 
[dialogue] September, 1995.  The writer speculated 
[dialogue] that this graffiti might be related to 
[dialogue] the epidemic that by that time had 
[dialogue] already killed thirty million people 
[dialogue] world-wide and was getting worse.  He 
[dialogue] says, certain people, unnamed, were 
[dialogue] questioned, but what came of that is not 
[dialogue] known.  But it is a clue you should pursue. 

[action] COLE stares at the picture. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  FAA STOREFRONT - DAY 

[action] LOUD BANGING!  The storefront window, completely covered with 
[action] posters, quivers violently.  Images of MONKEYS covered with 
[action] electrodes, BABY SEALS being viciously clubbed, DOGS jammed into 
[action] tiny cages quiver as somebody beats on the window.  It's RAILLY. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] IS ANYBODY IN THERE?  HELLO?  IS 
[dialogue] SOMEONE IN THERE?  IF YOU'RE IN THERE, 
[dialogue] I NEED TO TALK TO YOU. 

[scene_heading] INT.  FAA STORE 

[action] JEFFREY, BEN, TEDDY, and two of JEFFREY'S youthful cohorts, SANDY 
[action] and KWESKIN, wait motionless beside a heap of cardboard cartons 
[action] as FALE peeks out the front window through a slit between posters. 

[character] FALE 
[dialogue] It's the kidnap woman -- the one who 
[dialogue] was with the guy who tied us up. 

[character] BEN 
[dialogue] What's she doing? 

[character] FALE 
[dialogue] She's drawing attention to us, that's 
[dialogue] what she's doing. ... I don't know what 
[dialogue] you're up to this time, Mason, but 
[dialogue] you're gonna get us in deep shit! 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Whine, whine, whine.  What about walkie 
[dialogue] talkies?  We used to have walkie talkies. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  FAA STOREFRONT 

[action] From littered doorways, DERELICTS sneak peeks at RAILLY as she, 
[action] seemingly mad, shakes the doorknob, then hammers on the door. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] I SAW YOU!  I SAW SOMEONE MOVING.  I 
[dialogue] KNOW YOU'RE IN THERE! 

[character] RASPY VOICE (o.s) 
[dialogue] Secret experiments! 

[action] RAILLY whirls, sees LOUIE, the raspy-voiced toothless derelict. 

[character] LOUIE 
[dialogue] That's what they do -- secret weird stuff! 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] You!  I know you! 

[action] But LOUIE is studying the pictures of the tortured animals now. 

[character] LOUIE 
[dialogue] Not just on them.  Do 'em on people, 

[dialogue] too -- down at the shelters.  Feed 'em 
[dialogue] chemicals 'n take pictures of 'em. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Have you seen James Cole?  The man...? 

[character] LOUIE 
[dialogue] They're watchin' you.  Takin' pictures. 

[action] RAILLY follows his look. 

[action] ANGLE ON AN OLD CHEVY, parked across the street, the PLAINCLOTHES 
[action] COP slouched at the wheel, pretending to read a newspaper. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] The police.  I know.  Listen, I need to 
[dialogue] talk to James, but he has to be careful 
[dialogue] how he contacts me.  He mustn't get 
[dialogue] caught.  Do you understand me? 

[character] LOUIS 
[dialogue] Uh, yeah, sure.  Uh...who's James? 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] He was with me, he spoke to you. 
[dialogue] Several weeks ago.  He said you were 
[dialogue] from the future...watching him. 

[action] LOUIS gives her a look that says, "I'm outta here!" 

[action] But just then, RAILLY spots two TEZNAGE PUNKS surreptitiously 
[action] "tagging" their way along the street with cans of spray paint. 

[action] RAILLY stares at the PUNKS. 

[scene_heading] INT.  FAA STORE 

[action] FALE watches JEFFREY go over a check list while KWESKIN, SANDY, 
[action] and TEDDY organize materials, and BEN peeks out the small opening 
[action] between posters at the front window. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] You get the bolt cutters? 

[character] KWESKIN 
[dialogue] One dozen.  They're in the van. 

[character] FALE 
[dialogue] One dozen bolt cutters!  Whadda you 
[dialogue] gonna do with one dozen bolt cutters? 

[character] JEFFREY 
[parenthetical] (grins) 
[dialogue] You really want to know? 

[character] FALE 
[dialogue] No!  Absolutely not. Don't tell me anything. 

[character] BEN 
[dialogue] Hey!  Do you know what she's doing? 

[action] Everybody freezes, looking toward 3EN, who's peeking outside. 

[action] Then, except for JEFFREY, they all crowd around BEN to get a look. 

[action] POV THROUGH SLIT:  a glimpse of RAILLY, spray painting the front 
[action] of the store! 

[character] TEDDY 
[dialogue] What's it say? 

[character] BEN 
[dialogue] I can't see it. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[parenthetical] (erupting) 
[dialogue] WHY DON'T WE FORGET MY GODDAMN 
[dialogue] PSYCHIATRIST AND DEAL WITH THE TASK AT 
[dialogue] HAND.  THIS IS IMPORTANT. 

[character] FALE 
[parenthetical] (spinning around) 
[dialogue] Your psychiatrist?  Did you just say, 
[dialogue] "your psychiatrist"? 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Ex-psychiatrist!  Now, what about 
[dialogue] flashlights?  How many flashlights...? 

[character] FALE 
[dialogue] That woman is...was...your... 
[dialogue] psychiatrist?  And now she's spray- 
[dialogue] painting our building? 

[scene_heading] EXT.  FAA STOREFRONT/SECOND AVENUE 

[action] ANGLE ON SLACK PLAINCLOTHES COP, across the street in the CHEVY, 
[action] amazed, watching RAILLY spray painting.  He shakes his head wearily. 

[action] ANGLZ ON STREET TYPES, inching closer, watching RAILLY with 
[action] amazement, too.  They include... 

[action] an IRISH DRUNK, white haired, red-faced, bloated... 

[action] a NATIVE AMERICAN with tormented eyes and a mangled ear... 

[action] an AFRICAN AMERICAN with one eye... 

[action] the TEENAGED PUNKS... 

[action] a WHITE MAN, shabbily dressed, joining the knot of ONLOOKERS, 
[action] reacting at the sight of RAILLY.  It's COLE!  He pushes toward her. 

[character] COLE 

[dialogue] Kathryn! 

[action] RAILLY stops spraying, whips around at the sound of his VOICE. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] James! 

[action] With a quick glance toward the PLAINCLOTHES COP, RAILLY takes 
[action] urgent charge of the situation. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] James!  That's a policeman.  Pretend 
[dialogue] you don't know me.  If he sees you... 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (turning, looking) 
[dialogue] No, I want to turn myself in.  Where is he? 
[dialogue] (placing his hands on his head) 
[dialogue] Don't worry -- it's all okay now.  I'm 
[dialogue] not crazy any more!  I mean, I am 
[dialogue] crazy, mentally divergent, actually, 
[dialogue] but I know it now and I want you to 
[dialogue] help me.  I want to get well... 

[action] ANGLE on RAILLY, desperately pulling COLE'S hands off his head as 
[action] she tries to block the COP'S view of COLE. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] James -- put your hands down and listen 
[dialogue] to me.  Things have changed! 

[action] ANGLE ON THE PLAINCLOTHES COP, checking the mug shot of COLE on 
[action] his clipboard, then reaching for his radio mike. 

[action] ANGLE ON RAILLY, reacting to the COP speaking into his mike:  she 
[action] tosses the spray paint can aside, grabs COLE and tries to pull 
[action] him along...but COLE isn't moving.  He's staring at the front of 
[action] the FAA Store with disbelief! 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] James, come on!  We have to get out of here! 

[action] COLE looks from the wall to the can rolling on the sidewalk, 
[action] then back to the wall where RAILLY has sprayed the huge words: 

[centered] ATTENTION!!!  POLICE ARE WATCHING! 
[centered] IS THERE A VIRUS?  IS THIS THE 
[centered] SOURCE?  THREE BILLION DIE? 

[action] It's the graffiti COLE saw in the future, in the picture! 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I've seen that...before. 

[action] But RAILLY'S total attention is on their dilemma. 

[character] RAILLY 

[dialogue] James, trust me.  We're in terrible 
[dialogue] trouble.  We have to run. 

[action] Very confused, COLE lets her drag him along the sidewalk, past 
[action] ONLOOKERS.  She looks crazier than he does. 

[action] ANGLE ON THE CHEVY, making a sudden, urgent u-turn, almost 
[action] colliding with a passing car.  BRAKES SQUEAL and a HORN BLARES. 

[scene_heading] INT.  FAA STORE - DAY 

[action] ANGLE ON BEN, peeking out, reacting to the drama. 

[character] BEN 
[dialogue] Wow, a guy in a Chevy is chasing her 
[dialogue] and some other guy I can't see. 

[character] FALE 
[dialogue] Hey, no problem, it's probably just 
[dialogue] another kidnapping featuring Jeffrey's 
[dialogue] shrink, pardon me, make that ex-shrink. 
[dialogue] (indicating Jeffrey to the others) 
[dialogue] This is your leader, a certifiable lunatic 
[dialogue] who told his former psychiatrist all his 
[dialogue] plans for God knows what whacko irresponsible 
[dialogue] schemes, and now who knows what she's 
[dialogue] painted out there on our wall? 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] WHO CARES WHAT PSYCHIATRISTS WRITE ON 
[dialogue] WALLS? 
[parenthetical] (moves to Fale, jabs him with a finger) 
[dialogue] You think I told her about the Army of 
[dialogue] the 12 Monkeys?  Impossible!  Know why, 
[dialogue] you pathetically ineffectual and 
[dialogue] pusillanimous "pretend-friend-to- 
[dialogue] animals"?!  I'll tell you why:  because 
[dialogue] when I had anything to do with her six 
[dialogue] years ago, there was no such thing -- I 
[dialogue] hadn't even thought of it yet! 

[character] FALE 
[parenthetical] (triumphant) 
[dialogue] Then how come she knows what's going on? 

[action] JEFFREY abruptly switches from rage to good humor, adopting a 
[action] supercilious smile and a patronizing tone. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Here's my theory on that.  While I was 
[dialogue] institutionalized, my brain was studied 
[dialogue] exhaustively in the guise of mental health. 
[dialogue] I was interrogated, x-rayed, studied 
[dialogue] thoroughly.  Then, everything about me 
[dialogue] was entered into a computer where they 
[dialogue] created a model of my mind. 

[action] They all stare, mesmerized, at the strutting JEFFREY.  Is he 
[action] serious?  Is he crazy?  Doesn't matter -- he's charismatic. 

[character] JEFFREY (cont.) 
[dialogue] Then, using the computer model, they 
[dialogue] generated every thought I could possibly 
[dialogue] have in the next, say ten years, which 
[dialogue] they then filtered through a probability 
[dialogue] matrix to determine everything I was 
[dialogue] going to do in that period.  So you 
[dialogue] see, she knew I was going to lead the 
[dialogue] Army of the Twelve Monkeys into the 
[dialogue] pages of history before it ever even 
[dialogue] occurred to me.  She knows everything 
[dialogue] I'm ever going to do before I know it 
[dialogue] myself.  How about that? 

[action] JEFFREY smiles smugly into FALE'S flabbergasted face. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Now I have to get going -- do my part. 
[dialogue] You guys check all this stuff out and 
[dialogue] load up the van.  Make sure you have 
[dialogue] everything.  I'm outta here. 

[action] JEFFREY exits.  The others stare at the door. 

[character] FALE 
[dialogue] He's seriously crazy -- you know that. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  SKID ROW ALLEY - DAY 

[action] An overflowing dumpster squats near the mouth of an alley. 

[action] The unmarked CHEVY crawls slowly past the alley, the PLAINCLOTHES 
[action] COP'S eyes searching everywhere. 

[action] Trash stirs in the dumpster and RAILLY'S eyes peer up out of the 
[action] torn cardboard boxes, rotting food, and styrofoam litter. 

[action] HER POV:  the POLICE CAR passes from view. 

[action] ANGLE ON RAILLY, emerging from the refuse, hissing, 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] James!  Come on. 

[action] A confused COLE emerges from the opposite end of the dumpster, 
[action] bits of lettuce in his hair. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I don't understand what we're doing. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] (climbing out of the dumpster) 
[dialogue] We're avoiding the police until I 
[dialogue] can....talk to you. 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (climbing out after her) 
[dialogue] You mean, treat me?  Cure me?  Kathryn, 
[dialogue] those words on the wall -- I've seen 
[dialogue] them before...  I...I...dreamed them. 

[action] But she's not listening.  She's peeking out the alley entrance. 

[action] RAILLY'S POV:  across the street is a run-down skid row hotel, 
[action] THE GLOBE, ROOMS WEEKLY, DAILY. 

[scene_heading] INT.  GLOBE HOTEL/LOBBY - MINUTES LATER 

[action] The DESK CLERK, an old alkie who hates trouble but finds it often, 
[action] stares across the counter suspiciously at RAILLY and COLE. 

[character] DESK CLERK 
[dialogue] Twenty five bucks an hour. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] An hour?! 

[character] DESK CLERK 
[dialogue] You want quarter hours, go someplace else. 

[character] RAILLY 
[parenthetical] (catches on, pulls out her last bills) 
[dialogue] Here's ninety eight.  For the night.  Deal? 

[action] The DESK CLERK squints warily at this turn of events.  Then, he 
[action] turns, gets a key, turns back and hands it to her. 

[character] DESK CLERK 
[dialogue] Forty four.  Fourth floor, turn right. 
[dialogue] Elevator's busted. 

[action] RAILLY turns, COLE follows, and they walk quickly to the stairs 
[action] passing the stares of gloomy RESIDENTS sitting on torn sofas 
[action] chairs in front of an old TV with hideous color. 

[action] ANGLE ON THE DESK CLERK, watching RAILLY and COLE climb 
[action] stairs.  As they disappear from view, he picks up the phone, 
[action] punches a number, speaks into the phone. 

[character] DESK CLERK 
[dialogue] Tommy?  This is Charlie at the Globe. 
[dialogue] You know if Wallace has a new girl? 
[dialogue] Sort of a rookie type?  Blonde? 

[scene_heading] INT.  GLOBE HOTEL ROOM 44 - MINUTES LATER (DAY) 

[action] COLE sits on the lumpy bed in the dingy room, watching RAILLY 
[action] pace back and forth like a mad woman. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Okay...you were standing there looking 

[dialogue] at the moon...you were eating grass... 
[dialogue] then what? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I thought I was in...prison again. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Just like that?  You were in prison? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] No, not really.  It's...it's in my 
[dialogue] mind.  Like you said. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] You disappeared!  One minute you were 
[dialogue] there, the next minute you were gone. 
[dialogue] Did you run through the woods? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I don't know -- I don't remember. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] The boy in the well.  How did you know 
[dialogue] that was just a hoax? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] It was?  I didn't...know. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] James, you said he was hiding in the barn... 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I think I saw a TV show like that when 
[dialogue] I was a kid.  Where a boy... 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] IT WASN'T A TV SHOW!  IT WAS REAL! 

[action] COLE looks at her.  She's really upset. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Well, maybe that kid saw the same TV 
[dialogue] show and copied it.  Listen, you were 
[dialogue] right, it's all in my head.  I'm mentally 
[dialogue] ill, I imagine all that stuff.  I know 
[dialogue] they're not real, I can trick them, make 
[dialogue] them do what I want.  I just worked on 
[dialogue] them in my head and I got back here.  I 
[dialogue] can get better.  I can stay here. 

[action] RAILLY pulls a photo from her purse, shows it to COLE. 

[action] It's the uncropped picture from her book, the photo of JOSE in 
[action] WWI with a fuzzy image of COLE on the edge of the frame, 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] What does this mean to you? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] ...I had a dream about...something 
[dialogue] like that. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] You had a bullet from World War One in 
[dialogue] your leg, James!  How did it get there? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] You said I had delusions -- that I 
[dialogue] created a world -- you said you could 
[dialogue] explain everything... 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Well, I can't. ... I mean...I'm trying 
[dialogue] to.  I can't believe that everything we 
[dialogue] do or say has already happened, that we 
[dialogue] can't change what's going to happen, 
[dialogue] that I'm one of the three billion 
[dialogue] people who are going to die...soon. 

[action] COLE stands, moves close to her. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I want to be here.  In this time.  With 
[dialogue] you.  I want to become...become a whole 
[dialogue] person.  I want this to be the present. 
[dialogue] I want the future to be unknown. 

[character] RAILLY 
[parenthetical] (sudden hopeful idea!) 
[dialogue] James...do you remember...six years 
[dialogue] ago...you had a phone number!  You 
[dialogue] tried to call and... 

[action] WHAM!  The door flies open, kicked violently, the flimsy lock not 
[action] holding.  A menacing figure stands in the doorway.  WALLACE.  A 
[action] wiry biker-type with jail house tattoos and mean eyes. 

[action] COLE and RAILLY are too stunned to say anything as WALLACE looks 
[action] them over coldly, insolently, then advances on RAILLY. 

[character] WALLACE 
[dialogue] This is my territory, bitch! 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] (confused, turns to Railly) 
[dialogue] Is this real -- or is this one of my 
[dialogue] delusions? 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] This is definitely real: 
[parenthetical] (to Wallace) 
[dialogue] Excuse me, I think we have a little 
[dialogue] misunderstanding here... 

[action] WALLACE smashes RAILLY in the face.  She flies back against the 
[action] wall and onto the floor as WALLACE spins around to COLE who is 
[action] stepping toward him, but WALLACE is now holding a knife 

[character] WALLACE 
[dialogue] What're you -- some kind of tough guy? 
[dialogue] You wanna be a hero?  You gonna try and 
[dialogue] mess with me?  Come on... 

[action] A beat.  COLE puts his hands up placatingly as he backs around 
[action] WALLACE and moves to RAILLY, whose eye is already swelling. 

[character] WALLACE 
[dialogue] Now that's a smart boy.  But you, 
[dialogue] honey, you think you can go 'round 
[dialogue] me and peddle your fancy ass in this 
[dialogue] part of town, you bet your life we got 
[dialogue] what I would call a major goddamn 
[dialogue] misunderstandin'. 

[action] RAILLY reaches a hand up to COLE for assistance, but, he grabs 
[action] her purse instead, swings it around, SMASHES WALLACE in the face 
[action] with it, then grabs the pimp's arm and SNAPS it like it was a 
[action] twig!  The knife clatters to the floor as WALLACE yelps in pain 
[action] and COLE slams him to the floor, straddles his chest, retrieves 
[action] the near-by knife, and presses it sharply against WALLACE'S neck. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] JAMES -- DON'T! 

[action] COLE hesitates. 

[character] WALLACE 
[parenthetical] (eyes bulging) 
[dialogue] You...heard...her.  Don't do it, man. 

[character] RAILLY 
[parenthetical] (gets up, looks around) 
[dialogue] Put him in the closet, ... But get his 
[dialogue] money first. 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (amazed) 
[dialogue] You want me to rob him? 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] I...I...We need cash, James. 

[action] A shadow.  RAILLY turns toward the door and glimpses a FACE 
[action] disappearing, then hears SHOUTS from the hallway... 

[character] SHOUTS (o.s.) 
[dialogue] They're killing him!  Call the cops. 

[action] Being very careful not to move his neck, WALLACE reaches into his 
[action] pocket and produces a thick roll of bills, which RAILLY grabs. 

[character] WALLACE 
[dialogue] You two are crazy.  I got friends.  You 
[dialogue] put me in a closet, they're gonna be 
[dialogue] really pissed. 

[action] COLE moves off Wallace and, keeping the knife close, yanks him to 
[action] his teeth while RAILLY hurries to the window and looks out. 

[action] HER POV:  A fire escape leads down into an alley. 

[action] RAILLY turns just in time to see COLE shove WALLACE into the 
[action] bathroom, follow him in, slam the door behind them, and LOCK it. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] James, no! 

[scene_heading] INT.  BATHROOM 

[character] RAILLY'S VOICE (from the other room) 
[parenthetical] (rattling the door knob) 
[dialogue] James, what are you doing? 

[action] WALLACE cowers back against the shower stall. 

[character] WALLACE 
[dialogue] I have friends, man -- if you cut me... 

[action] WALLACE breaks off, bug-eyed, reacting to something we don't see! 

[character] WALLACE 
[dialogue] What...the...fuck..are you doing??? 

[scene_heading] INT.  GLOBE MOTEL ROOM 44 

[action] RAILLY is pounding on the bathroom door now as, suddenly, it 
[action] opens and COLE steps out, the knife in his right hand, dripping 
[action] with blood. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Oh, my God, James.  Did you kill him? 

[action] COLE shakes his head "no" as blood oozes from his mouth. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Just...just in case.  In case I'm not 
[dialogue] crazy... 
[dialogue] (holds up two bloody molars) 
[dialogue] That's how they find us.  By our teeth. 
[parenthetical] (a beat, eye contact) 
[dialogue] I don't want them to find me.  Ever.  I 
[dialogue] don't want to go back. 

[action] RAILLY's astonishment turns to emotion as it dawns on her the 
[action] choice he's made.  Given up the future (if it exists, and it's 
[action] looking like it does)!  Risking his life to be with her!  For 
[action] this brief time!  She's overwhelmed, lips trembling, tears 
[action] welling in her eyes.  But just then, the SOUNDS of SHOUTS and 

[action] feet THUNDERING up the stairs snap her back to reality. 

[scene_heading] INT.  STAIRWELL/GLOBE HOTEL 

[action] COPS' boots THUNDER up the stairwell. 

[scene_heading] INT.  ROOM 44/GLOBE HOTEL 

[action] In between nervous glances toward the door, RAILLY supervises as 
[action] COLE obediently blocks the bathroom door with the bureau. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Push it tight! 

[character] WALLACE'S VOICE (from the bathroom) 
[dialogue] NO PROBLEM!  I'LL JUST STAY IN HERE! 
[dialogue] DON'T WORRY ABOUT ME. 

[scene_heading] INT.  4TH FLOOR HALLWAY/GLOBE HOTEL 

[action] POLICE OFFICERS, led by the PLAINCLOTHES COP, make a cautious 
[action] entrance onto the 4th floor, guns drawn. 

[action] Seedy HOTEL RESIDENTS point to Room 44, then cower back into 
[action] their doorways. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  GLOBE HOTEL FIRE ESCAPE/ALLEY - DAY 

[action] COLE and RAILLY clatter down the fire-escape, COLE in the lead. 
[action] They come to the end of the metal stairway.  It's a long distance 
[action] to the ground.  COLE jumps down, turns, reaches up to her. 

[action] She lets herself down to him.  Their eyes meet.  He holds her in 
[action] his arms for a moment.  Then, reluctantly, he puts her down. 
[action] They start running down the alley. 

[scene_heading] INT.  GLOBE HOTEL FOURTH FLOOR - DAY 

[action] The PLAINCLOTHES COP has his pistol out, his back to the wall 
[action] alongside the open doorway to Room 44.  The UNIFORMED OFFICERS 
[action] are backing him up, weapons drawn. 

[character] PLAINCLOTHES COP 
[dialogue] POLICE!  THROW YOUR WEAPONS OUT AND 
[dialogue] COME OUTTA THERE! 

[action] No response. 

[scene_heading] INT.  GLOBE HOTEL ROOM 44 

[action] The PLAINCLOTHES COP charges into the room in a crouch, pistol 
[action] extended in both hands.  He pans the gun around the empty room. 

[action] HIS POV:  the open window...the blood on the floor. 

[character] WALLACE'S VOICE (from the bathroom) 
[dialogue] HEY!  ZAT THE POLICE!  I'M AN INNOCENT 

[dialogue] VICTIM IN HERE!  I WAS ATTACKED BY A 
[dialogue] COKED-UP WHORE AND A CRAZY DENTIST! 

[scene_heading] EXT.  BUS STOP/DOWNTOWN - LATE AFTERNOON 

[action] A city bus disgorges a stream of PASSENGERS at a stop in the 
[action] toney downtown shopping district.  Among them, RAILLY looks 
[action] furtively left and right, COLE on her heels.  She's hidden her 
[action] bruised eye behind sun glasses; he's holding a bloody 
[action] handkerchief to his mouth. 

[action] As he steps to the sidewalk, COLE is overwhelmed by the bustling 
[action] city, the tall downtown buildings.  His eyes go up. 

[action] COLE'S POV:  a building, towering toward the sky.  A building 
[action] with a ledge.  The same ledge the lion prowled in the future!!! 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, shaken, as RAILLY ushers him into the recessed 
[action] entrance to a store. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Wait here.  I'm going to try that phone 
[dialogue] number.  Let's hope it's nothing! 

[action] Dazed by his experience and the flow of SHOPPERS, COLE watches 
[action] her hurry to a pay phone twenty yards away, his view of her made 
[action] intermittent by PEDESTRIANS streaming past him, their FACES 
[action] looming frighteningly close. 

[action] A BUSINESSMAN jostles COLE, forcing him back against the display 
[action] window.  Turning, he faces the angry jaws of a BEAR only inches away 

[action] Recovering from a jolt of terror, COLE realizes the BEAR is a life-
[action] size 
[action] toy in the display window.  Relieved, he looks back at RAILLY. 

[action] COLE'S POV:  RAILLY, well out of earshot, speaking earnestly into 
[action] the phone. 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, startled, as a BUSINESSMAN, mistaking him for a 
[action] panhandler, shoves a dollar into his hand.  Confused, COLE stares 
[action] at the dollar, then turns to say something to the retreating 
[action] BUSINESSMAN, but just then he sees RAILLY rushing toward him, 
[action] eyes sparkling with happiness, LAUGHING, ebullient. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] James!  James!  It's okay.  We're 
[dialogue] insane!  We're crazy! 

[action] COLE doesn't know how to respond, but a PASSERBY gives them a look. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] It's a Carpet Cleaning Company... 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] A Carpet Cleaning Company? 

[character] RAILLY 
[parenthetical] (laughing) 
[dialogue] No superiors!  No scientists.  No 
[dialogue] people from the future.  It's just a 
[dialogue] Carpet Cleaning Company.  They have 
[dialogue] voice mail -- you leave a message 
[dialogue] telling them when you want your carpet 
[dialogue] cleaned. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] You... you left them a message? 

[character] RAILLY 
[parenthetical] (impishly) 
[dialogue] I couldn't resist.  I was so relieved. 
[dialogue] Wait'll they hear this nutty woman 
[dialogue] telling them...they better watch out 
[dialogue] for the Army of the Twelve Monkeys... 

[action] Looking at her laughing face, COLE is struck with horror as he 
[action] realizes the truth!  He starts to recite... 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] "The Army of the Twelve Monkeys -- 
[dialogue] they're the ones who are going to do 
[dialogue] it.  I can't do anything more.  The 
[dialogue] police are watching me." 

[action] Now she's stunned.  She glances back and sees the phone booth 
[action] twenty yards away. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] You... you couldn't have heard me. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] They got your message, Kathryn.  They 
[dialogue] played it for me.  It was a bad 
[dialogue] recording...distorted.  I didn't 
[dialogue] recognize your voice. 

[action] RAILLY'S eyes fill with horror as she grasps the meaning. 

[action] ANGLE ON A UNIFORMED COP, staring out the window of a POLICE 
[action] CRUISER as it inches along in the bumper to bumper traffic. 
[action] Noticing something, he reaches for his radio. 

[action] ANGLE ON RAILLY, spotting the CRUISER, grabbing COLE, pulling him 
[action] into the CROWD. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Come on. 

[scene_heading] INT.  DEPARTMENT STORE/MEN'S DEPARTMENT - 6:00 PM 

[action] RAILLY, whose sun glasses don't really hide her bruised eye, adds 
[action] a man's Hawaiian shirt to the pile of other men's things heaped 
[action] on a counter in front of a very suspicious CLERK. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] And this. 
[parenthetical] (turning) 
[dialogue] Anything else? 

[action] But COLE'S not here.  He's a short distance away...staring. 

[action] COLE'S POV:  aisle after aisle of eager shoppers and a bounty of 
[action] brand new consumer goods. 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, remembering another department store, dark and 
[action] full of moldering merchandise. 

[action] ANGLE ON RAILLY, turning again to the CLERK. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] I guess that's it. 

[character] CLERK 
[dialogue] Shall I put this on your account, Ma'am? 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] (producing Wallace's roll of bills) 
[dialogue] No -- I'll pay cash. 

[action] The CLERK stares at the huge roll of bills! 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] What floor are the wigs on, please? 

[scene_heading] EXT.  PEST CONTROL VAN - NIGHT 

[action] Surrounded by stripped and abandoned vehicles, the VAN, with a 
[action] PEST CONTROL logo on its side, is parked on a trash-littered 
[action] street beside the massive pillars of a towering freeway. 

[scene_heading] INT.  PARKED PEST CONTROL VAN 

[action] The VAN is packed with SIX ACTIVISTS, SANDY and KWESKIN among 
[action] them, all wearing black.  Some of them have climbing gear, tool 
[action] belts, all sorts of paraphernalia.  KWESKIN is telling his story. 

[character] KWESKIN 
[dialogue] So then he goes into this incredible riff 
[dialogue] about how his shrink, like, replicated 
[dialogue] his brain while he was in the nut house. 
[dialogue] Turned it into a computer. 

[character] WELLER 
[dialogue] And Fale believed it? 

[character] KWESKIN 
[dialogue] Oh, you know Fale!  He's like, "If you 
[dialogue] guys get nailed -- and I'm sure you will -- 
[dialogue] I never saw you before in nay life!" 

[action] LAUGHTER from all of them.  Then, there's a sharp, rhythmic 
[action] series of RAPS on the side door, a signal. 

[action] POPE quickly slides the door open.  It's JEFFREY...grinning. 

[action] Three other activists, GOINES, ICHIOKA, and BRUHNS, stagger out of 
[action] the darkness behind JEFFREY, lugging a huge, squirming GARBAGE BAG. 

[action] The van occupants react with murmurs of "Awwwwwright" and "Far 
[action] out", then they help maneuver the writhing bag into the van. 

[action] Then, JEFFREY and the other three scramble in, too. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Let's do it! 

[scene_heading] EXT.  VAN/FREEWAY 

[action] The PEST CONTROL VAN lumbers up a ramp and onto the freeway. 

[scene_heading] INT.  PEST CONTROL VAN/MOVING 

[action] The GARBAGE BAG squirms and grunts as JEFFREY holds a map under a 
[action] flashlight and goes over "the plan" with the other ACTIVISTS. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Okay, that's Stage One.  In Stage Two, 
[dialogue] Monkey Four is over here... 

[action] A loud GROAN from the bag distracts the others. 

[character] GOINES 
[dialogue] What's the harm in opening the bag? 
[dialogue] His eyes are taped. 

[character] SANDY 
[dialogue] Yeah, it's cruel leaving him like that. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Ah, but cruelty is his specialty. 

[character] ICHIOKA 
[dialogue] So why should we be like him? 

[action] Shrugging cheerfully, JEFFREY tears open the garbage bag revealing 
[action] DR. MASON, trussed up, duct tape covering his eyes and mouth. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] Want the full effect? 

[action] Grinning wickedly, JEFFREY rips the tape from his father's mouth. 

[character] DR. MASON 
[dialogue] Jeffrey?  I know it's you, Jeffrey.  I 
[dialogue] recognize your voice. 

[action] JEFFREY puts his finger to his lips, silencing everyone. 

[character] DR. MASON 
[dialogue] JEFFREY???  ... Very well.  You're out 
[dialogue] of your mind, Jeffrey.  I know all 
[dialogue] about your insane plan.  That woman -- 
[dialogue] your psychiatrist -- she told me. 

[action] JEFFREY raises his eyebrows.  This he hadn't expected. 

[character] DR. MASON 
[dialogue] I didn't believe her -- it seemed too crazy 
[dialogue] even for you.  But, just in case, I took steps 
[dialogue] to make sure you couldn't do it.  I took myself 
[dialogue] out of the loop!  I don't have the code any 
[dialogue] more.  I don't have access to the virus.  So, 
[dialogue] go ahead -- torture me, but you can't extract 
[dialogue] anything of use to yourself. 

[action] The ACTIVISTS are all exchanging puzzled looks. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] What...virus? 

[character] DR. MASON 
[dialogue] (spins his head toward Jeffrey) 
[dialogue] She knew about it, Jeffrey.  She knew 
[dialogue] you were going to try this. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] What virus are we talking about, Dad? 

[character] DR. MASON 
[dialogue] You're insane, Jeffrey. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] You "develop" viruses and you're calling 
[dialogue] me insane?  Typical.  What does this 
[dialogue] virus attack?  Don't tell me, you sick 
[dialogue] fuck, it doesn't matter. 
[parenthetical] (to the others) 
[dialogue] Have I ever "developed" a virus?  Do I 
[dialogue] put helpless animals in cages and measure 
[dialogue] their reactions to electrical stimuli? 
[dialogue] Do I inject radioactive substances into 
[dialogue] living creatures and examine their 
[dialogue] bowel movements?  Wow!  And I'm crazy! 

[character] DR. MASON 
[dialogue] Please tell me, Jeffrey, what exactly 
[dialogue] are you going to do?  I don't have to 
[dialogue] tell you I'm afraid. 

[character] JEFFREY 
[dialogue] THIS IS A FUCKING EXPERIMENT!  YOU'RE 
[dialogue] OUR HELPLESS LITTLE TEST ANIMAL, DADDY. 
[dialogue] GOT THAT?  NOW -- WHAT FUCKING VIRUS 
[dialogue] HAVE YOU COME UP WITH, YOU DEMENTED 

[dialogue] FUCKING MANIAC? 

[scene_heading] INT.  MOVIE SCREEN/THEATER - NIGHT 

[action] Spooky BERNARD HERRMAN MUSIC, giant redwoods looming skyward. 
[action] It's DAYTIME in Muir Woods.  SCOTTY (JAMES STEWART) AND MADELINE 
[dialogue] (KIM NOVAK) walk toward a display of a cross cut section of a 
[action] redwood tree.  We're watching Hitchcock's VERTIGO. 

[character] SCOTTY (up on the screen) 
[dialogue] Here's a cross section of one of the 
[dialogue] old trees that's been cut down. 

[action] They look at the lines of the tree marked with cards that say, 
[action] "BIRTH OF CHRIST", "DISCOVERY OF AMERICA", "MAGNA CARTA SIGNED", 
[action] "1066 - BATTLE OF HASTINGS", and "1930 TREE CUT DOWN". 

[action] ANGLE ON THE THEATER AUDIENCE, empty seats dimly visible in the 
[action] flickering light, a few shadowy MOVIEGOERS scattered here and there. 

[action] ANGLE ON THE SCREEN, MADELINE pointing, saying with profound 
[action] melancholy. 

[character] MADELINE (up on the screen) 
[dialogue] Somewhere in here I was born.  And here 
[dialogue] -- I die.  There's only a moment for 
[dialogue] you.  You don't notice. 

[action] ANGLE ON THE AUDIENCE, a shadowy COUPLE near the back of the 
[action] theater.  WE CAN'T REALLY SEE THEM, but we recognize their VOICES 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Here, let me help you. 

[action] The theater is briefly illuminated by a very bright scene on the 
[action] screen, revealing enough of COLE and RAILLY for us to see she's 
[action] doing something to his upper lip while he tries to watch the movie. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I think I've seen this movie before. 
[dialogue] When I was a kid.  It was on TV. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] (fussing with his upper lip) 
[dialogue] Shh -- don't talk.  Hold still. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] (moving his head to see the film) 
[dialogue] I have seen it, but I don't remember 
[dialogue] this part.  Funny, it's like what's 
[dialogue] happening to us, like the past.  The 
[dialogue] movie never changes -- it can't change 
[dialogue] -- but everytime you see it, it seems 
[dialogue] to be different because you're different 
[dialogue] -- you notice different things. 

[character] RAILLY 

[dialogue] If we can't change anything...because 
[dialogue] it's already happened, then we ought to 
[dialogue] at least smell the flowers. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Flowers!  What flowers? 

[action] From the darkness, a MOVIE PATRON makes a SSSSHHHHH shound. 

[character] RAILLY 
[parenthetical] (whispering) 
[dialogue] It's an expression.  Here... 

[action] She's pulling something from a shopping bag at her feet, placing 
[action] it on COLE'S head, adjusting it... 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Why are we doing this? 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] So we can stick our heads out the 
[dialogue] window and feel the wind and listen to 
[dialogue] the music.  So we can appreciate what 
[dialogue] we have while we have it.  Forgive me, 
[dialogue] psychiatrists don't cry. 

[action] There are tears in her eyes.  They discomfit COLE. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] But maybe I'm wrong.  Maybe you're 
[dialogue] wrong.  Maybe we're both crazy. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] In a few weeks, it will have started or 
[dialogue] it won't.  If there are still baseball 
[dialogue] games and traffic jams, armed robberies 
[dialogue] and boring TV shows -- we'll be so 
[dialogue] happy, we'll be glad to turn ourselves 
[dialogue] in to the police. 

[character] SHADOWY MOVIEGOER 
[dialogue] SHHHHHHH     

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (whispers) 
[dialogue] Where can we hide for a few weeks? 

[action] ANGLE ON THE SCREEN, where SCOTTY and MADELINE are in the foreground, 
[action] the OCEAN behind them. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] You said you'd never seen the ocean... 

[scene_heading] EXT.  GORILLA'S PEN/ZOO - NIGHT 

[action] CLOSE ON A GORILLA, by moonlight, angry, a true nightmare vision. 

[action] URGENT WHISPERS off screen, RUSTLING SOUNDS.  Then, METAL ON METAL. 
[action] Only now are we aware that the GORILLA is in an outdoor zoo pen 
[action] with phony rocks.  Stalking back and forth.  Huge.  Upset. 

[action] CLANK.  CLANK.  METAL ON METAL.  GRUNTS of effort, then, DR. 
[action] MASON'S VOICE, plaintive, frightened. 

[character] DR. MASON'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] What are you doing to me?  Where are 
[dialogue] we?  Jeffrey, please... 

[character] SANDY'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] For God's sake, put the tape back on 
[dialogue] his mouth! 

[action] The GORILLA bellows angrily, beating his chest. 

[character] JEFFREY'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Forget the tape.  The monkey's louder than 
[dialogue] he is.  You gonna tape the monkey's mouth? 

[scene_heading] EXT.  PANTHERS' PEN/ZOO - NIGHT 

[action] Under the full moon, PANTHERS pace back and forth, back and 
[action] forth, uttering ominous guttural SNARLS. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  LION'S PEN/ZOO 

[action] The KING OF BEASTS gives a deep, fierce ROAR.  From the darkness, 
[action] unseen ELEPHANTS TRUMPET their response. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  AVIARIES/ZOO 

[action] A PANDEMONIUM of WINGS RUSTLING, the sharp metallic CLINKING of 
[action] metal on metal, the MUTTER of HUMAN VOICES, then a cacophonous 
[action] CRESCENDO of frenzied SCREECHING as PARROTS, COCKATOOS and other 
[action] EXOTIC BIRDS careen madly in their cages. 

[scene_heading] INT.  MOVIE THEATER AUDITORIUM 

[action] CLOSE ON COLE, dozing fitfully, as the SOUNDS of SCREAMING BIRDS 
[action] continue.  Suddenly, he comes awake with a start...sees the movie 
[action] filling his field of vision. 

[action] HIS POV:  the MOVIE SCREEN.  TIPPI HEDRIN, overwhelmed by 
[action] screeching BIRDS in an attic in Hitchcock's THE BIRDS. 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, orienting himself, looking around.  Empty seats on 
[action] both sides of him.  He's alone.  He panics. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Kathryn?! 

[scene_heading] INT.  THEATER LOBBY 

[action] A lobby poster boasts "Classics 24 Hours A Day" and "Hitchcock 
[action] Festival".  PANNING OFF the poster, passing a SNORING USHER, dead 

[action] to the world in an old velvet chair, WE DISCOVER a BRUNETTE in a 
[action] tight dress, just hanging up the lobby pay phone.  Turning, she 
[action] reveals heavy make-up, gaudy costume jewelry, and sun glasses. 
[action] She's the BRUNETTE in COLE'S DREAM!  Crossing the lobby toward 
[action] the auditorium, it's a pleasure to watch her nice body undulate 
[action] in the tight dress. 

[action] Just then, the auditorium doors burst open and a BLONDE MAN in a 
[action] Hawaiian shirt appears, the man from COLE'S DREAM, except this 
[action] man's moustache is fixed firmly on his upper lip.  The BLONDE MAN 
[action] stops, stunned at the sight of the BRUNETTE. 

[character] BRUNETTE 
[dialogue] We're booked on a 9:30 flight to Key West. 

[action] The Brunette is RAILLY, no longer the frazzled professional, 
[action] revealed now by her disguise as a sexy babe.  The Blonde Man is 
[action] COLE!  He's confused. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] You were in my dream just now.  I 
[dialogue] didn't recognize you. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Well, you look pretty different, too. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I mean in my dream -- I didn't realize 
[dialogue] it was you.  Then...I woke up and I...I 
[dialogue] thought you were gone. 

[character] RAILLY 
[parenthetical] (studies him seriously) 
[dialogue] I remember you...like this.  I feel 
[dialogue] I've known you before.  I feel I've 
[dialogue] always known you. 

[action] Their eyes lock.  Suddenly, she backs up, gently maneuvering him 
[action] with her, past the sleeping USHER, to and through an unlocked, 
[action] unmarked door, then closing it behind them. 

[scene_heading] INT.  STORAGE ROOM/MOVIE THEATER 

[action] RAILLY and COLE are in a dimly lit cluttered storage room.  She 
[action] kisses him hungrily amid the brooms, plastic trash barrels, other 
[action] janitorial items.  COLE responds to her passion as they move 
[action] deeper into the room, its walls covered with old movie posters. 
[action] Tearing at each other's clothes, they collapse on a rolled 
[action] theater curtain among stacks of ancient theater seats. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  SUBURBS - DAWN 

[action] The red rim of the rising sun is just becoming visible beyond the 
[action] silhouetted roofs of an upper middle-class suburban neighborhood. 
[action] The early light is so vague that when a huge SIBERIAN TIGER pads 
[action] across a neatly-trimmed lawn, he's more a shadowy vision than 
[action] reality.  Did we really see him at all? 

[scene_heading] EXT.  CONSTRUCTION SITE - DAWN 

[action] The rising sun flares behind the towering silhouette of an unfinished 
[action] building, deserted in the early morning light.  High up, a MONKEY 
[action] his head around a girder. 

[action] Four stories below, other MONKEYS are climbing. 

[scene_heading] EXT.  SHOPPING MALL - DAWN 

[action] Deserted in the first light of dawn, the stores face each other 
[action] across a broad promenade with blank staring windows. 

[action] Nothing happens.  For a long moment.  Then, an AFRICAN BULL 
[action] ELEPHANT appears, turning the corner, lumbering toward us along 
[action] the promenade, raising his trunk to TRUMPET triumphantly to the 
[action] other ELEPHANTS trotting into view behind him. 

[scene_heading] INT.  TAXICAB/CITY STREETS - EARLY MORNING 

[action] A fiftyish WOMAN CABBIE with white hair and a Southern twang is 
[action] at the wheel of the cab. 

[character] WOMAN CABBlE 
[dialogue] What time's your flight, friends? 

[action] In the back seat, COLE, in the blonde wig and moustache, looks to 
[action] his companion, the sexy babe in sun glasses and heavy make-up, RAILLY. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Nine thirty 

[character] WOMAN CABBIE 
[dialogue] Might be tight. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] (startled, checks her watch) 
[dialogue] Tight?  My watch says 7:30. 

[character] WOMAN CABBIE 
[dialogue] On your normal mornin', okay, plenty a 
[dialogue] time, but today, gotta take inta account 
[dialogue] your Army-of-the-Twelve-Monkeys factor. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] What?  What did you say? 

[character] WOMAN CABBIE 
[dialogue] Twelve Monkeys, honey.  Guess you folks 
[dialogue] didn't turn on your radio this morning. 

[action] COLE and RAILLY exchange a quick look. 

[character] WOMAN CABBIE 
[dialogue] Bunch a weirdoes let all the animals outta 
[dialogue] the zoo last night.  Then they locked up 

[dialogue] this big shot scientist in one of the cages. 
[dialogue] Scientist's own kid was one a the ones did it! 

[action] RAILLY and COLE stare at the cabbie, stunned. 

[character] WOMAN CABBlE 
[dialogue] Now they got animals all over the 
[dialogue] place.  Buncha zebras shut down the 
[dialogue] thruway 'bout an hour ago and some 
[dialogue] kinda thing called an "e-mu" it's got 
[dialogue] traffic blocked for miles over on 22. 

[action] Flabbergasted, RAILLY'S eyes suddenly fill with hope. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] That's what they were up to!  Freeing 
[dialogue] animals! 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] On the walls -- they meant the animals 
[dialogue] when they said, "We did it." 

[character] WOMAN CABBIE 
[dialogue] You can hear it on the radio all the 
[dialogue] stations... 

[action] As the WOMAN CAEBIE switches on the RADIO, RA:LLY points and COLE 
[action] follows her look. 

[action] COLE'S POV:  two CHEETAHS, sleek and magnificent against the 
[action] cityscape, streaking past the cab at ninety mph! 

[character] ANNOUNCER/RADIO (o.s.) 
[dialogue] In the meantime, numerous animal rights 
[dialogue] activists have joined the chorus 
[dialogue] condemning what they're calling the 
[dialogue] "loose canon" activities of Jeffrey 
[dialogue] Mason and his Army of the Twelve 
[dialogue] Monkeys. 
[dialogue] RIGHTS ACTIVIST/RADIO (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Can these fools seriously believe that 
[dialogue] releasing a captive animal into an 
[dialogue] urban environment is being compassionate 
[dialogue] to the animal?  It's mindlessly cruel, 
[dialogue] almost as indefensible as holding the 
[dialogue] animal in captivity in the first place. 

[action] RAILLY and COLE are watching FLAMINGOS cross the sky against a 
[action] backdrop of skyscrapers in silhouette. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Maybe it's going to be okay. 

[scene_heading] INT.  TICKET COUNTER/AIRPORT TERMINAL - MORNING 

[action] CLOSY ON copies of the mug shot of COLE and a photo of RAILLY 
[action] while the airport P.A. SYSTEM DRONES in the b.g.. 

[action] REVEAL a DETECTIVE, giving the flyers to the uniformed SUPERVISOR 
[action] at one end of the ticket counter. 

[character] DETECTIVE 
[dialogue] Tell your people if they spot either 
[dialogue] one of them, not to try and apprehend 
[dialogue] then.  They should notify us and... 

[action] ANGLE ON RAILLY AND COLE, thirty yards away, entering the terminal. 

[character] P.A. SYSTEM 
[dialogue] -- Flight 531 for Chicago is now ready 
[dialogue] for boarding at Gate Seventeen. 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, reacting to the P.A., stopping, seeing the 
[action] bustling airport lobby. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I know this place! ... This is my dream. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Airports all look the same.  Maybe 
[dialogue] it's... 
[parenthetical] (turning, reacting) 
[dialogue] James!  Your moustache!  It's slipping. 

[action] But COLE isn't listening.  He's looking around, mesmerized. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] It's not just my dream.  I was actually 
[dialogue] here!  I remember now.  My parents 
[dialogue] brought me to meet my uncle.  About a 
[dialogue] week or two before...before...before 
[dialogue] everybody started dying. 

[action] RAILLY glances around nervously. 

[action] RAILLY'S POV:  two UNIFORMED POLICEMEN, strolling through the 
[action] lobby, their eyes scanning the faces of TRAVELERS. 

[action] ANGLE ON RAILLY, pulling a small tube from her purse. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] They may be looking for us, James. 
[dialogue] (placing the tube in his hand) 
[dialogue] Use this.  You can fix it in the Men's Room. 

[character] COLE 
[parenthetical] (confounded) 
[dialogue] I was here...as a kid.  I think you 
[dialogue] were here, too.  But you...looked just 
[dialogue] like you look now. 

[character] RAILLY 
[parenthetical] (getting desperate) 
[dialogue] James, if we're identified, they're 

[dialogue] going to send us someplace...but not to 
[dialogue] Key West! 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] (snaps out of it, hand to moustache) 
[dialogue] Right!  You're right.  I have to fix this. 

[character] RAILLY 
[parenthetical] (indicating a sign) 
[dialogue] I'll get the tickets and meet you... in 
[dialogue] the Gift Shop. 

[action] COLE follows her look, nods, then heads for the Men's Room as 
[action] RAILLY, in sun glasses, gaudy outfit, high heels, starts clip 
[action] clopping toward the ticket counter, her ass attracting admiring 
[action] glances. 

[scene_heading] INT.  TELEPHONES/LOBBY - DAY 

[action] BUSINESS TRAVELERS huddle over pay phones, talking earnestly, as 
[action] COLE walks past on his way to the Men's Room. 

[action] Seeing an unoccupied phone, COLE hesitates, considers it.  Coming 
[action] to a decision, he reaches into his pocket, pulls out some change. 

[scene_heading] INT.  TZCKET COUNTER/TERMINAL 

[action] CLOSE ON the flyer of COLE and RAILLY taped under the counter, 
[action] hidden from the customers, but in clear view of the TICKET AGENT 
[action] who has just finished serving a PORTLY GENTLEMAN.  The GENTLEMAN 
[action] walks away. 

[action] ANGLE ON RAILLY, stepping up to the counter, smiling, looking 
[action] nothing like the Railly on the flyer. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Judy Simmons.  I have reservations for 
[dialogue] Key West. 

[scene_heading] INT.  PAY PHONES/TERMINAL 

[action] COLE is speaking into the phone very low, very private, very intense. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Listen, I don't know whether you're 
[dialogue] there or not.  Maybe you just clean 
[dialogue] carpets.  If you do, you're lucky -- 
[dialogue] you're gonna live a long, happy life. 
[dialogue] But if you other guys exist and you're 
[dialogue] picking this up -- forget about the 
[dialogue] Army of The Twelve Monkeys -- they 
[dialogue] didn't do it.  It was a mistake' 
[dialogue] Someone else did it.  The Army of The 
[dialogue] Twelve Monkeys are just dumb kids 
[dialogue] playing revolutionaries.  It was 
[dialogue] someone else! 

[action] COLE looks around nervously, catches a BUSINESSMAN at the next 
[action] phone looking away quickly.  COLE touches his loose moustache as 
[action] he averts his face and speaks into the phone in an urgent whisper. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I've done my job.  I did what you wanted. 
[dialogue] Good luck.  I'm not coming back! 

[action] COLE hangs up the phone, looks around, catches a few stares. 
[action] Averting his face, he heads for the Men's Room. 

[scene_heading] INT.  TICKET COUNTER - DAY 

[action] The TICKET AGENT is counting out a stack of bills. 

[character] TICKET AGENT 
[dialogue] Don't see a lot of this... cash. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] It's...a long story. 

[character] TICKET AGENT 
[dialogue] (smiles, hands over the tickets) 
[dialogue] They'll begin boarding in about twenty 
[dialogue] minutes.  Have a nice flight, Mrs. Simmons. 

[action] Turning to go, RAILLY fumbles the tickets while trying to put 
[action] them in her purse and they flutter to the floor.  As she kneels 
[action] to retrieve them, WE SEE the long line of waiting TRAVELERS from 
[action] the waist down.  WE SEE a familiar Chicago Bulls Sports Bag 
[action] resting on the floor beside sneakers and gaudy baggy pants. 
[action] we've seen this outfit before...in COLE'S dream...on MR. PONYTAIL! 

[scene_heading] INT.  MEN'S ROOM/AIRPORT - DAY 

[action] The P.A. DRONES as CCLE, head down, lingers at a sink, washing 
[action] and rewashing his hands while another TRAVELER finishes drying 
[action] his hands, gives COLE a quizzical look, then leaves. 

[action] Quickly, COLE glances around, checks the seemingly empty Men's 
[action] Room, then takes the tube of adhesive from his pocket, puts some 
[action] goop under the loose edge of his moustache and presses it firmly 
[action] against his face as he leans close to the mirror. 

[character] RASPY VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Got yourself a prob, Bob? 

[action] COLE whirls, looks for the source of the VOICE.  Nothing!  Until 
[action] he spots shoes peeking from dropped trousers indicating an 
[action] occupied stall.  It must be him! 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Leave me alone!  I made a report.  I 
[dialogue] didn't have to do that. 

[character] RASPY VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Point of fact -- you don't belong here. 

[dialogue] It's not permitted to let you stay. 

[action] A toilet FLUSHES in the "occupied" stall.  COLE'S answer is loud 
[action] and defiant. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] This is the present.  This is not the past. 
[dialogue] This is not the future.  This is right now! 

[action] A PLUMP BUSINESSMAN emerges from the "occupied" stall, gives COLE 
[action] a wary look and a wide berth as he heads for a sink. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I'm staying here! You got that?  You 
[dialogue] can't stop me! 

[character] PLUMP MAN 
[parenthetical] (high pitched voice) 
[dialogue] Anything you say, chief.  It's none of 
[dialogue] my business. 

[action] COLE looks dismayed.  This guy couldn't be "THE VOICE"!  And there 
[action] are no feet showing under the other stalls.  Did he imagine it? 

[scene_heading] INT.  TICKET COUNTER - DAY 

[action] The Chicago Bulls bag!  It's on the counter in front of the 
[action] TICKET AGENT who's reviewing a stack of tickets in awe... 

[character] TICKET AGENT 
[dialogue] Woooo-eeee.  San Francisco, New 
[dialogue] Orleans, Rio de Janeiro, Rome, 
[dialogue] Kinshasa, Karachi, Bangkok, Peking! 
[dialogue] That's some trip you're taking, sir, 
[dialogue] All in one week! 

[character] MR. PONYTAIL (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Business. 

[character] TICKET AGENT 
[dialogue] (handing over the tickets) 
[dialogue] Have a good one, sir. 

[scene_heading] INT.  TERMINAL LOBBY 

[action] COLE emerges from the Men's Room, shaken, paranoid.  He glances 
[action] around nervously.  Then, keeping his head down, he starts walking 
[action] toward the Gift Shop.  Before he gets more than a few steps, 
[action] someone suddenly grabs his shoulder from behind. 

[character] FAMILIAR VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] You gotta be crazy, man! 

[action] COLE whirls, finds himself facing a Puerto Rican youth in an L.A. 
[action] Raiders jacket, a sideways baseball cap, and mirrored sun glasses 

[character] COLE 

[dialogue] Jo...Jose???? 

[character] JOSE 
[dialogue] Pulling out the tooth, man, that was 
[dialogue] nuts!  Here, take this. 

[action] JOSE tries to slip COLE a 9mm pistol.  Astonished, COLE resists! 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] What?  What for?  Are you crazy? 

[action] Frustrated, JOSE conceals the gun but keeps a grip on COLE'S arm. 

[character] JOSE 
[dialogue] Me?  Are you kiddin?  You're the one! 
[dialogue] You were a hero, man.  They gave you a 
[dialogue] pardon!  And whadda you do?  You come 
[dialogue] back and fuck with your teeth!  Wow! 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] How did you find me? 

[character] JOSE 
[dialogue] The phone call, man.  The phone call. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] The call I just made?  Five minutes ago? 

[character] JOSE 
[dialogue] Hey, five minutes ago, thirty years ago! 
[dialogue] Yes, that phone call.  I been in training 
[dialogue] for this a couple a months now -- ever 
[dialogue] since I got back from that... "weird" 
[dialogue] war we were in.  You remember that? 
[dialogue] (pressing the pistol on Cole) 
[dialogue] Here, take it, man!  You could still be 
[dialogue] a hero if you'd cooperate! 

[scene_heading] INT.  GIFT SHOP/TERMINAL - DAY 

[action] RAILLY takes a travel book on Key West from a rack, considers it, 
[action] includes it with several magazines she's holding.  She doesn't 
[action] notice MR. PONYTAZL enter the Gift Shop behind her! 

[action] The P.A. System DRONES flight info as RAILLY checks her watch and 
[action] frowns.  It's getting late and where's Cole?  She turns, heads 
[action] for the cash register to make her purchases. 

[action] MR. PONYTAIL, seen from behind, is at the cash register already. 
[action] He sets a newspaper on the counter and searches for change. 

[action] The paper features a banner headline..."ANIMALS SET FREE" and a 
[action] sub head..."PROMINENT SCIENTIST FOUND LOCKED IN GORILLA CAGE" 
[action] over a photo of DR. MASON being released from the cage and 
[action] another photo of a GORILLA perched atop a parked car. 

[action] Stepping in line behind MR. PONYTAIL, RAILLY checks her watch 

[action] again. Then, MR. PONYTAIL, having paid, turns to go and RAILLY 
[action] looks up and sees his face. though it is not visible to us. 

[action] Startled, RAILLY frowns.  Does she know this man? 

[action] MR. PONYTAIL pauses for a moment, considering the babe in the 
[action] shades, gaudy earrings, the tight skirt, and high heels. 

[action] RAILLY doesn't recognize the man, but we do!  He's DR. MASON'S 
[action] ASSISTANT, DR. PETERS...the man who attended RAILLY's lecture! 

[action] Smiling, DR. PETERS steps around RAILLY and exits the Gift Shop. 

[action] Still puzzled, RAILLY puts her purchases on the counter and the 
[action] CLERK starts ringing them up as a DELIVERY MAN comes in and drops 
[action] a bundle of newspapers at her feet. 

[action] RAILLY'S POV:  the front page shows a photo of three frightened 
[action] GIRAFFES in freeway gridlock under a headline proclaiming, "TERRORISTS 
[action] CREATE CHAOS".  Further down are two more photos...DR. MASON in the 
[action] gorilla cage and a file photo of DR. MASON in his lab. 

[action] CLOSE ON THE SHOT OF DR. MASON in his lab.  There's someone else 
[action] in the picture.  It's a man wearing a lab coat and a PONYTAIL! 

[action] ANGLE ON RAILLY, reacting, suddenly remembering! 

[action] MEMORY FLASHBACK!  INT.  RECEPTION ROOM/BREITROSE HALL - NIGHT 

[action] RAILLY looks up from the book she's signing and sees DR. PETERS. 

[character] DR. PETERS 
[dialogue] Isn't it obvious that "Chicken Little" 
[dialogue] represents the sane vision and that 
[dialogue] Homo Sapiens' motto, "Let's go shopping!" 
[dialogue] is the cry of the true lunatic? 

[scene_heading] INT.  GIFT SHOP - DAY 

[action] RAILLY, stunned, stares in the direction PETERS/PONYTAIL went. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Oh, my God! 

[character] P.A  SYSTEM 
[dialogue] -- flight 764 for San Francisco is now 
[dialogue] ready for boarding at Gate 36. 

[scene_heading] INT.  LOBBY - DAY 

[action] In the confusion of TRAVELERS streaming in different directions, 
[action] COLE hurries toward the Gift Shop while JOSE struggles to keep up. 

[character] JOSE 
[dialogue] Coma on, Cole, don't be an asshole. 
[parenthetical] (then, blurting it out) 
[dialogue] Look, I got orders, man!  You know what 

[dialogue] I'm sposed to do if you don't go along? 
[dialogue] I'm sposed to shoot the lady!  You got 
[dialogue] that?  They said, "If Cole don't obey 
[dialogue] this time, Garcia, you gotta shoot his 
[dialogue] girlfriend!" 

[action] COLE stops in his tracks, blown away, too stunned to speak. 

[character] JOSE 
[dialogue] I got no choice, man.  These are my 
[dialogue] orders.  Just take it, okay? 

[action] COLE accepts the gun this time, resigned now.  They've got him. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] This part isn't about the virus, is it? 

[character] JOSE 
[dialogue] Hey, man... 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] It's about obeying, about doing what 
[dialogue] you're told. 

[character] JOSE 
[dialogue] They gave you a pardon, man.  Whatdaya 
[dialogue] want? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Who am I supposed to shoot? 

[action] Just then, RAILLY rushes up to COLE, not even noticing JOSE. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] James!  Thank God!  I thought you'd 
[dialogue] disappeared.  Listen, I think I know 
[dialogue] who it is!  I saw him!  It's Dr. Mason's 
[dialogue] assistant.  An apocalypse nut!  The next 
[dialogue] flight to San Francisco leaves from Gate 38. 
[dialogue] If he's there, it has to be him. 

[action] JOSE, having heard this, steps back into the crowd as RAILLY 
[action] grabs COLE and pulls him toward the Security Check Points. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I love you, Kathryn.  Remember that. 

[action] She doesn't hear him or see the look of doom in his eyes. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Maybe we can stop him.  Maybe we can 
[dialogue] actually do something. 

[scene_heading] INT.  SECURITY CHECK POINT/TERMINAL - DAY 

[action] A young boy of nine passes through the magnetic arch grinning. 
[action] YOUNG COLE!  Exactly as he appears in the dream! 

[action] He joins his PARENTS, who are only visible from their chests 
[action] down, and they continue along the concourse.  WE LINGER and 
[action] DISCOVER two DETECTIVES watching TRAVELERS as they pass through 
[action] the magnetic arch and retrieve their bags from the X-ray machine, 
[action] comparing their faces to photos of COLE and RAILLY. 

[action] ANGLE ON A SECURITY OFFICER, watching the x-ray monitor. 

[action] ANGLE ON THE MONITOR, showing the X-RAY IMAGE of a sports bag moving 
[action] along the conveyer belt.  The bag contains some strange objects. 

[action] ANGLE ON THE SECURITY OFFICER, reacting. 

[character] SECURITY OFFICER 
[dialogue] Excuse me, sir.  Would you mind letting me 
[dialogue] have a look at the contents of your bag? 

[action] ANGLE ON DR. PETERS, coming through the magnetic arch, reacting. 

[character] DR. PETERS 
[dialogue] Me?  Oh, yes, of course.  My samples. 
[dialogue] I have the appropriate papers. 

[scene_heading] INT.  END OF LINE/SECURITY CHECK POINT 

[action] RAILLY AND COLE arrive at the very long suddenly stalled line of 
[action] TRAVELERS waiting to pass through security. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Oh, God, we don't have time for this. 

[action] ANGLE ON THE SECURITY CHECK POINT, where DR. PETERS unpacks his 
[action] Bulls bag, pulls out six metal cylinders along with a change of 
[action] clothes and a Walkman. 

[character] DR. PETERS 
[dialogue] Biological samples.  I have the 
[dialogue] paperwork right here. 

[action] DR. PETERS produces a sheaf of official papers while the SECURITY 
[action] OFFICER examines one of the tubes, turning it over in his hands. 

[character] SECURITY OFFICER 
[dialogue] I'm going to have to ask you to open 
[dialogue] this, sir. 

[character] DR. PETERS 
[dialogue] Open it? 
[parenthetical] (blinks stupidly, then) 
[dialogue] Of course. 

[action] DR. PETERS takes the metal cylinder and starts opening it. 

[action] There's a SOUND of VOICES RAISED behind them.  DR. PETERS pays no 
[action] attention, but the SECURITY OFFICER turns toward the NOISE. 

[action] SECURITY OFFICER'S POV:  RAILLY, trying to explain something to a 
[action] SECOND SECURITY OFFICER. 

[action] ANGLE ON THE TWO DETECTIVES, nearby, showing interest in the 
[action] commotion. 

[action] ANGLE ON DR. PETERS, oblivious to the fuss, pulling a closed 
[action] glass tube out of the metal cylinder. 

[character] DR. PETERS 
[dialogue] Here!  You see?  Biological!  Check the 
[dialogue] papers -- it's all proper.  I have a 
[dialogue] permit. 

[character] SECURITY OFFICER 
[dialogue] It's empty! 

[action] Indeed, it looks like a sealed clear glass tube with nothing in it. 

[character] DR. PETERS 
[dialogue] Well, yes, to be sure, it looks empty! 
[dialogue] But I assure you, it's not. 

[action] ANGLE ON RAILLY, at the end of the line, arguing with the SECOND 
[action] SECURITY OFFICER. 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] Please listen to me -- this is very urgent! 

[character] SECOND SECURITY OFFICER 
[dialogue] You'll have to get in line, ma'am. 

[character] TRAVELER 
[dialogue] We're all in a hurry, lady.  What's so 
[dialogue] special about you? 

[action] ANGLE ON DR. PETERS, producing the glass tubes from the other 
[action] metal cylinders as the SECURITY OFFICER examines the papers. 

[character] DR. PETERS 
[dialogue] You see!  Also invisible to the naked eye. 

[action] A beat.  OR. PETERS grins suddenly, opens one of the glass tubes, 
[action] and waves it under the SECURITY OFFICER'S nose! 

[character] DR. PETERS 
[dialogue] It doesn't even have an odor. 

[action] The SECURITY OFFICER glances up, sees what DR. PETERS is doing, 
[action] and smiles as he hands the papers back to the scientist. 

[character] SECURITY OFFICER 
[dialogue] That's not necessary, sir.  Here you 
[dialogue] go.  Thanks for your cooperation.  Have 
[dialogue] a good flight. 

[action] Hastily, DR. PETERS snatches up all the tubes and cylinders and 

[action] shoves them back into his gym bag. 

[action] ANGLE ON RAILLY, raging as the SECOND SECURITY OFFICER jabs her 
[action] with his finger. 

[character] SECOND SECURITY OFFICER 
[dialogue] Who are you calling a "moron"? 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] Get your hands off her! 

[action] The SECOND SECURITY OFFICER stiffens for trouble. 

[action] ANGLE ON THE DETECTIVES, watching the fuss, ready to get 
[action] involved.  Suddenly, the FIRST DETECTIVE frowns. 

[action] FIRST DETECTIVE9S POV:  COLE'S moustache is slipping.  COLE 
[action] senses it, reaches up to touch it, catches the DETECTIVE'S look. 
[action] For half a second their eyes meet, then COLE looks away. 

[action] ANGLE ON DR. PETERS, hurrying away. 

[] SECURITY OFFICER'S VOICE (o.s.} 
[] HOLD IT!  JUST A MOMENT. 

[action] DR. PETERS freezes, turns, ashen. 

[action] The SECURITY OFFICER is retrieving a pair of jockey shorts from 
[action] the floor beside the search table.  He waves them at DR. PETERS. 

[action] DR. PETERS hurries back for his underpants. 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, trying to keep his head turned away as he 
[action] confronts the SECURITY OFFICER. 

[character] COLE 
[dialogue] I said, get your hands off her.  She's 
[dialogue] not a criminal.  She's a doctor...a 
[dialogue] psychiatrist. 

[action] RAILLY looks alarmed at that. 

[action] ANGLE ON THE DETECTIVES, coming this way.  The FIRST DETECTIVE 
[action] has the photos in his hand. 

[action] ANGLE OW DR. PETERS, bagging his jockey shorts, then starting 
[action] hastily down the windowed concourse toward the gates. 

[action] ANGLE ON RAZZLY, suddenly spotting DR. PETERS! 

[character] RAILLY 
[dialogue] THERE HE IS!  HE'S CARRYING A DEADLY 
[dialogue] VIRUS!  STOP HIM! 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, following RAILLY'S look, seeing MR. PONYTAIL, THE 
[action] MAN FROM HIS DREAM! 

[action] ANGLE ON DR. PETERS, frightened, glancing back, walking faster. 

[character] RAILLY (o.s.) 
[dialogue] PLEASE, SOMEBODY -- STOP HIM! 

[action] ANGLE ON DETECTIVES, reaching RAILLY and COLE. 

[character] FIRST DETECTIVE 
[parenthetical] (raising his badge) 
[dialogue] Police Officers.  Would you step over 
[dialogue] here, please. 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, spotting something behind the DETECTIVES! 

[action] COLE'S POV:  SCARFACE, dressed like a "businessman"!  He gives 
[action] COLE a cold look. 

[action] A beat.  COLE lunges at the SECOND DETECTIVE, knocking him off 
[action] balance, then sprints toward the magnetic arch and through it. 

[action] The ALARM goes off!!!! 

[action] The FIRST SECURITY OFFICER tries to stop COLE, but COLE knocks 
[action] him aside like a rag doll. 

[action] ANGLE ON DR. PETERS, fifty yards up the concourse, glancing back. 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, pulling his pistol. 

[action] ANGLE ON THE SECOND SECURITY OFFICER. 

[character] FIRST SECURITY OFFICER 
[dialogue] HE'S GOT A GUN! 

[action] ANGLE ON THE FIRST DETECTIVE, raising his pistol at COLE. 

[character] FIRST DETECTIVE 
[dialogue] STOP OR I'LL SHOOT! 

[action] ANGLE ON COLE, gun in hand, sprinting along the concourse toward 
[action] DR. PETERS as frightened TRAVELERS SCREAM and dive for cover. 

[action] ANGLE ON YOUNG COLE, standing at a concourse window, watching a 
[action] plane land, flanked by his parents whose faces we don't see. 

[action] IT'S SUDDENLY AS IF THE DREAM IS HAPPENING IN REAL LIFE!!!  THE 
[action] SAME MOKENTS INTERSPERSED WITH "NEW" MOMENTS FROM THE POV OF 
[action] YOUNG COLE who, hearing the commotion, turns just as DR. PETERS 
[action] hurries by.  DR. PETERS bumps into YOUNG COLE and reacts by 
[action] pulling his Bulls bag close to his body and calling... 

[character] DR. PETERS 
[dialogue] WATCH IT! 

[action] ANGLE ON YOUNG COLE, wide eyed, watching... 

[action] YOUNG COLE'S POV:  a BLONDE MAN. dashing up the concourse, his 

[action] moustache slipping over his lip, a pistol in his hand. 

[action] YOUNG COLE'S POV:  the FIRST DETECTIVE aims, looking for a clear 
[action] shot in the crowded passageway. 

[action] YOUNG COLE'S POV:  a BRUNETTE in flashy clothes, gaudy earrings, 
[action] high heels, and sun glasses SCREAMS... 

[character] BRUNETTE (RAILLY) 
[dialogue] N0OOOOO0O!!!!!! 

[action] YOUNG COLE'S POV:  the FIRST DETECTIVE, firing!  CRACK! 

[action] YOUNG COLE'S POV:  the BLONDE MAN, shuddering, staggering, falling. 

[action] ANGLE ON YOUNG COLE, stunned, as his PARENTS try to shield him. 

[character] MOTHER'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] My God!  They shot that man! 

[action] Mesmerized, YOUNG COLE watches the BRUNETTE rush to the BLONDE 
[action] MAN, kneel beside him, minister to his bloody wound. 

[action] YOUNG COLE'S POV:  the BLONDE MAN, fatalistically reaching up 
[action] and tenderly touching the BRUNETTE'S cheek, touching her tears. 
[action] (WE'VE SEEN THIS EXACT IMAGE IN COLE'S DREAM, A POWERFUL MOMENT, 
[action] UNFOLDING UNNATURALLY SLOWLY, OPENING LIKE A FLOWER.) 

[action] ANGLE ON YOUNG COLE, not able to hear their words, but he can see 
[action] emotion as the BLONDE MAN tries to tell the sobbing BRUNETTE 
[action] something. 

[action] YOUNG COLE'S POV:  PARAMEDICS, breaking the spell, pushing the 
[action] BRUNETTE aside as they crouch beside the BLONDE MAN. 

[character] FATHER'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Come along, son, this is no place for us. 

[action] ANGLE ON YOUNG COLE, as his FATHER'S ARM drapes over his shoulder, 
[action] steering him.  YOUNG COLE turns to look back as he's led away. 

[action] YOUNG COLE'S POV:  the PARAMEDICS, exchanging glances, shrugging 
[action] helplessly.  It's too late.  The BLONDE MAN is dead. 

[action] YOUNG COLE sees the BRUNETTE, her face streaked with tears, 
[action] suddenly turn and look around, scanning the crowd, searching for 
[action] something.  POLICE OFFICERS approach her, say something to her. 
[action] Even as she responds, her eyes continue to scan the concourse. 

[action] ANGLE ON YOUNG COLE, being hurried toward the lobby by his PARENTS 
[dialogue] (whose faces remain out of view).  He can't help sneaking another 
[action] look back. 

[action] YOUNG COLE'S POV:  POLICE, handcuffing a distracted, unresisting 
[action] RAILLY.  Even now, she continues to look around almost frantically. 

[action] Suddenly, her gaze falls on YOUNG COLE and she reacts...she's 

[action] found what she's looking for! 

[action] ANGLE ON YOUNG COLE, reacting to the intensity of her look. 

[action] ANGLE ON RAILLY, her eyes speaking to the boy across the crowded 
[action] concourse. 

[action] ANGLE ON YOUNG COLE, overwhelmed by the look. 

[character] FATHER'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Hurry up, son. 

[action] With a last lingering look toward the mysterious BRUNETTE, YOUNG 
[action] COLE turns away, tears welling in his eyes.  WE MOVE IN...CLOSE... 
[action] CLOSE...CLOSER...on his eyes.  WE WANT TO KNOW WHAT THE TEAR MEANS, 
[action] BUT THERE IS NO WAY TO TELL.  WE DON'T KNOW WHAT HE IS THINKING, 
[action] BUT WE KNOW VERY WELL WHAT HE WILL REMEMBER! 

[character] MOTHER'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] Pretend it was just a bad dream, Jimmy. 

[scene_heading] INT.  747 CABIN - DAY 

[action] DR. PETERS closes the door to the overhead luggage rack 
[action] containing his Chicago Bulls bag and takes his seat.  Next to 
[action] him, a FELLOW TRAVELER, unseen, says... 

[character] FELLOW TRAVELER'S VOICE (o.s.) 
[dialogue] It's obscene, all the violence, all the 
[dialogue] lunacy.  Shootings even at airports now. 
[dialogue] You might say...we're the next endangered 
[dialogue] species...human beings! 

[action] CLOSE ON DR. PETERS, smiling affably, turning to his neighbor. 

[character] DR. PETERS 
[dialogue] I think you're right. sir.  I think 
[dialogue] you've hit the nail on the head. 

[action] DR. PETERS' POV:  the FELLOW TRAVELER, a silver haired gentleman 
[action] in a business suit, offering his hand congenially.  DR. PETERS 
[action] doesn't know who this man is, but we do.  It's the ASTROPHYSICIST! 

[character] ASTROPHYSICIST 
[dialogue] Jones is my name.  I'm in insurance. 

[scene_heading] EXT.   PARKING LOT/AIRPORT 

[action] As YOUNG COLE'S PARENTS (seen only as sleeves and torsos) usher 
[action] YOUNG COLE into their station wagon, the boy hesitates, looks 
[action] back, watches a 747 climb into the sky. 

[] FADE OUT:
